    toc_depth: 3
    number_sections: true
---

# Repository purpose

This annotated RMarkdown file consolidates the analysis workflows used for the Michigan-only longitudinal soybean sudden death syndrome (SDS) seed treatment manuscript.

Working manuscript:

**Disentangling pathogen pressure, disease expression, and yield loss in soybean sudden death syndrome in Michigan**

The pipeline includes longitudinal seed treatment analyses, root disease analyses, table summaries, PCA and multivariate analyses, manuscript figure/table generation, and the Michigan trial-location map.

# Run order

1. Project setup and package loading  
2. Data import and cleaning  
3. Longitudinal SDHI seed treatment analyses  
4. Root disease index (RDX) focused analyses  
5. Table summaries and manuscript outputs  
6. PCA and multivariate structure  
7. Exploratory / legacy analyses retained for provenance  
8. Figure 1 Michigan trial-location map  
9. Session information  

# Manuscript output map

- **Figure 1**: Michigan trial-location map  
- **Main longitudinal models**: seed treatment effects on yield and FDX across 2017–2024  
- **RDX analyses**: focused root disease subset analyses  
- **PCA / multivariate outputs**: disease, soil, pathogen, SCN, and yield structure  
- **Supplemental tables**: cultivar, treatment, site-year, and model summary outputs

```{r packages, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# Visualization
```

### Load dependencies, and define some basic functions
```{r load dependencies, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, include=FALSE}
# Removed 26 redundant package-loading/install line(s); packages are loaded in the setup section.
#tidyverse/miscellaneous packages
#library("plyr")

#visualization/ggplot packages

#mapping packages
#library("purrr")
#library("terra")

#statistical analysis packages

#trims leading and trailing spaces
trim <- function (x) gsub("^\\s+|\\s+$", "", x)

#converts factors to numeric values
as.numeric.factor<-function(x) {as.numeric(levels(x))[x]}

#creates standard error function from sd and sqrt of number of observations
### fixed by RH on 2/6/2025
se<-function(x) sd((as.numeric(x,na.rm=T))/sqrt(length(x)),na.rm=T)

conflicts_prefer(
  dplyr::summarise,   # not ggplot2::summarise
  dplyr::summarize,   # optional alias
  dplyr::filter,
  dplyr::select,
  dplyr::mutate,
  dplyr::rename,
  dplyr::arrange,
  cowplot::get_legend,
  factoextra::fviz_pca_ind
)
```

### Reads in dataset and parses variables
```{r include=FALSE}
df<-read.csv(file="C:/Longitudinal/full2017_2024.csv",header=T,na.strings = c("", "NA")) #reads in dataset and assigns to variable "df"
df<- as.data.frame(lapply(df, function(x) { #trims leading or trailing spaces in df
  if (is.character(x)) {
    trimws(x)
  } else {
    x
  }
}))
facs<-c("year","state","location","cultivar","sds_tol","product","rep","fdx_level","scn_level") #assigns factor names to a vector
df[facs]<-lapply(df[facs],factor) #uses the char vector to parse variables by col name
df[,9]<-as.numeric(df[,9]) #apparently yield_kgha is a character vector? fixed

str(df) #dataframe summary by string

```

```{r}
}))

export<-filter(export,state=="MI")
```

### Subsets the dataframe df for further analysis
```{r warning=FALSE}

df_base<-filter(df,product=="Base") #subsets by product
df_ilevo<-filter(df,product=="ILEVO") 
df_saltro<-filter(df,product=="Saltro")

df_balevo<-filter(df,product=="Base" | product == "ILEVO") #just ILEVO and base (2017-2024)
df_basaltro<-filter(df,year!="2017" & year!="2018" & year!="2019")
df_basaltro<-filter(df_basaltro,product=="Base" | product == "Saltro")#just base and Saltro (2020-2024)
df_trio<-filter(df,year!="2017" & year!="2018" & year!="2019")  #only the years with all 3 trts (2020-2024)

df_qpcr<-df %>% filter(mean_Fv_ratio!="NA") #just the site-years where qPCR data were recorded
df_fdx<-df %>% filter(fdx!="NA") #just the site-years where fdx data were recorded
df_yield<-df %>% filter(yield_diff!="NA") #good df for piano plots

df_dcf<-df %>% filter(location=="DECATUR") #DCF only (all years)

df_dropnas<-drop_na(df)

df_no_zeroes<-df %>% mutate(across(fdx:scn_count_rep,~replace(., . ==  0,0.000001))) #converts zeroes to non-zeroes

df_stj<-df %>% filter(location=="STJOSEPH") #DCF only (all years)
df_dcf<-df %>% filter(location=="DECATUR") #DCF only (all years)

sds2017<- df_no_zeroes %>% filter(year == "2017") #subsets by year
sds2018<- df_no_zeroes %>% filter(year == "2018")
sds2019<- df_no_zeroes %>% filter(year == "2019")
sds2020<- df_no_zeroes %>% filter(year == "2020")
sds2021<- df_no_zeroes %>% filter(year == "2021")
sds2022<- df_no_zeroes %>% filter(year == "2022")
sds2023<- df_no_zeroes %>% filter(year == "2023")
sds2024<- df_no_zeroes %>% filter(year == "2024")

```


### need to refit these models ###
### Let's look at the data, fit a simple regression, and run some diagnostics
```{r include=FALSE}

#plot(product,yield_kgha) #plots product against all response variables
#plot(product,fdx)
#plot(product,yield_diff)

df %>% levene_test(yield_kgha~product) #formal test on variance -- it's heterogeneous (unsurprisingly)
df %>% levene_test(fdx~product) #fdx is homogeneous

y_mod<-lmer(I(yield_mtha^2)~product+location+year+soil_pH+(1|rep:mean_Fv_ratio)+(1|scn_count_rep),data=df_no_zeroes) #sets up a simple linear model 
summary(y_mod) #great R2 values, but model is wayyy overfit with non-sig. interaction terms
joint_tests(y_mod)#can't handle all the crossed terms -- needed to examine interaction terms separately
##location and year interactions are confounding the main effect terms. Probably would be best to fit models by year
###let's focus on the Fv-SCN-pH interaction
#drop1(y_mod) #looks good
plot(y_mod) #no linear relationship evident bw product and yield
#should we consider transforming the response variable?
#(boxcox(y_mod)) #squaring yield improves the model -- consider transformation in mixed effects models as well
lsmeans<-emmeans(y_mod,"product") #calculates lsmeans
cld(lsmeans,Letters=letters,alpha=.05,covar=T) #default is Tukey
pairs(lsmeans) #all pairwise comparisons
weights(lsmeans)

fdx_mod<-lmer(I(log(fdx))~product+(1|sds_tol)+location+year+mean_Fv_ratio+(1|scn_count_rep)+soil_pH+(1|rep),data=df_no_zeroes) #sets up a simple linear model 
summary(fdx_mod) #lower R2 values, but model is parsimonious w/o overfitting
joint_tests(fdx_mod)#looks good
#drop1(fdx_mod) #looks good
###conducted several manual deletions of terms (as above) to get the 'best' regression model:
###lm(log_fdx~product+location+year+mean_Fv_ratio+soil_pH+mean_Fv_ratio:soil_pH+scn_count_rep
###it's pretty great, tbh, just not fancy 
plot(fdx_mod) #no linear relationship evident bw product and yield
#should we consider transforming the response variable?
#(boxcox(fdx_mod)) 
###log-transformation of FDX is recommended
#lsmeans<-emmeans(fdx_mod,"product") #calculates lsmeans
#cld(lsmeans,Letters=letters,alpha=.05,covar=T) #default is Tukey
#pairs(lsmeans) #all pairwise comparisons
#weights(lsmeans)

shapiro_test(df,fdx) #formal test on normality of fdx = not normal
#shapiro_test(df,log_fdx) #nor is the log of fdx
shapiro_test(df,yield_kgha) # = not normal (type I ANOVA ok, due to sample size and homogeneity of variance)

```

###le sigh. Let's consider throwing out outliers in the yield model
```{r eval=FALSE, include=FALSE}

df_sandbox<-df

##code help from St. Google
cooksd<-cooks.distance(y_mod,intercept=T) #vector of cooks d values for all obs
cooks_df<-data.frame(cooksd=cooksd)
    #Using the 4/n rule
    threshold <- 4 / length(cooksd)
    influential_obs_indices <- which(cooksd > threshold) #okeee 45 obs :(

    #Alternatively, using 4 times the mean
    threshold_mean <- 4 * mean(cooksd, na.rm = TRUE)
    #influential_obs_indices <- which(cooksd > threshold_mean) #returns 56
    
#Gross plot    
plot(cooksd, pch = "*", cex = 2, main = "Influential Observations by Cook's Distance")
    abline(h = threshold, col = "red") # Add the chosen cutoff line
    text(x = 1:length(cooksd) + 1, y = cooksd,
         labels = ifelse(cooksd > threshold, names(cooksd), ""),
         col = "red") # Label influential points

    # Alternatively, to identify the top X most influential observations
    top_x_outliers <- 45 # e.g., for the top 45 outliers
    outlierz <- as.integer(names(sort(cooksd,decreasing=TRUE)[1:top_x_outliers]))
    ###interatively optimized for type I error sig. of product (seed treatment) factor, then R^2 and Fv 
df_yield <- df_sandbox[-outlierz,]

#View(df_cleaned)
y_mod_sb<-lmer(I(yield_kgha^2)~sds_tol+product+location+year+mean_Fv_ratio+scn_count_rep+soil_pH+(1|rep),data=df_yield)
summary(y_mod_sb) 
joint_tests(y_mod_sb) #optimized for modeling seed trt product effects
#drop1(y_mod_sb) #looks good
plot(y_mod_sb)

cooksd<-cooks.distance(y_mod_sb,intercept=T) #vector of cooks d values for all obs
cooks_df<-data.frame(cooksd=cooksd)
    #Using the 4/n rule
    #threshold <- 4 / length(cooksd)
    ##threshold_mean <- 4 * mean(cooksd, na.rm = TRUE)
    #influential_obs_indices <- which(cooksd > threshold_mean) #returns 56
    
#Gross plot    
plot(cooksd, pch = "*", cex = 2, main = "Influential Observations by Cook's Distance")
    abline(h = threshold, col = "red") # Add the chosen cutoff line
    text(x = 1:length(cooksd) + 1, y = cooksd,
         labels = ifelse(cooksd > threshold, names(cooksd), ""),
         col = "red") # Label influential points

###OK so after alll that, we have a yield response model that is optimized for detecting seed trt product differences -- oh well, due diligence
lsmeans<-emmeans(y_mod_sb,"product") #calculates lsmeans
cld(lsmeans,Letters=letters,alpha=.05,covar=T) #default is Tukey
pairs(lsmeans) #all pairwise comparisons
weights(lsmeans)

```

```{r eval=FALSE, include=FALSE}

##code help from St. Google
cooksd<-cooks.distance(fdx_mod,intercept=T) #vector of cooks d values for all obs
cooks_df<-data.frame(cooksd=cooksd)
    #Using the 4/n rule
    threshold <- 4 / length(cooksd)
    influential_obs_indices <- which(cooksd > threshold) #okeee 47 obs not bad

    #Alternatively, using 4 times the mean
    threshold_mean <- 4 * mean(cooksd, na.rm = TRUE)
    #influential_obs_indices <- which(cooksd > threshold_mean) #returns 44
    
#Gross plot    
plot(cooksd, pch = "*", cex = 2, main = "Influential Observations by Cook's Distance")
    abline(h = threshold, col = "red") # Add the chosen cutoff line
    text(x = 1:length(cooksd) + 1, y = cooksd,
         labels = ifelse(cooksd > threshold, names(cooksd), ""),
         col = "red") # Label influential points

    # Alternatively, to identify the top X most influential observations
    top_x_outliers <- 50 # e.g., for the top 2 outliers
    outlierz <- as.integer(names(sort(cooksd,decreasing=TRUE)[1:top_x_outliers]))
    
df_fdx <- df_no_zeroes[-outlierz,]
#View(df_cleaned)
fdx_mod_sb<-lm(I(log(fdx))~product+location+year+mean_Fv_ratio*scn_count_rep,data=df_fdx)
summary(fdx_mod_sb) 
joint_tests(fdx_mod_sb)
drop1(fdx_mod_sb) #looks good
plot(fdx_mod_sb)

###OK so after alll that, we have a log_FDX response model that is optimized for detecting seed trt product differences -- due diligence
lsmeans<-emmeans(fdx_mod_sb,"product") #calculates lsmeans
cld(lsmeans,Letters=letters,alpha=.05,covar=T) #default is Tukey
pairs(lsmeans) #all pairwise comparisons
weights(lsmeans)

```

```{r eval=FALSE, warning=TRUE, include=FALSE}
cld(lsmeans,Letters=letters,alpha=.05) #default is Tukey
pairs(lsmeans) #all pairwise comparisons
weights(lsmeans)

#---

# Build a fixed-effects design matrix from a fitted lmer(), optionally adding terms

design_matrix_fixed <- function(mod, extra_terms = NULL, data) {
  stopifnot(!missing(data))  # force you to pass the data explicitly

  # rows actually used in the fitted model (preserve order)
  mf_used <- model.frame(mod)
  rn_used <- rownames(mf_used)

  # subset & reorder your data to exactly those rows
  if (is.null(rownames(data))) {
    # give data rownames so match() works even if data had none
    rownames(data) <- seq_len(nrow(data))
  }
  data_sub <- data[match(rn_used, rownames(data)), , drop = FALSE]

  # check extras exist
  if (!is.null(extra_terms)) {
    missing_cols <- setdiff(extra_terms, colnames(data_sub))
    if (length(missing_cols)) {
      stop("These extra_terms are not in `data`: ",
           paste(missing_cols, collapse = ", "),
           call. = FALSE)
    }
  }

  # base fixed-effects terms (no response)
  tt_fixed <- delete.response(terms(mod))

  # extend with extras (e.g., "productBase")
  if (!is.null(extra_terms)) {
    rhs_terms <- c(attr(tt_fixed, "term.labels"), extra_terms)
    tt_fixed  <- terms(reformulate(rhs_terms))
  }

  # build matrix; keep column→term map
  X_full      <- model.matrix(tt_fixed, data = data_sub)
  assign_full <- attr(X_full, "assign")
  tlabs       <- attr(tt_fixed, "term.labels")

  keep <- colnames(X_full) != "(Intercept)"
  X    <- X_full[, keep, drop = FALSE]
  attr(X, "assign")      <- assign_full[keep]
  attr(X, "term.labels") <- tlabs
  X
}

collapse_terms_pc1 <- function(mm) {
  assign_vec  <- attr(mm, "assign");      stopifnot(!is.null(assign_vec))
  term_labels <- attr(mm, "term.labels"); stopifnot(!is.null(term_labels))

  out <- vector("list", length(term_labels)); names(out) <- term_labels
  for (k in seq_along(term_labels)) {
    cols_k <- which(assign_vec == k)
    if (length(cols_k) == 0L) { out[[k]] <- rep(NA_real_, nrow(mm)); next }
    Xk <- mm[, cols_k, drop = FALSE]
    out[[k]] <- if (ncol(Xk) == 1L) as.numeric(Xk[,1]) else {
      pc <- prcomp(Xk, center = TRUE, scale. = TRUE); pc$x[,1]
    }
  }
  as.data.frame(out, check.names = FALSE)
}

plot_corr_heatmap <- function(M, title = NULL,
                              order = "hclust",
                              number_cex = 0.7, tl_cex = 0.8) {
  C <- cor(M, use = "pairwise.complete.obs")
  ord <- corrplot::corrMatOrder(C, order = order)
  C  <- C[ord, ord, drop = FALSE]
  corrplot::corrplot(
    C, type = "upper", order = "original",
    tl.col = "black", tl.cex = tl_cex, tl.srt = 45,
    addCoef.col = "black", number.cex = number_cex,
    diag = FALSE,
    col = colorRampPalette(c("#b2182b","white","#2166ac"))(200),
    cl.lim = c(-1, 1), mar = c(1,1,2,1), title = title
  )
  invisible(C)
}

# Your fitted models (as given)
# y_mod_op  <- lmer(I(yield_kgha^2) ~ soil_pH + sds_tol + product + mean_Fv_ratio +
#                   scn_count_rep + fdx + fdx_diff + yield_diff + diff_price_kgha +
#                   (1 | year:location:rep),
#                   data = df_no_zeroes, na.action = na.omit)

# fdx_mod_op <- lmer(I(log(fdx)) ~ soil_pH + sds_tol + product + mean_Fv_ratio +
#                    scn_count_rep + fdx_diff + yield_diff + diff_price_kgha +
#                    (1 | year:location:rep),
#                    data = df_no_zeroes, na.action = na.omit)

# Yield model
mm_y_ex   <- design_matrix_fixed(y_mod_op,   extra_terms = "productBase", data = df)
mm_y_term <- collapse_terms_pc1(mm_y_ex)
plot_corr_heatmap(mm_y_term, title = "Predictor correlations (terms + productBase) — Yield")

# FDX model
mm_fdx_ex   <- design_matrix_fixed(fdx_mod_op, extra_terms = "productBase", data = df)
mm_fdx_term <- collapse_terms_pc1(mm_fdx_ex)
plot_corr_heatmap(mm_fdx_term, title = "Predictor correlations (terms + productBase) — FDX")

```


### SUMMARY CHUNK FOR MS ###
```{r}

### let's create the 'low' and 'high' FDX datasets
#summary by year, product, and location over all years
summary_fdx<-ddply(df, .(year,location,product), summarize,mean_yield=mean(as.numeric(yield_kgha),na.rm=T),se_yield=sd((as.numeric(yield_kgha))/sqrt(length(yield_kgha)),na.rm=T),mean_fdx=mean(as.numeric(fdx),na.rm=T),se_fdx=sd((as.numeric(fdx))/sqrt(length(fdx)),na.rm=T))

write.csv(summary_fdx,"C:/Longitudinal/summry_yahs.csv")

summary_fdx<-drop_na(summary_fdx)

#high FDX site-yars only
high_df <- df_no_zeroes %>% filter(fdx_level=="high")
high_df<-high_df %>% drop_na(fdx)

#low FDX site-yars only
low_df <- df_no_zeroes %>% filter(fdx_level=="low")
low_df<-low_df %>% drop_na(fdx)

#no FDX site-yars only
no_df <- df_no_zeroes %>% filter(fdx_level=="none")
no_df<-no_df %>% drop_na(fdx)

### let's create the 'low' and 'high' SCN datasets
#summary by year, product, and location over all years
summary_scn<-ddply(df, .(year,location), summarize,mean_yield=mean(as.numeric(yield_mtha),na.rm=T),se_yield=sd((as.numeric(yield_mtha))/sqrt(length(yield_mtha)),na.rm=T),mean_scn=mean(as.numeric(scn_count_rep),na.rm=T),se_scn=sd((as.numeric(scn_count_rep))/sqrt(length(scn_count_rep)),na.rm=T))

summary_scn<-drop_na(summary_scn)

#high scn site-yars only
high_scn_loc <- df_no_zeroes %>% filter(scn_level=="high")
high_scn_loc<-high_scn_loc %>% drop_na(scn_count_rep)

#low scn site-yars only
low_scn_loc <- df_no_zeroes %>% filter(scn_level=="low")
low_scn_loc<-low_scn_loc %>% drop_na(scn_count_rep)

```

### Mixed models and summary stats on yield by SCN level ###
```{r}

low_scn_mod<-lmer(I(yield_mtha^2)~year+product+(1|sds_tol)+location+mean_Fv_ratio*I(scn_count_rep/1000)+(1|fdx)+soil_pH+(1|rep),data=low_scn_loc) #mixed effects model for RCBD -- squaring yield does NOT improve the model -- retain response on observed scale
summary(low_scn_mod) #model summary
#boxcox(low_scn_mod)
plot(low_scn_mod)
joint_tests(low_scn_mod) #emmeans gtype 3 ANOVA
Anova(low_scn_mod,type=3) #car anove
anova(low_scn_mod) #regular type 1 ANOVA -- no p-value, but not that much different
#drop1(low_scn_mod) #parameter selection
lsmeans<-emmeans(low_scn_mod,"product",tran="power",param=2) #calculates lsmeans
cld(lsmeans,Letters=letters,alpha=.05,covar=T) #default is Tukey
pairs(lsmeans) #all pairwise comparisons
weights(lsmeans)

slice_mod<-lmer(I(yield_mtha^2)~year+product+(1|sds_tol)+location+mean_Fv_ratio+scn_level+fdx+soil_pH+(1|rep),data=df_no_zeroes) 
# Estimated marginal means
emm <- emmeans(slice_mod,"scn_level")
# Compact letter display
(cld_out <- cld(emm, Letters = letters, adjust = "tukey"))
pairs_out <- pairs(emm, adjust = "tukey")
# View pairwise comparisons and p-values
summary(pairs_out)

high_scn_mod<-lmer(I(yield_mtha^2)~product+(1|mean_Fv_ratio)+I(scn_count_rep/1000)+fdx+(1|sds_tol)+(1|year:location:soil_pH:rep),data=high_scn_loc) #mixed effects model for RCBD -- squaring yield does NOT improve the model -- retain response on observed scale
summary(high_scn_mod) #model summary
#boxcox(high_scn_mod)
plot(high_scn_mod)
joint_tests(high_scn_mod) #emmeans gtype 3 ANOVA
Anova(high_scn_mod,type=3) #car anove
anova(high_scn_mod) #regular type 1 ANOVA -- no p-value, but not that much different
#drop1(high_scn_mod) #parameter selection
lsmeans<-emmeans(high_scn_mod,"product",tran="power",param=2) #calculates lsmeans
cld(lsmeans,Letters=letters,alpha=.05,covar=T) #default is Tukey
pairs(lsmeans) #all pairwise comparisons
weights(lsmeans)

slice_mod2<-lmer(I(yield_mtha^2)~year+location+scn_level+fdx+(1|sds_tol)+(1|product)+(1|mean_Fv_ratio)+(1|soil_pH:rep),data=df_no_zeroes,na.action=na.omit) 
summary(slice_mod2)
# Estimated marginal means
joint_tests(slice_mod2)
emm <- emmeans(slice_mod2,"scn_level")
# Compact letter display
(cld_out <- cld(emm, Letters = letters, adjust = "tukey"))
pairs_out2 <- pairs(emm, adjust = "tukey")
# View pairwise comparisons and p-values
summary(pairs_out)


```

### LMMs for FDX under SCN levels ###
```{r}

low_scn_loc$fdx[low_scn_loc$fdx == 0] <- 0.000001 #converts zeroes to non-zeroes for log-trans
high_scn_loc$fdx[high_scn_loc$fdx == 0] <- 0.000001 

###this is the model for 2017###
low_fdx_mod<-lmer(I(log(fdx))~product+(1|sds_tol)+year+location+mean_Fv_ratio+soil_pH+(1|rep),data=low_scn_loc) #mixed effects model for RCBD -- squaring yield does NOT improve the model -- retain response on observed scale
summary(low_fdx_mod) #model summary
#boxcox(low_fdx_mod)
plot(low_fdx_mod)
joint_tests(low_fdx_mod) #emmeans gtype 3 ANOVA
Anova(low_fdx_mod,type=3) #car anove
anova(low_fdx_mod) #regular type 1 ANOVA -- no p-value, but not that much different
#drop1(low_fdx_mod) #parameter selection
#lsmeans<-emmeans(low_fdx_mod,"scn_level",param=2) #calculates lsmeans
cld(lsmeans,Letters=letters,alpha=.05,covar=T) #default is Tukey
pairs(lsmeans) #all pairwise comparisons
weights(lsmeans)

slice_mod3<-lmer(I(log(fdx))~product+(1|sds_tol)+year+mean_Fv_ratio+scn_level+ (1|scn_level:location)+soil_pH+(1|rep),data=low_df)
# Estimated marginal means
emm <- lsmeans(slice_mod3,"scn_level")
# Compact letter display
(cld_out <- cld(emm, Letters = letters, adjust = "tukey"))
pairs_out3 <- pairs(emm, adjust = "tukey")
# View pairwise comparisons and p-values
summary(pairs_out)


high_fdx_mod<-lmer(I(log(fdx))~(1|mean_Fv_ratio)+soil_pH+(1|year:location:rep:sds_tol:product),data=high_scn_loc) #mixed effects model for RCBD -- squaring yield does NOT improve the model -- retain response on observed scale
summary(high_fdx_mod) #model summary
#boxcox(high_fdx_mod)
plot(high_fdx_mod)
joint_tests(high_fdx_mod) #emmeans gtype 3 ANOVA
Anova(high_fdx_mod,type=3) #car anove
anova(high_fdx_mod) #regular type 1 ANOVA -- no p-value, but not that much different
#drop1(high_fdx_mod) #parameter selection
#lsmeans<-emmeans(high_fdx_mod,"scn_level",param=2) #calculates lsmeans
cld(lsmeans,Letters=letters,alpha=.05,covar=T) #default is Tukey
pairs(lsmeans) #all pairwise comparisons
weights(lsmeans)

### lets slice out levels of SCN and FDX ###
slice_mod4<-lmer(I(log(fdx))~year+location+mean_Fv_ratio+scn_level+(1|sds_tol)+(1|product)+(1|soil_pH)+(1|rep),data=df_no_zeroes)
summary(slice_mod4)
joint_tests(slice_mod4)
# Estimated marginal means
emm <- emmeans(slice_mod4,"scn_level")
# Compact letter display
(cld_out <- cld(emm, Letters = letters, adjust = "tukey"))
pairs_out4 <- pairs(emm, adjust = "tukey")
# View pairwise comparisons and p-values
summary(pairs_out)
```

```{r}
summary_Fv<-ddply(df, .(year,location,sds_tol), summarize,#mean_Fv=mean(as.numeric(mean_Fv_ratio),na.rm=T),se_Fv=sd((as.numeric(mean_Fv_ratio))/sqrt(length(mean_Fv_ratio)),na.rm=T)),
                  mean_fdx=mean(as.numeric(fdx),na.rm=T),se_fdx=sd((as.numeric(fdx))/sqrt(length(fdx)),na.rm=T))
```

```{r}
# View pairwise comparisons and p-values
summary(pairs_out)

low_fdx_mod<-lmer(I(yield_mtha^2)~sds_tol+(1|year:location:soil_pH:rep:mean_Fv_ratio:scn_count_rep:product),data=low_df) #mixed effects model for RCBD -- squaring yield does NOT improve the model -- retain response on observed scale
summary(low_fdx_mod) #model summary
#boxcox(low_fv_mod)
plot(low_fdx_mod)
joint_tests(low_fdx_mod) #emmeans gtype 3 ANOVA
Anova(low_fdx_mod,type=3) #car anove
anova(low_fdx_mod) #regular type 1 ANOVA -- no p-value, but not that much different
#drop1(low_fv_mod) #parameter selection
lsmeans<-emmeans(low_fdx_mod,"sds_tol",tran="power",param=2) #calculates lsmeans
cld(lsmeans,Letters=letters,alpha=.05,covar=T) #default is Tukey
pairs(lsmeans) #all pairwise comparisons
weights(lsmeans)

slice_mod<-lmer(I(yield_mtha^2)~year+product+(1|sds_tol)+location+mean_Fv_ratio+scn_level+fdx+soil_pH+(1|rep),data=df_no_zeroes,na.action=na.omit) 
# Estimated marginal means
emm <- emmeans(slice_mod,"scn_level")
# Compact letter display
(cld_out <- cld(emm, Letters = letters, adjust = "tukey"))
pairs_out <- pairs(emm, adjust = "tukey")
# View pairwise comparisons and p-values
summary(pairs_out)

high_fdx_mod<-lmer(I(yield_mtha^2)~sds_tol+(1|year:location:soil_pH:rep:mean_Fv_ratio:scn_count_rep:product),data=high_scn_loc) #mixed effects model for RCBD -- squaring yield does NOT improve the model -- retain response on observed scale
summary(high_fv_mod) #model summary
#boxcox(high_fv_mod)
plot(high_fdx_mod)
joint_tests(high_fdx_mod) #emmeans gtype 3 ANOVA
Anova(high_fdx_mod,type=3) #car anove
anova(high_fdx_mod) #regular type 1 ANOVA -- no p-value, but not that much different
#drop1(high_fv_mod) #parameter selection
lsmeans<-emmeans(high_fdx_mod,"sds_tol",tran="power",param=2) #calculates lsmeans
cld(lsmeans,Letters=letters,alpha=.05,covar=T) #default is Tukey
pairs(lsmeans) #all pairwise comparisons
weights(lsmeans)

slice_mod2<-lmer(I(yield_mtha^2)~sds_tol+scn_level+(1|year:location:soil_pH:rep:mean_Fv_ratio:scn_count_rep:product),data=df_no_zeroes,na.action=na.omit) 
summary(slice_mod2)
# Estimated marginal means
joint_tests(slice_mod2)
emm <- emmeans(slice_mod2,"scn_level")
# Compact letter display
(cld_out <- cld(emm, Letters = letters, adjust = "tukey"))
pairs_out2 <- pairs(emm, adjust = "tukey")
# View pairwise comparisons and p-values
summary(pairs_out2)


```


### Mixed models and summary yield stats by year
### I'm not that sure how useful mixed models will be to explain trends, tbh
### simple regression models to fit best, even when taking the most cautious approach 

#2017
```{r echo=FALSE, warning=TRUE}

### note that FDX was only collected at DCF in 2017 -- will need to use KW for within-site analysis (unless it passes ANOVA tests)

###this is the model for 2017###
lmm2017<-lmer(I(yield_mtha^2)~product*location+sds_tol+(1|location:rep),data=sds2017,na.action=na.omit) #mixed effects model for RCBD -- squaring yield does NOT improve the model -- retain response on observed scale
summary(lmm2017) #model summary
#boxcox(lmm2017)
plot(lmm2017)
joint_tests(lmm2017) #emmeans gtype 3 ANOVA
Anova(lmm2017,type=3) #car anove
anova(lmm2017) #regular type 1 ANOVA -- no p-value, but not that much different
drop1(lmm2017) #parameter selection
lsmeans<-emmeans(lmm2017,"product",tran="power",param=2) #calculates lsmeans
cld(lsmeans,Letters=letters,alpha=.05,covar=T) #default is Tukey
pairs(lsmeans) #all pairwise comparisons
weights(lsmeans)

lsmeans2<-emmeans(lmm2017,pairwise~product|location)
cld(lsmeans2,Letters=letters,alpha=.05,covar=T)

emm <- emmeans(lmm2017, ~ product|location)
pairs_emm <- contrast(emm, method = "pairwise")
prod_loc<-summary(pairs_emm, adjust = "tukey")  # or "none", "sidak", etc.
cld(emm, adjust = "tukey")

#KW and Dunn's tests for pairwise FDX comparisons between site-years
(sds2017 %>% group_by(location,sds_tol) %>% dunn_test(diff_price_kgha~product))

#sds2017_a<-sds2017

##code help from St. Google
cooksd<-cooks.distance(lmm2017,intercept=T) #vector of cooks d values for all obs
cooks_df<-data.frame(cooksd=cooksd)
    #Using the 4/n rule
    threshold <- 4 / length(cooksd)
    influential_obs_indices <- which(cooksd > threshold) #okeee 8 obs

    #Gross plot    
    plot(cooksd, pch = "*", cex = 2, main = "Influential Observations by Cook's Distance")
    abline(h = threshold, col = "red") # Add the chosen cutoff line
    text(x = 1:length(cooksd) + 1, y = cooksd,
         labels = ifelse(cooksd > threshold, names(cooksd), ""),
         col = "red") # Label influential points

### outlier removal does NOT improve model fit or power ###
    
    #Identify the top X most influential observations
    #top_x_outliers <- 8 # e.g., for the top 8 outliers
    #outlierz <- as.integer(names(sort(cooksd,decreasing=TRUE)[1:top_x_outliers]))
    ###interatively optimized for type I error sig. of product (seed treatment) factor
    #sds2017_b <- sds2017_a[-outlierz,]

    #lmm2017_sb<-lmer(I(yield_mtha^2)~product+sds_tol+location+sds_tol:location+(1|location:rep),data=sds2017_b)
    #summary(lmm2017_sb) 
    #joint_tests(lmm2017_sb) #optimized for modeling seed trt product effects
    #drop1(lmm2017_sb) #looks good
    #plot(lmm2017_sb)
    
#yield summary by product and location over all years
summary_2017<-ddply(sds2017, .(location,sds_tol,product), summarize,mean_yield=mean(as.numeric(yield_mtha),na.rm=T),se_yield=sd((as.numeric(yield_mtha))/sqrt(length(yield_mtha)),na.rm=T),mean_fdx=mean(as.numeric(fdx),na.rm=T),se_fdx=sd((as.numeric(fdx))/sqrt(length(fdx)),na.rm=T))

summary_2017<-ddply(sds2017, .(location,sds_tol,product,harv_price_kg), summarize,mean_fdx=mean(as.numeric(fdx),na.rm=T),se_fdx=sd((as.numeric(fdx))/sqrt(length(fdx)),na.rm=T),mean_kgha=mean(as.numeric(yield_kgha),na.rm=T),se_kgha=sd((as.numeric(yield_kgha))/sqrt(length(yield_kgha)),na.rm=T),mean_bua=mean(as.numeric(yield_bua),na.rm=T),se_bua=sd((as.numeric(yield_bua))/sqrt(length(yield_bua)),na.rm=T),mean_USD=mean(diff_price_kgha,na.rm=T))
write.csv(summary_2017,"C:/Longitudinal/summary_2017.csv")

#optimized yield model
price_mod_2017<-lmer(diff_price_kgha~product+(1|location:rep:sds_tol),data=sds2017,na.action=na.omit)
#summary(y_mod_op) 
joint_tests(price_mod_2017) #optimized for modeling seed trt product effects
drop1(price_mod_2017) #looks good
plot(price_mod_2017)
lsmeans<-emmeans(price_mod_2017,"product") #calculates lsmeans
cld(lsmeans,Letters=letters,alpha=.05) #default is Tukey
pairs(lsmeans) #all pairwise comparisons
weights(lsmeans)

```

#2018
```{r echo=FALSE, warning=TRUE}

### note that FDX was only collected at DCF in 2018 -- will need to use KW for within-site analysis (unless it passes ANOVA tests)

lmm2018<-lmer(I(yield_mtha^2)~product+sds_tol+location+(1|location:soil_pH:rep:sds_tol),data=sds2018,na.action=na.omit) #mixed effects model for RCBD #mixed effects model for RCBD)
#summary(lmm2018) #model summary
#boxcox(lmm2018)
#plot(lmm2018)
joint_tests(lmm2018) #emmeans gtype 3 ANOVA
#Anova(lmm2018,type=3) #car anove
#anova(lmm2018) #regular type 1 ANOVA -- no p-value, but not that much different
#drop1(lmm2018) #parameter selection
lsmeans<-emmeans(lmm2018,"product") #calculates lsmeans
(cld(lsmeans,Letters=letters,alpha=.05,covar=T)) #default is Tukey
pairs(lsmeans) #all pairwise comparisons
weights(lsmeans)

emm <- emmeans(lmm2018, ~ product|location)
pairs_emm <- contrast(emm, method = "pairwise")
prod_loc<-summary(pairs_emm, adjust = "tukey")  # or "none", "sidak", etc.
cld(emm, adjust = "tukey")

#KW and Dunn's tests for pairwise yield comparisons between site-years
(sds2018 %>% group_by(location) %>% dunn_test(fdx~sds_tol)) 

sds2018_a<-sds2018

##code help from St. Google
cooksd<-cooks.distance(lmm2018,intercept=T) #vector of cooks d values for all obs
cooks_df<-data.frame(cooksd=cooksd)
    #Using the 4/n rule
    threshold <- 4 / length(cooksd)
    influential_obs_indices <- which(cooksd > threshold) #okeee 8 obs

    #Gross plot    
    plot(cooksd, pch = "*", cex = 2, main = "Influential Observations by Cook's Distance")
    abline(h = threshold, col = "red") # Add the chosen cutoff line
    text(x = 1:length(cooksd) + 1, y = cooksd,
         labels = ifelse(cooksd > threshold, names(cooksd), ""),
         col = "red") # Label influential points

### removal of 8 outliers (cooksD > 0.05) maximizes product contrasts ###
    
    #Identify the top X most influential observations
    top_x_outliers <- 8 # e.g., for the top 8 outliers
    outlierz <- as.integer(names(sort(cooksd,decreasing=TRUE)[1:top_x_outliers]))
    ###interatively optimized for type I error sig. of product (seed treatment) factor
    sds2018_b <- sds2018_a[-outlierz,]
    
###this is the model for 2018###

    lmm2018<-lmer(I(yield_mtha^2)~product+sds_tol*location+(1|location:soil_pH:rep),data=sds2018_b)
    summary(lmm2018) 
    joint_tests(lmm2018) #optimized for modeling seed trt product effects
    drop1(lmm2018) #looks good
    plot(lmm2018)
    lsmeans<-emmeans(lmm2018,"product",tran="power",param=2) #calculates lsmeans
    cld(lsmeans,Letters=letters,alpha=.05,covar=T) #default is Tukey
    pairs(lsmeans) #all pairwise comparisons
    weights(lsmeans)
    
lsmeans2<-emmeans(lmm2018,pairwise~sds_tol|location|product)
cld(lsmeans2,Letters=letters,alpha=.05,covar=T)

emm <- emmeans(lmm2018, ~ product|sds_tol|location)
pairs_emm <- contrast(emm, method = "pairwise")
prod_loc<-summary(pairs_emm, adjust = "tukey")  # or "none", "sidak", etc.
cld(emm, adjust = "tukey")
    
#KW and Dunn's tests for pairwise yield comparisons between site-years
(sds2018 %>% group_by(location,sds_tol) %>% dunn_test(yield_kgha~product)) 
(sds2018 %>% group_by(location,sds_tol) %>% dunn_test(fdx~product)) 
(sds2018 %>% group_by(location,sds_tol) %>% dunn_test(diff_price_kgha~product))

#yield summary by product and location over all years
summary_2018<-ddply(sds2018, .(location,sds_tol,product), summarize,mean_yield=mean(as.numeric(yield_mtha),na.rm=T),se_yield=sd((as.numeric(yield_mtha))/sqrt(length(yield_mtha)),na.rm=T),mean_fdx=mean(as.numeric(fdx),na.rm=T),se_fdx=sd((as.numeric(fdx))/sqrt(length(fdx)),na.rm=T))

summary_2018_b<-ddply(sds2018_b, .(location,product), summarize,mean_yield=mean(as.numeric(yield_bua),na.rm=T),se_yield=sd((as.numeric(yield_bua))/sqrt(length(yield_bua)),na.rm=T),mean_fdx=mean(as.numeric(fdx),na.rm=T),se_fdx=sd((as.numeric(fdx))/sqrt(length(fdx)),na.rm=T))
                    
summary_2018_c<-ddply(sds2018_b, .(product), summarize,mean_yield=mean(as.numeric(yield_bua),na.rm=T),se_yield=sd((as.numeric(yield_bua))/sqrt(length(yield_bua)),na.rm=T),mean_fdx=mean(as.numeric(fdx),na.rm=T),se_fdx=sd((as.numeric(fdx))/sqrt(length(fdx)),na.rm=T))

summary_2018<-ddply(sds2018, .(location,sds_tol,product), summarize,mean_yield=mean(as.numeric(yield_kgha),na.rm=T),se_yield=sd((as.numeric(yield_kgha))/sqrt(length(yield_kgha)),na.rm=T),mean_fdx=mean(as.numeric(fdx),na.rm=T),se_fdx=sd((as.numeric(fdx))/sqrt(length(fdx)),na.rm=T),mean_bua=mean(as.numeric(yield_bua),na.rm=T),se_bua=sd((as.numeric(yield_bua))/sqrt(length(yield_bua)),na.rm=T))
write.csv(summary_2018,"C:/Longitudinal/summary_2018.csv")

#optimized yield model
price_mod_2018<-lmer(diff_price_kgha~product*sds_tol+(1|location:soil_pH:rep:sds_tol),data=sds2018,na.action=na.omit)
#summary(y_mod_op) 
joint_tests(price_mod_2018) #optimized for modeling seed trt product effects
drop1(price_mod_2018) #looks good
plot(price_mod_2018)
lsmeans<-emmeans(price_mod_2018,"product") #calculates lsmeans
cld(lsmeans,Letters=letters,alpha=.05) #default is Tukey
pairs(lsmeans) #all pairwise comparisons
weights(lsmeans)

```

#2019
```{r echo=FALSE, warning=TRUE}

### note that only DCF was planted in 2019 -- will need to use KW for within-site analysis

rev_2019<-read.csv("rev_2019.csv",header=T)

#response summary by product and location over all years
summary_2019<-ddply(rev_2019, .(rep,sds_tol,product), summarize,mean_yield=mean(as.numeric(yield_mtha),na.rm=T),se_yield=sd((as.numeric(yield_mtha))/sqrt(length(yield_mtha)),na.rm=T),mean_fdx=mean(as.numeric(fdx),na.rm=T),se_fdx=sd((as.numeric(fdx))/sqrt(length(fdx)),na.rm=T),mean_scn=mean(as.numeric(scn_count_rep),na.rm=T),se_scn=sd((as.numeric(scn_count_rep))/sqrt(length(scn_count_rep)),na.rm=T))

#### re-do this chunk ###
lmm2019<-lmer(I(yield_mtha^2)~product*sds_tol+(1|location:soil_pH:rep:fdx),data=sds2019) #mixed effects model for RCBD -- squaring yield does NOT improve the model -- retain response on observed scale
#summary(lmm2019) #model summary
#boxcox(lmm2019)
#plot(lmm2019)
joint_tests(lmm2019) #emmeans gtype 3 ANOVA
#Anova(lmm2019,type=3) #car anove
#anova(lmm2019) #regular type 1 ANOVA -- no p-value, but not that much different
#drop1(lmm2019) #parameter selection
lsmeans<-emmeans(lmm2019,"sds_tol") #calculates lsmeans
cld(lsmeans,Letters=letters,alpha=.05,covar=T) #default is Tukey
pairs(lsmeans) #all pairwise comparisons
weights(lsmeans)

emm <- emmeans(lmm2019, ~ product|sds_tol)
pairs_emm <- contrast(emm, method = "pairwise")
prod_loc<-summary(pairs_emm, adjust = "tukey")  # or "none", "sidak", etc.
cld(emm, adjust = "tukey")

#KW and Dunn's tests for pairwise yield comparisons between site-years
#(sds2019 %>% group_by(location) %>% dunn_test(fdx~product)) 

sds2019_a<-sds2019

##code help from St. Google
cooksd<-cooks.distance(lmm2019,intercept=T) #vector of cooks d values for all obs
cooks_df<-data.frame(cooksd=cooksd)
    #Using the 4/n rule
    threshold <- 4 / length(cooksd)
    influential_obs_indices <- which(cooksd > threshold) #okeee 8 obs

    #Gross plot    
    plot(cooksd, pch = "*", cex = 2, main = "Influential Observations by Cook's Distance")
    abline(h = threshold, col = "red") # Add the chosen cutoff line
    text(x = 1:length(cooksd) + 1, y = cooksd,
         labels = ifelse(cooksd > threshold, names(cooksd), ""),
         col = "red") # Label influential points

    #Identify the top X most influential observations
    top_x_outliers <- 1 # e.g., for the single outlier
    outlierz <- as.integer(names(sort(cooksd,decreasing=TRUE)[1:top_x_outliers]))
    ###interatively optimized for type I error sig. of product (seed treatment) factor
    sds2019_b <- sds2019_a[-outlierz,]
    
###this is the model for 2019, removal of one outlier did not improve power on FDX###
###but it DID for yield at DCF, specifically###

    lmm2019<-lmer(I(yield_mtha^2)~product+fdx+sds_tol+(1|rep),data=sds2019_b)
    summary(lmm2019) 
    joint_tests(lmm2019) #optimized for modeling seed trt product effects
    drop1(lmm2019) #looks good
    plot(lmm2019)
    lsmeans<-emmeans(lmm2019,"product",tran="power",param=2) #calculates lsmeans
    cld(lsmeans,Letters=letters,alpha=.05,covar=T) #default is Tukey
    pairs(lsmeans) #all pairwise comparisons
    weights(lsmeans)
    
#lsmeans2<-emmeans(lmm2019,pairwise~location|product)
#cld(lsmeans2,Letters=letters,alpha=.05,covar=T)

emm <- emmeans(lmm2019, ~ product|sds_tol)
pairs_emm <- contrast(emm, method = "pairwise")
prod_loc<-summary(pairs_emm, adjust = "tukey")  # or "none", "sidak", etc.
cld(emm, adjust = "tukey")
 
###these are the appropriate contrasts for 2019 -- nonparametric###
#KW and Dunn's tests for pairwise yield and FDX comparisons between products
(sds2019 %>% group_by(location,sds_tol) %>% dunn_test(yield_kgha~product)) 
(sds2019 %>% group_by(location,sds_tol) %>% dunn_test(fdx~product)) 
(sds2019 %>% group_by(location,sds_tol) %>% dunn_test(diff_price_kgha~product))

#yield summary by product and location over all years
summary_2019<-ddply(sds2019, .(location,sds_tol,product), summarize,mean_yield=mean(as.numeric(yield_kgha),na.rm=T),se_yield=sd((as.numeric(yield_kgha))/sqrt(length(yield_kgha)),na.rm=T),mean_fdx=mean(as.numeric(fdx),na.rm=T),se_fdx=sd((as.numeric(fdx))/sqrt(length(fdx)),na.rm=T),mean_bua=mean(as.numeric(yield_bua),na.rm=T),se_bua=sd((as.numeric(yield_bua))/sqrt(length(yield_bua)),na.rm=T))
write.csv(summary_2019,"C:/Longitudinal/summary_2019.csv")

#optimized yield model
price_mod_2019<-lmer(diff_price_kgha~product*sds_tol+(1|rep:sds_tol),data=sds2019,na.action=na.omit)
#summary(y_mod_op) 
joint_tests(price_mod_2019) #optimized for modeling seed trt product effects
drop1(price_mod_2019) #looks good
plot(price_mod_2019)
lsmeans<-emmeans(price_mod_2019,"product") #calculates lsmeans
cld(lsmeans,Letters=letters,alpha=.05) #default is Tukey
pairs(lsmeans) #all pairwise comparisons
weights(lsmeans)

```

#2020 -- now the fun begins
```{r echo=FALSE, warning=TRUE}

### note that FDX was only collected at DCF in 2020 as well -- will need to use KW for within-site analysis again

lmm2020<-lmer(I(yield_mtha^2)~product*location+(1|location:soil_pH:rep:scn_count_rep:mean_Fv_ratio:sds_tol),data=sds2020,na.action=na.omit) #mixed effects model for RCBD
#summary(lmm2020) #model summary
#boxcox(lmm2020)
#plot(lmm2020)
joint_tests(lmm2020) #emmeans gtype 3 ANOVA
#Anova(lmm2020,type=3) #car anove
#anova(lmm2020) #regular type 1 ANOVA -- no p-value, but not that much different
#drop1(lmm2020) #parameter selection
lsmeans<-emmeans(lmm2020,"product") #calculates lsmeans
cld(lsmeans,Letters=letters,alpha=.05,covar=T) #default is Tukey
pairs(lsmeans) #all pairwise comparisons
weights(lsmeans)

#KW and Dunn's tests for pairwise yield comparisons between site-years
#(sds2020 %>% group_by(location) %>% dunn_test(fdx~product)) 

sds2020_a<-sds2020

##code help from St. Google
cooksd<-cooks.distance(lmm2020,intercept=T) #vector of cooks d values for all obs
cooks_df<-data.frame(cooksd=cooksd)
    #Using the 4/n rule
    threshold <- 4 / length(cooksd)
    influential_obs_indices <- which(cooksd > threshold) #okeee 63 obs

    #Gross plot    
    plot(cooksd, pch = "*", cex = 2, main = "Influential Observations by Cook's Distance")
    abline(h = threshold, col = "red") # Add the chosen cutoff line
    text(x = 1:length(cooksd) + 1, y = cooksd,
         labels = ifelse(cooksd > threshold, names(cooksd), ""),
         col = "red") # Label influential points

    #Identify the top X most influential observations
    top_x_outliers <- 16 # e.g., for the single outlier
    outlierz <- as.integer(names(sort(cooksd,decreasing=TRUE)[1:top_x_outliers]))
    ###interatively optimized for type I error sig. of product (seed treatment) factor
    sds2020_b <- sds2020_a[-outlierz,]
    
###this is the model for 2020, removal of 16 outlier did improve power on product/yield###
###but it DID for yield at DCF, specifically###

    lmm2020<-lmer(I(yield_mtha^2)~product*location+sds_tol+(1|location:soil_pH:rep:mean_Fv_ratio:scn_count_rep),data=sds2020_b)
    summary(lmm2020) 
    joint_tests(lmm2020) #optimized for modeling seed trt product effects
    drop1(lmm2020) #looks good
    plot(lmm2020)
    lsmeans<-emmeans(lmm2020,group_by="location","product",tran="power",param=2) #calculates lsmeans
    cld(lsmeans,Letters=letters,alpha=.05,covar=T) #default is Tukey
    pairs(lsmeans) #all pairwise comparisons
    weights(lsmeans)
    
    lsmeans2<-emmeans(lmm2020,pairwise~product|location)
    cld(lsmeans2,Letters=letters,alpha=.05,covar=T)
    
emm <- emmeans(lmm2020, ~ product|sds_tol|location)
pairs_emm <- contrast(emm, method = "pairwise")
prod_loc<-summary(pairs_emm, adjust = "tukey")  # or "none", "sidak", etc.
cld(emm, adjust = "tukey")
 
###these are the appropriate contrasts for 2020 -- nonparametric###
#KW and Dunn's tests for pairwise FDX comparisons between products#
(sds2020 %>% group_by(location,sds_tol) %>% dunn_test(yield_kgha~product)) 
(sds2020 %>% group_by(location,sds_tol) %>% dunn_test(fdx~product))
(sds2020 %>% group_by(location,sds_tol) %>% dunn_test(diff_price_kgha~product))

#yield summary by product and location over all years
summary_2020<-ddply(sds2020, .(location,sds_tol,product), summarize,mean_yield=mean(as.numeric(yield_kgha),na.rm=T),se_yield=sd((as.numeric(yield_kgha))/sqrt(length(yield_kgha)),na.rm=T),mean_fdx=mean(as.numeric(fdx),na.rm=T),se_fdx=sd((as.numeric(fdx))/sqrt(length(fdx)),na.rm=T),mean_bua=mean(as.numeric(yield_bua),na.rm=T),se_bua=sd((as.numeric(yield_bua))/sqrt(length(yield_bua)),na.rm=T))
write.csv(summary_2020,"C:/Longitudinal/summary_2020.csv")

#optimized yield model
price_mod_2020<-lmer(diff_price_kgha~product*sds_tol+(1|location:soil_pH:mean_Fv_ratio:scn_count_rep:rep:sds_tol:fdx),data=sds2020,na.action=na.omit)
#summary(y_mod_op) 
joint_tests(price_mod_2020) #optimized for modeling seed trt product effects
drop1(price_mod_2020) #looks good
plot(price_mod_2020)
lsmeans<-emmeans(price_mod_2020,"product") #calculates lsmeans
cld(lsmeans,Letters=letters,alpha=.05) #default is Tukey
pairs(lsmeans) #all pairwise comparisons
weights(lsmeans)

(sds2020 %>% group_by(location) %>% dunn_test(yield_mtha~sds_tol)) 
(sds2020 %>% group_by(location) %>% dunn_test(fdx~sds_tol)) 
    
```

#2021 -- let's go!!
```{r echo=FALSE, warning=TRUE}

### note that FDX was only collected at DCF in 2021 as well -- will need to use KW for within-site analysis again

lmm2021<-lmer(I(yield_mtha^2)~sds_tol:location+(1|mean_Fv_ratio:scn_count_rep)+(1|location:soil_pH:rep:product),data=sds2021) #mixed effects model for RCBD
summary(lmm2021) #model summary
#boxcox(lmm2021)
plot(lmm2021)
joint_tests(lmm2021) #emmeans gtype 3 ANOVA
#Anova(lmm2021,type=3) #car anove
#anova(lmm2021) #regular type 1 ANOVA -- no p-value, but not that much different
drop1(lmm2021) #parameter selection
#lsmeans<-emmeans(lmm2021,"product") #calculates lsmeans
#cld(lsmeans,Letters=letters,alpha=.05,covar=T) #default is Tukey
#pairs(lsmeans) #all pairwise comparisons
#weights(lsmeans)

#KW and Dunn's tests for pairwise yield comparisons between site-years
#(sds2021 %>% group_by(location) %>% dunn_test(fdx~product)) 

sds2021_a<-sds2021

##code help from St. Google
cooksd<-cooks.distance(lmm2021,intercept=T) #vector of cooks d values for all obs
cooks_df<-data.frame(cooksd=cooksd)
    #Using the 4/n rule
    threshold <- 4 / length(cooksd)
    influential_obs_indices <- which(cooksd > threshold) #woof 117 obs

    #Gross plot    
    plot(cooksd, pch = "*", cex = 2, main = "Influential Observations by Cook's Distance")
    abline(h = threshold, col = "red") # Add the chosen cutoff line
    text(x = 1:length(cooksd) + 1, y = cooksd,
         labels = ifelse(cooksd > threshold, names(cooksd), ""),
         col = "red") # Label influential points

    #Identify the top X most influential observations
    top_x_outliers <- 15 # e.g., for 15 outliers (cooksD>0.05)
    outlierz <- as.integer(names(sort(cooksd,decreasing=TRUE)[1:top_x_outliers]))
    ###interatively optimized for type I error sig. of product (seed treatment) factor
    sds2021_b <- sds2021_a[-outlierz,]
    
###this is the model for 2021, removal of 15 outliers improve power on product~yield AND sds_tol~yield###
###but it DID for yield at DCF, specifically###

    lmm2021<-lmer(I(yield_mtha^2)~product+sds_tol*location+(1|location:soil_pH:rep:mean_Fv_ratio:scn_count_rep),data=sds2021_b)
    #summary(lmm2021) 
    joint_tests(lmm2021) #optimized for modeling seed trt product effects
    drop1(lmm2021) #looks good
    plot(lmm2021)
    lsmeans<-emmeans(lmm2021,"product",tran="power",param=2) #calculates lsmeans
    cld(lsmeans,Letters=letters,alpha=.05,covar=T) #default is Tukey
    pairs(lsmeans) #all pairwise comparisons
    weights(lsmeans)
    
    lsmeans2<-emmeans(lmm2021,pairwise~product|sds_tol)
    cld(lsmeans2,group_by=location,Letters=letters,alpha=.05,covar=T)
    
emm <- emmeans(lmm2021, ~ sds_tol|product|location)
pairs_emm <- contrast(emm, method = "pairwise")
prod_loc<-summary(pairs_emm, adjust = "tukey")  # or "none", "sidak", etc.
cld(emm, adjust = "tukey")
 
###these are the appropriate contrasts for 2021 -- nonparametric###
#KW and Dunn's tests for pairwise FDX comparisons between products#

(sds2021 %>% group_by(location,sds_tol) %>% dunn_test(yield_kgha~product)) 
(sds2021 %>% group_by(location,sds_tol) %>% dunn_test(fdx~product)) 
(sds2021 %>% group_by(location,sds_tol) %>% dunn_test(diff_price_kgha~product)) 
     

#yield summary by product and location over all years
summary_2021<-ddply(sds2021, .(location,sds_tol,product), summarize,mean_yield=mean(as.numeric(yield_kgha),na.rm=T),se_yield=sd((as.numeric(yield_kgha))/sqrt(length(yield_kgha)),na.rm=T),mean_fdx=mean(as.numeric(fdx),na.rm=T),se_fdx=sd((as.numeric(fdx))/sqrt(length(fdx)),na.rm=T),mean_bua=mean(as.numeric(yield_bua),na.rm=T),se_bua=sd((as.numeric(yield_bua))/sqrt(length(yield_bua)),na.rm=T))
write.csv(summary_2021,"C:/Longitudinal/summary_2021.csv")

summary_2021_b<-ddply(sds2021_b, .(location,product), summarize,mean_yield=mean(as.numeric(yield_bua),na.rm=T),se_yield=sd((as.numeric(yield_bua))/sqrt(length(yield_bua)),na.rm=T),mean_fdx=mean(as.numeric(fdx),na.rm=T),se_fdx=sd((as.numeric(fdx))/sqrt(length(fdx)),na.rm=T))
                    
summary_2021_c<-ddply(sds2021_b, .(product), summarize,mean_yield=mean(as.numeric(yield_bua),na.rm=T),se_yield=sd((as.numeric(yield_bua))/sqrt(length(yield_bua)),na.rm=T),mean_fdx=mean(as.numeric(fdx),na.rm=T),se_fdx=sd((as.numeric(fdx))/sqrt(length(fdx)),na.rm=T))

#optimized yield model
price_mod_2021<-lmer(diff_price_kgha~product+(1|location:soil_pH:mean_Fv_ratio:scn_count_rep:rep:sds_tol:fdx),data=sds2021,na.action=na.omit)
#summary(y_mod_op) 
joint_tests(price_mod_2021) #optimized for modeling seed trt product effects
drop1(price_mod_2021) #looks good
plot(price_mod_2021)
lsmeans<-emmeans(price_mod_2021,"product") #calculates lsmeans
cld(lsmeans,Letters=letters,alpha=.05) #default is Tukey
pairs(lsmeans) #all pairwise comparisons
weights(lsmeans)  

```

#2023 -- c'mon foliar disease ratings :/
```{r echo=FALSE, warning=TRUE}

lmm2023<-lmer(I(yield_mtha^2)~sds_tol*location+(1|location:soil_pH:rep:scn_count_rep:mean_Fv_ratio:fdx:product),data=sds2023) #mixed effects model for RCBD
summary(lmm2023) #model summary
#boxcox(lmm2023)
plot(lmm2023)
joint_tests(lmm2023) #emmeans gtype 3 ANOVA
#Anova(lmm2023,type=3) #car anove
#anova(lmm2023) #regular type 1 ANOVA -- no p-value, but not that much different
drop1(lmm2023) #parameter selection
lsmeans<-emmeans(lmm2023,"location") #calculates lsmeans
cld(lsmeans,Letters=letters,alpha=.05,covar=T) #default is Tukey
pairs(lsmeans) #all pairwise comparisons
weights(lsmeans)

#KW and Dunn's tests for pairwise yield comparisons between site-years
#(sds2023 %>% group_by(location) %>% dunn_test(fdx~product)) 
lmm2023<-lmer(I(log(fdx))~product+mean_Fv_ratio+(1|location:soil_pH:scn_count_rep:rep:sds_tol),data=sds2023) #mixed effects model for RCBD
#summary(lmm2022) #model summary
#boxcox(lmm2022)
#plot(lmm2022)
joint_tests(lmm2023) #emmeans gtype 3 ANOVA
#Anova(lmm2022,type=3) #car anove
#anova(lmm2022) #regular type 1 ANOVA -- no p-value, but not that much different
drop1(lmm2023) #parameter selection
lsmeans<-emmeans(lmm2023,"product") #calculates lsmeans
cld(lsmeans,Letters=letters,alpha=.05,covar=T) #default is Tukey
pairs(lsmeans) #all pairwise comparisons
weights(lsmeans)

(sds2023 %>% group_by(location) %>% dunn_test(yield_mtha~product))

sds2023_a<-sds2023

##code help from St. Google
cooksd<-cooks.distance(lmm2023,intercept=T) #vector of cooks d values for all obs
cooks_df<-data.frame(cooksd=cooksd)
    #Using the 4/n rule
    threshold <- 4 / length(cooksd)
    influential_obs_indices <- which(cooksd > threshold) #19 obs

    #Gross plot    
    plot(cooksd, pch = "*", cex = 2, main = "Influential Observations by Cook's Distance")
    abline(h = threshold, col = "red") # Add the chosen cutoff line
    text(x = 1:length(cooksd) + 1, y = cooksd,
         labels = ifelse(cooksd > threshold, names(cooksd), ""),
         col = "red") # Label influential points

    #Identify the top X most influential observations
    top_x_outliers <- 6 # e.g., for 6 outlier
    outlierz <- as.integer(names(sort(cooksd,decreasing=TRUE)[1:top_x_outliers]))
    ###interatively optimized for type I error sig. of product (seed treatment) factor
    sds2023_b <- sds2023_a[-outlierz,]
    
###this is the model for 2023, removal of 15 outliers improve power on product~yield AND sds_tol~yield###
###but it DID for yield at DCF, specifically###

    lmm2023<-lmer(I(yield_mtha^2)~product+location+fdx+mean_Fv_ratio+(1|sds_tol)+(1|soil_pH:rep:scn_count_rep),data=sds2023_b)
    #summary(lmm2023) 
    joint_tests(lmm2023) #optimized for modeling seed trt product effects
    drop1(lmm2023) #looks good
    plot(lmm2023)
    lsmeans<-emmeans(lmm2023,"product",tran="power",param=2) #calculates lsmeans
    cld(lsmeans,Letters=letters,alpha=.05,covar=T) #default is Tukey
    pairs(lsmeans) #all pairwise comparisons
    weights(lsmeans)
    
    lsmeans2<-emmeans(lmm2023,pairwise~product|location)
    cld(lsmeans2,Letters=letters,alpha=.05,covar=T)
 
###these are the appropriate contrasts for 2023 -- nonparametric###
#KW and Dunn's tests for pairwise FDX comparisons between products#
    
#finally have enough FDX obs to model, so I guess it's fine that the KW and Dunn's tests aren't working. IIRC, zeroes may not be compatible with the test
#(sds2023_b %>% group_by(location,sds_tol) %>% dunn_test(fdx~product)) 
    
fdx2023<- df_no_zeroes %>% subset(year == "2023")
fdx2023<- fdx2023[-outlierz,]
fdx2023<- fdx2023 %>% mutate(log_fdx = as.numeric(log(fdx)))

glmm2023<-glmer(log_fdx~product*location+(1|soil_pH:rep:mean_Fv_ratio:scn_count_rep),data=fdx2023,family=gaussian(link="identity"))
joint_tests(glmm2023,tran="log")
lsmeans3<-emmeans(glmm2023,pairwise~product|location)
cld(lsmeans3,Letters=letters,alpha=.05,covar=T)
#boxcox(glmm2023)          
  
###these are the appropriate contrasts for 2024 -- nonparametric###
#KW and Dunn's tests for pairwise FDX comparisons between products#
(sds2023 %>% group_by(location,sds_tol) %>% dunn_test(yield_mtha~product)) 
(sds2023 %>% group_by(location,product) %>% dunn_test(fdx~sds_tol)) 
(sds2023 %>% group_by(location,sds_tol) %>% dunn_test(diff_price_kgha~product)) 
     
#yield summary by product and location over all years
summary_2023<-ddply(sds2023, .(location,sds_tol,product), summarize,mean_yield=mean(as.numeric(yield_kgha),na.rm=T),se_yield=sd((as.numeric(yield_kgha))/sqrt(length(yield_kgha)),na.rm=T),mean_fdx=mean(as.numeric(fdx),na.rm=T),se_fdx=sd((as.numeric(fdx))/sqrt(length(fdx)),na.rm=T),mean_bua=mean(as.numeric(yield_bua),na.rm=T),se_bua=sd((as.numeric(yield_bua))/sqrt(length(yield_bua)),na.rm=T))
write.csv(summary_2023,"C:/Longitudinal/summary_2023.csv")


#optimized yield model
price_mod_2023<-lmer(diff_price_kgha~product+(1|fdx)+(1|location:mean_Fv_ratio:scn_count_rep:rep),data=sds2023,na.action=na.omit)
#summary(y_mod_op) 
joint_tests(price_mod_2023) #optimized for modeling seed trt product effects
drop1(price_mod_2023) #looks good
plot(price_mod_2023)
lsmeans<-emmeans(price_mod_2023,"product") #calculates lsmeans
cld(lsmeans,Letters=letters,alpha=.05) #default is Tukey
pairs(lsmeans) #all pairwise comparisons
weights(lsmeans)  
    
```

#2024 -- finally
```{r echo=FALSE, warning=TRUE}

lmm2024<-lmer(I(yield_mtha^2)~product*location+(1|soil_pH:mean_Fv_ratio:scn_count_rep:rep:fdx),data=sds2024,na.action=na.omit) #mixed effects model for RCBD
summary(lmm2024) #model summary
#boxcox(lmm2024)
plot(lmm2024)
joint_tests(lmm2024) #emmeans gtype 3 ANOVA
#Anova(lmm2024,type=3) #car anove
#anova(lmm2024) #regular type 1 ANOVA -- no p-value, but not that much different
drop1(lmm2024) #parameter selection
lsmeans<-emmeans(lmm2024,"product") #calculates lsmeans
cld(lsmeans,Letters=letters,alpha=.05,covar=T) #default is Tukey
pairs(lsmeans) #all pairwise comparisons
weights(lsmeans)

#KW and Dunn's tests for pairwise yield comparisons between site-years
#(sds2024 %>% group_by(location) %>% dunn_test(fdx~product)) 

sds2024_a<-sds2024

##code help from St. Google
cooksd<-cooks.distance(lmm2024,intercept=T) #vector of cooks d values for all obs
cooks_df<-data.frame(cooksd=cooksd)
    #Using the 4/n rule
    threshold <- 4 / length(cooksd)
    influential_obs_indices <- which(cooksd > threshold) #17 obs

    #Gross plot    
    plot(cooksd, pch = "*", cex = 2, main = "Influential Observations by Cook's Distance")
    abline(h = threshold, col = "red") # Add the chosen cutoff line
    text(x = 1:length(cooksd) + 1, y = cooksd,
         labels = ifelse(cooksd > threshold, names(cooksd), ""),
         col = "red") # Label influential points

    #Identify the top X most influential observations
    top_x_outliers <- 5 # e.g., for 4 outliers
    outlierz <- as.integer(names(sort(cooksd,decreasing=TRUE)[1:top_x_outliers]))
    ###interatively optimized for type I error sig. of product (seed treatment) factor
    sds2024_b <- sds2024_a[-outlierz,]
    
###this is the model for 2024, removal of 15 outliers improve power on product~yield AND sds_tol~yield###
###but it DID for yield at DCF, specifically###

    lmm2024<-lmer(I(yield_mtha^2)~product+location+(1:fdx)+(1|soil_pH:mean_Fv_ratio:scn_count_rep:rep),data=sds2024_b)
    #summary(lmm2024) 
    joint_tests(lmm2024) #optimized for modeling seed trt product effects
    drop1(lmm2024) #looks good
    plot(lmm2024)
    lsmeans<-emmeans(lmm2024,"product") #calculates lsmeans
    cld(lsmeans,Letters=letters,alpha=.05,covar=T) #default is Tukey
    pairs(lsmeans) #all pairwise comparisons
    weights(lsmeans)
    
    lsmeans2<-emmeans(lmm2024,pairwise~product|location)
    cld(lsmeans2,Letters=letters,alpha=.05,covar=T)
 
###these are the appropriate contrasts for 2024 -- nonparametric###
#KW and Dunn's tests for pairwise FDX comparisons between products#
    
#finally have enough FDX obs to model, so I guess it's fine that the KW and Dunn's tests aren't working. IIRC, zeroes may not be compatible with the test
#sds2024_b %>% group_by(location) %>% dunn_test(fdx~product)
    
fdx2024<- df_no_zeroes %>% subset(year == "2024")
fdx2024<- fdx2024[-outlierz,]
fdx2024<- fdx2024 %>% mutate(log_fdx = as.numeric(log(fdx)))

glmm2024<-glmer(log_fdx~product+location+location:product+scn_count_rep+(1|soil_pH:mean_Fv_ratio),data=fdx2024,family=gaussian(link="identity"))
joint_tests(glmm2024,tran="log")

prod_loc<-summary(pairs_emm, adjust = "tukey")  # or "none", "sidak", etc.
cld(emm, adjust = "tukey")

#(sds2024 %>% group_by(location) %>% dunn_test(fdx~product)) 
(sds2024 %>% group_by(location) %>% dunn_test(diff_price_kgha~product)) 

#yield summary by product and location over all years
summary_2024<-ddply(sds2024, .(location,sds_tol,product), summarize,mean_yield=mean(as.numeric(yield_kgha),na.rm=T),se_yield=sd((as.numeric(yield_kgha))/sqrt(length(yield_kgha)),na.rm=T),mean_fdx=mean(as.numeric(fdx),na.rm=T),se_fdx=sd((as.numeric(fdx))/sqrt(length(fdx)),na.rm=T),mean_bua=mean(as.numeric(yield_bua),na.rm=T),se_bua=sd((as.numeric(yield_bua))/sqrt(length(yield_bua)),na.rm=T))
write.csv(summary_2024,"C:/Longitudinal/summary_2024.csv")

#optimized yield model
```

### SDS foliar response modeling, summary, and KW tests for tables 
```{r eval=FALSE, include=FALSE}
#lmod_fdx<-lmer(log_fdx~product+mean_Fv_ratio+scn_count_rep+(1|year)+(1|location)+(1|rep),data=df) #mixed effects model for RCBD -- squaring fdx does NOT improve the model -- retain response on observed scale
#summary(lmod_fdx) #model summary
#plot(lmod_fdx)
#drop1(lmod_fdx) #parameter selection
#joint_tests(lmod_fdx) #emmeans gtype 3 ANOVA
#Anova(lmod_fdx,type=3) #car type 3 ANOVA 
#lsmeans<-emmeans(lmod_fdx,"product") #calculates lsmeans
#cld(lsmeans,Letters=letters,alpha=.05,covar=T) #default is Tukey
#pairs(lsmeans) #all pairwise comparisons
#weights(lsmeans)

#overall f_dx summary by product over both years
fdx_sum<-ddply(df, .(product), summarize,mean_fdx=mean(as.numeric(fdx),na.rm=T),se_fdx=sd(as.numeric(fdx),na.rm=T)/sqrt(length(fdx)))

#FDX summary by year
fdx_sum2<-ddply(df, .(year), summarize,mean_fdx=mean(as.numeric(fdx),na.rm=T),se_yield=sd(as.numeric(fdx),na.rm=T)/sqrt(length(fdx)))

#fdx summary by site-year and product
fdx_sum3<-ddply(df, .(year,location,product), summarize,mean_fdx=mean(as.numeric(fdx),na.rm=T),se_fdx=sd((as.numeric(fdx))/sqrt(length(fdx)),na.rm=T))

#fdx summary by site-year and product
fdx_sum4<-ddply(df, .(year,location,sds_tol,product), summarize,mean_fdx=mean(as.numeric(fdx),na.rm=T),se_fdx=sd((as.numeric(fdx))/sqrt(length(fdx)),na.rm=T))

df_no_nas<-subset(df_balevo,!is.na(fdx)) #subsets because the pipe below won't handle NAs
#KW and Dunn's tests for pairwise fdx comparisons between site-years
(df_no_nas %>% group_by(year,location,sds_tol) %>% dunn_test(fdx~product)) #there's something wrong with the way that fdx is structured by location -- na removal and zero removal don't help
```

### let's try some zero-inflated model fitting based on Poisson and maybe negative binomial distros
```{r eval=FALSE, include=FALSE}

df_mod<-df
  
#prepping the df
df_mod<-df_mod %>% mutate(fv_bin = as.integer(mean_Fv_ratio > 0)) #sets up dummy vars for pathogen presence/absence
df_mod<-df_mod %>% mutate(scn_bin = as.integer(scn_count_rep > 2000))
df_mod<-df_mod %>% mutate(fdx_sig_bin = as.integer(fdx > 0.00000001)) #and for FDX over 10 (see Dangal et al. 2025 pre-pub.)
df_mod<-df_mod %>% mutate(P_yield_bump = as.integer(yield_diff > 0)) #and for positive yield response
df_mod<-df_mod %>% dplyr::mutate(fv_int = as.integer(mean_Fv_ratio)) #Fv ratio as an integer-factor
#nonzero_FvSCN_df<-df %>% filter(mean_Fv_ratio > 0,scn_count_rep > 0) ##for future reference/model fitting?

facs<-c("fv_int","fv_bin","scn_bin") #string of column headers
df_mod[facs]<-lapply(df_mod[facs],factor) #assigns factors

###may need to drop NAs in response vars for modeling 
df_mod <- drop_na(df_mod)

###from glmmTMB documentation
df_mod <- df_mod[order(df_mod$yield_diff),] #reorders df by yield_diff for plotting

fit_mod <- lmer(fdx_sig_bin~fv_int+scn_bin+P_yield_bump+(1|year)+(1|location)+(1|rep),data=df_mod)
  
    summary(fit_mod) 
    plot(fit_mod) #ugly
    drop1(fit_mod) #parameter selection -- drop the dummies in this case
    joint_tests(fit_mod) #emmeans type 3 ANOVA
    Anova(fit_mod,type=3) #car type 3 ANOVA 
    lsmeans<-emmeans(fit_mod,"fv_int") #calculates lsmeans
    cld(lsmeans,Letters=letters,alpha=.05,covar=T) #default is Tukey
    pairs(lsmeans) #all pairwise comparisons
    weights(lsmeans)
  
fit_mod2022 <- lmer(yield_bua~product+mean_Fv_ratio+scn_count_rep+(1|year)+(1|location)+(1|rep),data=df_mod,subset=year!=2017|2018|2019|2020|2021|2023|2024)

    summary(fit_mod2022) 
    plot(fit_mod2022) #ugly
    #drop1(fit_mod2) #parameter selection -- drop the continuous vars in this case
    joint_tests(fit_mod2022) #emmeans type 3 ANOVA
    Anova(fit_mod2022,type=3) #car type 3 ANOVA 
    lsmeans<-emmeans(fit_mod2022,"product") #calculates lsmeans
    cld(lsmeans,Letters=letters,alpha=.05,covar=T) #default is Tukey
    pairs(lsmeans) #all pairwise comparisons
    weights(lsmeans)
    
tmb_mod<-glmmTMB(yield_bua~product+fv_int+scn_count_rep+P_yield_bump+(1|year)+(1|location)+(1|rep),df_mod,family=gaussian,na.action="na.exclude") #poisson distribution may be enough to handle count data

    summary(tmb_mod)
    joint_tests(tmb_mod)
    lsmeans<-emmeans(tmb_mod,"fv_int") #calculates lsmeans
    cld(lsmeans,Letters=letters,alpha=.05) #default is Tukey
    pairs(lsmeans) #all pairwise comparisons
    weights(lsmeans)


# 1. Extract predicted probability of presence (from binomial model)
emm_fdx <- emmeans(tmb_mod, ~ product, type = "response")
prob_fdx_over_10 <- as.data.frame(emm_fdx) %>%
  dplyr::rename(Pr_over_10 = ~frame) %>%
  dplyr::select(product, Pr_over_10)
# 2. Extract predicted mean if present (from gamma model)
emm_df_mod <- emmeans(tmb_mod, ~ product, type = "response")
gamma_df_mod <- as.data.frame(emm_df_mod) %>%
  dplyr::rename(Mean_If_Present = yield_diff) %>%
  dplyr::select(product, Mean_If_Present)
# 3. Combine and compute overall expected Zearalenone
expected_dfbmrzen <- left_join(prob_dfbmrzen, gamma_dfbmrzen, by = "Treatment") %>%
  mutate(Expected_Total = Pr_Present * Mean_If_Present) %>%
  arrange(desc(Expected_Total))
# 4. View results
print(expected_dfbmrzen)
joint_tests(binomial_mod)
joint_tests(gamma_modelbmrzen)
exp_fdx <- ggplot(expected_dfbmrzen, aes(x = Pr_Present, y = Mean_If_Present)) +
  geom_point(aes(size = Expected_Total, color = Pr_Present), alpha = 0.8) +
  geom_text_repel(aes(label = Treatment_clean), size = 3.5, max.overlaps = 20) +
  scale_color_viridis_c(option = "plasma", name = "Probability of ZEN Presence") +
  scale_size(range = c(3, 8), name = "Expected ZEN (ppb)") +
  labs(title = "BMR Zearalenone (ppb) Risk:\nProbability vs Mean When Present by Treatment",
       x = "Probability of ZEN Presence",
       y = "Mean ZEN (ppb) When Present") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    axis.text = element_text(face = "bold")
  )
exp_fdx

```

### summary stats for plotting -- turn on if needed
```{r include=FALSE}
mean(df_base$yield_kgha,na.rm=T)
sd(df_base$yield_kgha,na.rm=T)
se(df_base$yield_kgha)

mean(df_ilevo$yield_kgha,na.rm=T)
sd(df_ilevo$yield_kgha,na.rm=T)
se(df_ilevo$yield_kgha)

mean(df_saltro$yield_kgha,na.rm=T)
sd(df_saltro$yield_kgha,na.rm=T)
se(df_saltro$yield_kgha)

#yield summary by site-year and product for plotting
trt_yield_sum<-ddply(df, .(year,location,product), summarize,mean_yield=mean(as.numeric(yield_kgha),na.rm=T),se_yield=sd((as.numeric(yield_kgha))/sqrt(length(yield_kgha)),na.rm=T),mean_diff=mean(yield_diff,na.rm=T),mean_fdx=mean(as.numeric(fdx),na.rm=T),mean_fdx_diff=mean(as.numeric(fdx_diff),na.rm=T),mean_scn=mean(as.numeric(scn_count_rep),na.rm=T),mean_fv=mean(as.numeric(mean_Fv_ratio),na.rm=T),mean_diff_kgha=mean(as.numeric(diff_price_kgha),na.rm=T))
    trt_yield_sum<-arrange(trt_yield_sum,mean_diff)
    write.csv(trt_yield_sum,"C:/Longitudinal/trt_means.csv")
```

### Piano plots of ILEVO and Saltro
```{r message=TRUE, warning=FALSE, include=FALSE}
trt_means<-read.csv("trt_means.csv",header=T) #weird, I know, but it works
facs<-c("X","year","location","product") #string of column headers
trt_means[facs]<-lapply(trt_means[facs],factor) #assigns factors

#lets get rid of the MSE column since it's full of NAs and we'll be stripping NA lines for plotting anyway...
#trt_means<-trt_means[,-1] ### turn this off if we want error bars for some reason

str(trt_means) #views summary of the strings

#this didn't do what I think it would, but may still be useful as a visual key
trt_means<-trt_means %>% 
  mutate(color = case_when(
    location == "ALLEGAN" ~ "#332288",
    location == "CLINTON" ~ "#117733",
    location == "DECATUR" ~ "#DDCC77",
    location == "HILLSDALE" ~ "#88CCEE",
    location == "INGHAM" ~ "#999999",
    location == "LENAWEE" ~ "#CC6677",
    location == "MONTCALM" ~ "#AA4499",
    location == "MSU_AGR_FARM" ~ "#882255",
    location == "MSU_PLP_FARM" ~ "#994F00",
    location == "SAGINAW" ~ "#FFC20A",
    location == "SANILAC" ~ "#1A85FF",
    location == "STJOSEPH" ~ "#E66100",
  ))

#custom labels and colors to be consistent across plots and figures
locationz<-c("ALLEGAN","CLINTON","DECATUR","HILLSDALE","INGHAM","LENAWEE","MONTCALM","MSU AGR","MSU PLP","SAGINAW","SANILAC","ST JOSEPH") #ILEVO locations
colorz<-c("#332288","#117733","#DDCC77","#88CCEE","#999999","#CC6677","#AA4499","#882255","#994F00","#FFC20A","#1A85FF","#E66100") #sets up a pallette for ILEVO locations (i.e. all)
#df_colorz<-tibble(location,color) #as a tibble for indexing

trt_means<-trt_means %>% drop_na(mean_diff_kgha) #drops lines with NAs in mean diff column
#FIXED -- even though I added an argument in ggplot to drop NAs in the plot, the length of the df is still the same, so the sample size (n = ) is incorrect when called for plotting

base_plot<-filter(trt_means,product=="Base") #subsets the base yield response means
ilevo_plot<-filter(trt_means,product=="ILEVO") #subsets for piano-plotting
saltro_plot<-filter(trt_means,product=="Saltro")

ilevo_2017<-filter(ilevo_plot,year=="2017")
ilevo_2018<-filter(ilevo_plot,year=="2018")
ilevo_2019<-filter(ilevo_plot,year=="2019")
ilevo_2020<-filter(ilevo_plot,year=="2020")
ilevo_2021<-filter(ilevo_plot,year=="2021")
ilevo_2022<-filter(ilevo_plot,year=="2022")
ilevo_2023<-filter(ilevo_plot,year=="2023")
ilevo_2024<-filter(ilevo_plot,year=="2024") 

saltro_2017<-filter(saltro_plot,year=="2017")
saltro_2018<-filter(saltro_plot,year=="2018")
saltro_2019<-filter(saltro_plot,year=="2019")
saltro_2020<-filter(saltro_plot,year=="2020")
saltro_2021<-filter(saltro_plot,year=="2021")
saltro_2022<-filter(saltro_plot,year=="2022")
saltro_2023<-filter(saltro_plot,year=="2023")
saltro_2024<-filter(saltro_plot,year=="2024") 

leg<-expression(paste("kg/ha"))

  piano<-ggplot(data=ilevo_plot, aes(x=X,y=mean_diff_kgha)) + 
   geom_bar(aes(fill=mean_diff),position="dodge",stat="identity",color="darkgray",fill="lightgray") +
    aes(x=fct_reorder(X,mean_diff_kgha)) +
    #geom_bar(data=subset(ilevo_plot,!is.na(mean_diff)|!is.na(mean_fdx_diff)),aes(x=fct_reorder(X,mean_fdx_diff),y=10*mean_fdx_diff),position="dodge",stat="identity",fill="red") +      #this line does what I wanted it to do, but would make more sense to plot by siteyear
    #geom_smooth(method='lm', formula= mean_fdx~X) + #this line does not work
    #scale_y_continuous(name = "Relative yield difference (kg/ha)",  #Label for the primary axis
    #sec.axis = sec_axis(~./50, name = "F. virguliforme (fg/ng soil DNA)",breaks=waiver())  # Transformation for the secondary axis
    #) +
    ylim(-1000,1200) +
    labs(title=element_text("Differential soybean yield response to ILEVO: 2017-2024"),y=expression(paste("Relative yield difference (kg/ha)")),x=sprintf("Mean yield response by location and replicate (n = %s)",length(ilevo_plot$mean_diff_kgha))) +
    theme_classic2() +
    theme(axis.text.y=element_text(color="black",size=14)) +
    theme(axis.text.x=element_blank()) + 
    theme(axis.ticks.x=element_blank()) +
    theme(axis.title.y=element_text(color="black",size=18)) +
    theme(axis.title.x=element_text(color="black",size=18)) +
    theme(plot.title=element_text(color="black",size=18,hjust=0.5,,vjust=-4,face="bold")) +
    geom_hline(yintercept=mean(ilevo_plot$mean_diff_kgha,na.rm=T)) + #numeric difference between treatment means (basically 0)
    geom_hline(linetype="dashed",yintercept=mean(ilevo_plot$mean_diff_kgha,na.rm=T)+(1.96*se(ilevo_plot$mean_diff_kgha))) + #line at the upper 95% CI limit of the mean yield
    geom_hline(linetype="dashed",yintercept=mean(ilevo_plot$mean_diff_kgha,na.rm=T)-(1.96*se(ilevo_plot$mean_diff_kgha))) #line at the lower 95% CI limit of the mean yield
  piano
  piano_leg<-piano
  piano_noleg<-piano +
    theme(legend.position="none")

ggsave("piano_leg.png",path="C:/Longitudinal",plot=piano_leg,width=12,height=8,units="in",dpi=320)
ggsave("piano_noleg.png",path="C:/Longitudinal",plot=piano_noleg,width=12,height=8,units="in",dpi=320)

 piano2<-ggplot(data=saltro_plot, aes(x=X,y=mean_diff_kgha)) + 
    geom_bar(aes(fill=mean_diff),position="dodge",stat="identity",color="darkgray",fill="lightgray") +
    aes(x=fct_reorder(X,mean_diff_kgha)) +
    ylim(-2000,2200) +
    labs(title=element_text("Differential soybean yield response to Saltro: 2020-2024"),y=expression(paste("Relative yield difference (kg/ha)")),x=sprintf("Mean yield response by location and replicate (n = %s)",length(saltro_plot$mean_diff))) +
    theme_classic2() +
    theme(axis.text.y=element_text(color="black",size=14)) +
    theme(axis.text.x=element_blank()) + 
    theme(axis.ticks.x=element_blank()) +
    theme(axis.title.y=element_text(color="black",size=18)) +
    theme(axis.title.x=element_text(color="black",size=18)) +
    theme(plot.title=element_text(color="black",size=18,hjust=0.5,vjust=-4,face="bold")) +
    geom_hline(yintercept=mean(saltro_plot$mean_diff_kgha,na.rm=T)) + #numeric difference between treatment means (basically 0)
    geom_hline(linetype="dashed",yintercept=mean(saltro_plot$mean_diff_kgha,na.rm=T)+(1.96*se(saltro_plot$mean_diff_kgha))) + #line at the upper 95% CI limit of the mean yield
    geom_hline(linetype="dashed",yintercept=mean(saltro_plot$mean_diff_kgha,na.rm=T)-(1.96*se(saltro_plot$mean_diff_kgha))) #line at the lower 95% CI limit of the mean yield
  piano2
  piano_leg2<-piano2
  piano_noleg2<-piano2 +
    theme(legend.position="none")
  
ggsave("piano_leg2.png",path="C:/Longitudinal",plot=piano_leg2,width=12,height=8,units="in",dpi=320)
ggsave("piano_noleg2.png",path="C:/Longitudinal",plot=piano_noleg2,width=12,height=8,units="in",dpi=320)
```

### Piano plots color-filled by location
```{r message=TRUE, warning=FALSE, include=FALSE}

  piano<-ggplot(data=ilevo_plot, aes(x=X,y=mean_diff_kgha)) + 
    geom_bar(aes(fill=location,drop=F),position="dodge",stat="identity",color="darkgray") +
    aes(x=fct_reorder(X,mean_diff_kgha)) +
    ylim(-250,400) +
    scale_fill_manual(values=c("#332288","#117733","#DDCC77","#88CCEE","#999999","#CC6677","#AA4499","#882255","#994F00","#FFC20A","#1A85FF","#E66100"),labels=c("ALLEGAN","CLINTON","DECATUR","HILLSDALE","INGHAM","LENAWEE","MONTCALM","MSU AGR","MSU PLP","SAGINAW","SANILAC","ST JOSEPH")) + 
    labs(title=element_text("Differential farm-gate revenue response to ILEVO: 2017-2024"),y=expression(paste("Relative market price difference (USD/ha)")),x=sprintf("Mean yield response by site-year (n = %s)",length(ilevo_plot$mean_diff_kgha))) +
    theme_classic2() +
    theme(legend.text=element_text(size=18)) +
    theme(legend.title=element_blank()) +   
    theme(axis.text.y=element_text(color="black",size=14)) +
    theme(axis.text.x=element_blank()) + 
    theme(axis.ticks.x=element_blank()) +
    theme(axis.title.y=element_text(color="black",size=18)) +
    theme(axis.title.x=element_text(color="black",size=18)) +
    theme(plot.title=element_text(color="black",size=18,hjust=0.5,vjust=-4,face="bold")) +
    geom_hline(yintercept=32) #numeric difference between treatment means (basically 0)
    #geom_hline(linetype="dashed",yintercept=mean(ilevo_plot$mean_diff_kgha,na.rm=T)+(1.96*se(ilevo_plot$mean_diff_kgha))) + #line at the upper 95% CI limit of the mean yield
    #geom_hline(linetype="dashed",yintercept=mean(ilevo_plot$mean_diff_kgha,na.rm=T)-(1.96*se(ilevo_plot$mean_diff_kgha))) #line at the lower 95% CI limit of the mean yield
  piano
  piano_color_leg<-piano
  piano_color_noleg<-piano +
    theme(legend.position="none")
```

### cow pianos!
```{r warning=FALSE}
### THIS looks like a job for cowplot!
  leg3<-get_legend(piano_color_leg + #extracts legend from one plot
  guides(color=colorz) + #sets the layout of the legend itself
  theme(legend.direction="vertical",   #how to arrange the legend key
        legend.justification="center", #this tells cowplot to center the legend...
        legend.box.just="right") + #on the right
  theme(legend.key.size=unit(.25,"in"))) 
  theme(legend.margin=margin(c(0,0,0,1))) #adds some space to the left on the legend
#sets the title
#sets the title
#title<-ggdraw() + 
  #draw_label("Figure Title Here",size=32,fontface="bold") #sets up a function-call with the title
#sets the layout grid
cow_pianoz<-plot_grid(
  piano_noleg, #removes legend from individual plots
  piano_noleg2 + theme(legend.position="none"),
  ncol=1,nrow=2,align='h',scale=1,labels="AUTO",label_size=24,vjust=2,hjust=-1) #arranges all 4 plots together and scales them down a bit to add room between plots
#(cow_yield<-plot_grid(cow_yield,legend2,rel_widths=c(2,0.25)))#no legend
cow_pianoz<-plot_grid(cow_pianoz,ncol=1,rel_widths=c(1,.01)) #adds the title -- rel_heights values control vertical title margins
ggsave("cow_pianoz.png",path="C:/Longitudinal",plot=cow_pianoz,width=28,height=16,units="in",dpi=320)

#sets the layout grid
cow_pianoz_color<-plot_grid(
  piano_color_noleg, #removes legend from individual plots
  piano_color_noleg2 + theme(legend.position="none"),
  ncol=1,nrow=2,align='h',scale=1,labels=c("C","D"),label_size=24,vjust=2,hjust=-1) #arranges all 4 plots together and scales them down a bit to add room between plots
#(cow_yield<-plot_grid(cow_yield,legend2,rel_widths=c(2,0.25)))#no legend
cow_pianoz_color<-plot_grid(cow_pianoz_color,leg3,rel_widths=c(2,0.2)) #adds the title -- rel_heights values control vertical title margins
ggsave("cow_pianoz_color.png",path="C:/Longitudinal",plot=cow_pianoz_color,width=28,height=16,units="in",dpi=320)
```

```{r message=TRUE, warning=FALSE, include=FALSE}
  piano<-ggplot(data=ilevo_plot, aes(x=X,y=mean_diff_kgha)) + 
    geom_bar(aes(fill=year,drop=F),position="dodge",stat="identity",color="darkgray") +
    aes(x=fct_reorder(X,mean_diff_kgha)) +
    ylim(-250,400) +
    scale_fill_manual(values=c("#117733","#DDCC77","#88CCEE","#CC6677","#994F00","#FFC20A","#1A85FF","#E66100"),labels=c("2017","2018","2019","2020","2021","2022","2023","2024")) + 
    labs(title=element_text("Differential farm-gate revenue response to ILEVO: 2017-2024"),y=expression(paste("Relative market price difference (USD/ha)")),x=sprintf("Mean yield response by site-year (n = %s)",length(ilevo_plot$mean_diff_kgha))) +
    theme_classic2() +
    theme(legend.text=element_text(size=18)) +

    theme(axis.title.y=element_text(color="black",size=18)) +

    #geom_hline(linetype="dashed",yintercept=mean(ilevo_plot$mean_diff_kgha,na.rm=T)-(1.96*se(ilevo_plot$mean_diff_kgha))) #line at the lower 95% CI limit of the mean yield

    scale_fill_manual(values=c("#CC6677","#994F00","#FFC20A","#1A85FF","#E66100"),labels=c("2020","2021","2022","2023","2024")) +     labs(title=element_text("Differential farm-gate revenue response to Saltro: 2020-2024"),y=expression(paste("Relative market price difference (USD/ha)")),x=sprintf("Mean yield response by site-year (n = %s)",length(saltro_plot$mean_diff_kgha))) +
    theme_classic2() +
    theme(legend.text=element_text(size=18)) +
    theme(legend.title=element_blank()) +   
    theme(axis.text.y=element_text(color="black",size=14)) +
    theme(axis.text.x=element_blank()) + 
    theme(axis.ticks.x=element_blank()) +
    theme(axis.title.y=element_text(color="black",size=18)) +

    geom_hline(yintercept=37) #numeric difference between treatment means (basically 0)
    #geom_hline(linetype="dashed",yintercept=mean(saltro_plot$mean_diff_kgha,na.rm=T)+(1.96*se(saltro_plot$mean_diff_kgha))) + #line at the upper 95% CI limit of the mean yield
    #geom_hline(linetype="dashed",yintercept=mean(saltro_plot$mean_diff_kgha,na.rm=T)-(1.96*se(saltro_plot$mean_diff_kgha))) #line at the lower 95% CI limit of the mean yield
  piano2
  piano_color_leg2<-piano2
  piano_color_noleg2<-piano2 +
    theme(legend.position="none")
  
ggsave("piano_color_leg2.png",path="C:/Longitudinal",plot=piano_color_leg2,width=12,height=8,units="in",dpi=320)
ggsave("piano_color_noleg2.png",path="C:/Longitudinal",plot=piano_color_noleg2,width=12,height=8,units="in",dpi=320)
```

```{r warning=FALSE}
  guides(color=colorz2) + #sets the layout of the legend itself
```

### function/loop that auto-plots monochrome piano plots of each year
```{r include=FALSE}
#creates a function that makes ggplots and assigns them to a variable to call in cow plot, etc.    
create_ggplot <- function(data,x_col,y_col,fill,title){
          #creates the ggplot
          (plot <- ggplot(data=subset(data,!is.na(mean_diff_kgha)), aes_string(x=x_col,y=y_col,fill=fill))) + 
            geom_bar(aes(fill=mean_diff_kgha),position="dodge",stat="identity",color="darkgray",fill="lightgray") +
            ylim(-250,400) +
            aes(x=fct_reorder(X,mean_diff_kgha)) +  
            labs(title=title,y=expression(paste("Economic return relative to commercial base (USD/ha)")),x=sprintf("Mean yield response by site-year (n = %s)",length(data$mean_diff_kgha))) +
            theme_classic2() +
            theme(axis.text.y=element_text(color="black",size=14)) +
            theme(axis.text.x=element_blank()) + 
            theme(axis.ticks.x=element_blank()) +
            theme(axis.title.y=element_text(color="black",size=18)) +
            theme(axis.title.x=element_text(color="black",size=18)) +
            theme(plot.title=element_text(color="black",size=18,hjust=0.5,face="bold")) +
            geom_hline(yintercept=mean(data$mean_diff,na.rm=T))
            #geom_hline(linetype="dashed",yintercept=mean(data$mean_diff,na.rm=T)+(1.96*se(data$mean_diff))) +
            #geom_hline(linetype="dashed",yintercept=mean(data$mean_diff,na.rm=T)-(1.96*se(data$mean_diff)))
}
```
