### function/loop that auto-plots piano plots of each year with color-fill by location
```{r include=FALSE}

#creates a function that makes ggplots and assigns them to a variable to call in cow plot, etc.    
create_ggplot <- function(data,x_col,y_col,fill,title){
          #creates the ggplot
          plot <- ggplot(data=subset(data,!is.na(mean_diff_kgha)), aes_string(x=x_col,y=y_col,fill=as.factor(data$color),drop=FALSE)) + 
            geom_bar(aes(fill=location,drop=T),position="dodge",stat="identity",color="darkgray") +
            scale_fill_manual(values=c("#332288","#117733","#DDCC77","#88CCEE","#999999","#CC6677","#AA4499","#882255","#994F00","#FFC20A","#1A85FF","#E66100"),labels=c("ALLEGAN","CLINTON","DECATUR","HILLSDALE","INGHAM","LENAWEE","MONTCALM","MSU AGR","MSU PLP","SAGINAW","SANILAC","ST JOSEPH")) + 
            ylim(-250,400) +
            aes(x=fct_reorder(X,mean_diff_kgha)) +  
            labs(title=title,y=expression(paste("Economic return relative to commercial base (USD/ha)")),x=sprintf("Mean yield response by location (n = %s)",length(data$mean_diff_kgha))) +
            theme_classic2() +
            theme(legend.text=element_text(size=18)) +
            theme(legend.title=element_blank()) +   
            theme(axis.text.y=element_text(color="black",size=14)) +
            theme(axis.text.x=element_blank()) + 
            theme(axis.ticks.x=element_blank()) +
            theme(axis.title.y=element_text(color="black",size=18)) +
            theme(axis.title.x=element_text(color="black",size=18)) +
            theme(plot.title=element_text(color="black",size=18,hjust=0.5,face="bold")) +
            geom_hline(yintercept=37)
            #geom_hline(linetype="dashed",yintercept=mean(data$mean_diff,na.rm=T)+(1.96*se(data$mean_diff))) +
            #geom_hline(linetype="dashed",yintercept=mean(data$mean_diff,na.rm=T)-(1.96*se(data$mean_diff)))
}
```

### Lets Cow! -plot the ILEVO pianos together
```{r}
### if I want to use a single legend for all plots, I'll need to set the gradient_fill arguments in each ggplot call
#calls the legend and sets the themes within get_legend, not plot_grid
leg4<-get_legend(piano_color_leg + #extracts legend from one plot
  guides(color=colorz,label=locationz) + #sets the layout of the legend itself
  theme(legend.direction="vertical",   #how to arrange the legend key
        legend.justification="center", #this tells cowplot to center the legend...
        legend.box.just="right") + #on the right
  theme(legend.key.size=unit(.75,"in"))) 
  theme(legend.margin=margin(c(0,0,0,1.5))) #adds some space to the left on the legend
#sets the title
title<-ggdraw() + 
  draw_label("Figure Title Here",size=32,fontface="bold") #sets up a function-call with the title
#sets the layout grid
cow_piano_ilevo<-plot_grid(
  ilevo_piano_2017 + theme(legend.position="none"), #removes legend from individual plots
  ilevo_piano_2018 + theme(legend.position="none") + theme(axis.title.y=element_blank()),
  ilevo_piano_2019 + theme(legend.position="none"),
  ilevo_piano_2020 + theme(legend.position="none") + theme(axis.title.y=element_blank()),
  ilevo_piano_2021 + theme(legend.position="none"),
  ilevo_piano_2022 + theme(legend.position="none") + theme(axis.title.y=element_blank()),
  ilevo_piano_2023 + theme(legend.position="none"),
  ilevo_piano_2024 + theme(legend.position="none") + theme(axis.title.y=element_blank()),
  ncol=2,nrow=4,align='v',scale=1,labels="AUTO",label_size=24,vjust=2,hjust=-2.75) #arranges all 4 plots together and scales them down a bit to add room between plots
(cow_piano_ilevo<-plot_grid(cow_piano_ilevo,rel_widths=c(1,0.15)))#adds the legend
#cow_combo<-plot_grid(title,cow_combo,ncol=1,rel_heights=c(0.05,1)) #adds the title -- rel_heights values control vertical title margins
ggsave("cow_piano_ilevo.png",path="C:/Longitudinal",plot=cow_piano_ilevo,width=28,height=40,units="in",dpi=600)

#sets the layout grid
cow_ilevo_piano_color<-plot_grid(
  ilevo_piano_color_2017 + theme(legend.position="none"), #removes legend from individual plots
  ilevo_piano_color_2018 + theme(legend.position="none") + theme(axis.title.y=element_blank()),
  ilevo_piano_color_2019 + theme(legend.position="none"),
  ilevo_piano_color_2020 + theme(legend.position="none") + theme(axis.title.y=element_blank()),
  ilevo_piano_color_2021 + theme(legend.position="none"),
  ilevo_piano_color_2022 + theme(legend.position="none") + theme(axis.title.y=element_blank()),
  ilevo_piano_color_2023 + theme(legend.position="none"),
  ilevo_piano_color_2024 + theme(legend.position="none") + theme(axis.title.y=element_blank()),
  ncol=2,nrow=4,align='v',scale=1,labels="AUTO",label_size=24,vjust=2,hjust=-2.75) #arranges all 4 plots together and scales them down a bit to add room between plots
(cow_ilevo_piano_color<-plot_grid(cow_ilevo_piano_color,leg4,rel_widths=c(1,0.2)))#adds the legend
#cow_combo<-plot_grid(title,cow_combo,ncol=1,rel_heights=c(0.05,1)) #adds the title -- rel_heights values control vertical title margins
ggsave("cow_piano_ilevo_color.png",path="C:/Longitudinal",plot=cow_ilevo_piano_color,width=28,height=40,units="in",dpi=600)
```

### cool, but what's our experiment-wide type II error rate on treatment?
```{r include=FALSE}
t.test(df_base$yield_kgha,df_ilevo$yield_kgha,alternative="two.sided",var.equal=T) #students t-test -- classic 
t.test(df_base$yield_kgha,df_saltro$yield_kgha,alternative="two.sided",var.equal=T) #students t-test -- classic
t.test(df_ilevo$yield_kgha,df_saltro$yield_kgha,alternative="two.sided",var.equal=T) #students t-test -- classic 
power.t.test(n=380,sd=15.05,power=0.8,alternative="two.sided") #experiment-wise power test
power.t.test(n=617,sd=13.84,power=0.8,alternative="two.sided") #base/ilevo power test
power.t.test(n=380,sd=15.05,power=0.8,alternative="two.sided") #base/saltro power test
power.t.test(n=380,sd=15.05,power=0.8,alternative="two.sided") #ilevo/saltro power test
```

### Peyton-inspired function that auto-plots the yield plots 
```{r warning=FALSE}
#creates a function that makes ggplots and assigns them to a variable to call in cow plot, etc.    
create_ggplot <- function(data,x_col,y_col,fill,title){
          #creates the ggplot
          (plot <- ggplot(data=subset(data,!is.na(yield_mtha)),aes_string(x=x_col,y=y_col,fill=fill)) + 
            geom_boxplot() +
            ylim(0,7) +
            scale_fill_manual(values=c("#F8766D","#619CFF","#00BA38"),labels=c("Base", "Base +\nILEVO", "Base +\nSaltro")) +  
            labs(title=title,y=expression(paste("MT/ha"))) +
            theme_classic2() +
            #stat_kruskal_test(aes(label=sprintf("P = %5.2f",as.numeric(..p..))),size=5,label.y=0,vjust=-1, p.adjust.method="none",angle=45) +
            #stat_kruskal_test(aes(label=sprintf("p.signif")),size=5,label.y=0,vjust=-1, p.adjust.method="none") +
            stat_compare_means(method="kruskal.test",p.adjust.method="tukey",label="p.signif",size=5,label.y=0,vjust=-1) +
            theme(axis.text.x=element_text(angle=45,vjust=0.75,hjust=0.75,color="black")) +
            theme(legend.text=element_text(size=18)) +
            theme(legend.title=element_blank()) +    
            theme(axis.title.x = element_blank()) +
            theme(axis.text.x = element_text(size=16)) +
            theme(axis.text.y = element_text(size=14)) +
            theme(axis.title.y = element_text(color="black",size=18,face="bold")) +
            theme(plot.title=element_text(color="black",size=18,hjust=0.5,vjust=-2,face="bold")))
}

#2017
ggsave("yield_plot_2017.png",plot=(yield_plot_2017<-create_ggplot(sds2017,"location","yield_mtha","product","2017")),width=8,height=8,units="in",dpi=300)
#2018
ggsave("yield_plot_2018.png",plot=(yield_plot_2018<-create_ggplot(sds2018,"location","yield_mtha","product","2018")),width=8,height=8,units="in",dpi=300)   
#2019
ggsave("yield_plot_2019.png",plot=(yield_plot_2019<-create_ggplot(sds2019,"location","yield_mtha","product","2019")),width=8,height=8,units="in",dpi=300)   
#2020
ggsave("yield_plot_2020.png",plot=(yield_plot_2020<-create_ggplot(sds2020,"location","yield_mtha","product","2020")),width=8,height=8,units="in",dpi=300)   
#2021
ggsave("yield_plot_2021.png",plot=(yield_plot_2021<-create_ggplot(sds2021,"location","yield_mtha","product","2021")),width=8,height=8,units="in",dpi=300)   
#2022
ggsave("yield_plot_2022.png",plot=(yield_plot_2022<-create_ggplot(sds2022,"location","yield_mtha","product","2022")),width=8,height=8,units="in",dpi=300)   
#2023
ggsave("yield_plot_2023.png",plot=(yield_plot_2023<-create_ggplot(sds2023,"location","yield_mtha","product","2023")),width=8,height=8,units="in",dpi=300)
#2024
ggsave("yield_plot_2024.png",plot=(yield_plot_2024<-create_ggplot(sds2024,"location","yield_mtha","product","2024")),width=8,height=8,units="in",dpi=300)
```

###yield plots by location showing all years -- freedom units
```{r}

#creates a function that makes ggplots and assigns them to a variable to call in cow plot, etc.    
create_ggplot <- function(data,x_col,y_col,fill,title){
          #creates the ggplot
          (plot <- ggplot(data=subset(data,!is.na(yield_bua)),aes_string(x=x_col,y=y_col,fill=fill)) + 
            geom_boxplot() +
            #ylim(0,7) +
            scale_fill_manual(values=c("#F8766D","#619CFF","#00BA38"),labels=c("Base", "Base +\nILEVO", "Base +\nSaltro")) +  
            labs(title=title,y=expression(paste("Bushels per Acre (bu/A)"))) +
            theme_classic2() +
            #stat_kruskal_test(aes(label=sprintf("P = %5.2f",as.numeric(..p..))),size=5,label.y=0,vjust=-1, p.adjust.method="none",angle=45) +
            #stat_kruskal_test(aes(label=sprintf("p.signif")),size=5,label.y=0,vjust=-1, p.adjust.method="none") +
            stat_compare_means(method="kruskal.test",label="p.signif",size=5,label.y=0,vjust=-1) +
            theme(axis.text.x=element_text(angle=45,vjust=0.75,hjust=0.75,color="black")) +
            theme(legend.text=element_text(size=18)) +
            theme(legend.title=element_blank()) +    
            theme(axis.title.x = element_blank()) +
            theme(axis.text.x = element_text(size=16)) +
            theme(axis.text.y = element_text(size=14)) +
            theme(axis.title.y = element_text(color="black",size=18,face="bold")) +
            theme(plot.title=element_text(color="black",size=18,hjust=0.5,vjust=-2,face="bold")))
}

#STJ_only
ggsave("yield_plot_STJ.png",plot=(yield_plot_STJ<-create_ggplot(df_stj,"year","yield_bua","product","St. Joseph County, MI Soybean Trial Location")),width=8,height=8,units="in",dpi=300)

#DCF_only
ggsave("yield_plot_DCF.png",plot=(yield_plot_DCF<-create_ggplot(df_dcf,"year","yield_bua","product","Decatur, MI Soybean Trial Location")),width=8,height=8,units="in",dpi=300)

```


### Yeah... cowplot is too recursive to not have its own chunk
```{r}
### THIS looks like a job for cowplot!
#calls the legend and sets the themes within get_legend, not plot_grid
leg2<-get_legend(yield_plot_2023 + #extracts legend from one plot
  guides(color=guide_legend(nrow=3)) + #sets the layout of the legend itself
  theme(legend.direction="vertical",   #how to arrange the legend key
        legend.justification="center", #this tells cowplot to center the legend...
        legend.box.just="right") + #on the right
  theme(legend.key.size=unit(1,"in"))) 
  theme(legend.margin=margin(c(0,0,0,1.5))) #adds some space to the left on the legend
#sets the title
title<-ggdraw() + 
  draw_label("Figure Title Here",size=32,fontface="bold") #sets up a function-call with the title
#sets the layout grid
cow_yield<-plot_grid(
  yield_plot_2017 + theme(legend.position="none"), #removes legend from individual plots
  yield_plot_2018 + theme(legend.position="none") + theme(axis.title.y=element_blank()),
  yield_plot_2019 + theme(legend.position="none"),
  yield_plot_2020 + theme(legend.position="none") + theme(axis.title.y=element_blank()),
  yield_plot_2021 + theme(legend.position="none"),
  yield_plot_2022 + theme(legend.position="none") + theme(axis.title.y=element_blank()),
  yield_plot_2023 + theme(legend.position="none"),
  yield_plot_2024 + theme(legend.position="none") + theme(axis.title.y=element_blank()),
  ncol=2,nrow=4,align='v',scale=1,labels="AUTO",label_size=24,vjust=2,hjust=-2.75) #arranges all 4 plots together and scales them down a bit to add room between plots
(cow_yield<-plot_grid(cow_yield,leg2,rel_widths=c(1,0.15)))#adds the legend
#cow_combo<-plot_grid(title,cow_combo,ncol=1,rel_heights=c(0.05,1)) #adds the title -- rel_heights values control vertical title margins
ggsave("cow_yield.png",path="C:/Longitudinal",plot=cow_yield,width=20,height=24,units="in",dpi=300)
```

### Kassambara-inspired faceting
```{r warning=FALSE}
# Removed 1 redundant package-loading/install line(s); packages are loaded in the setup section.
#creates a function that makes ggplots and assigns them to a variable to call in cow plot, etc.    
create_ggplot <- function(data,x_col,y_col,fill,title){
          #creates the ggplot
          (plot <- ggplot(data,aes_string(x=x_col,y=y_col,fill=fill)) + 
            geom_boxplot() +
            ylim(0,7) +
            scale_fill_manual(values=c("#F8766D","#619CFF","#00BA38"),labels=c("Base", "Base +\nILEVO", "Base +\nSaltro")) + 
            labs(title=title,y=expression(paste("MT/hectare"))) +
            theme_classic2() +
            facet_wrap(~year,ncol=2) +
            #stat_kruskal_test(aes(label=sprintf("P = %5.2f",as.numeric(..p..))),size=5,label.y=0,vjust=-1, p.adjust.method="none",angle=45) +
            #stat_kruskal_test(aes(label=sprintf("p.signif")),size=5,label.y=0,vjust=-1, p.adjust.method="none") +
            stat_compare_means(method="anova",aes(group = product),label="p.signif",size=4,label.y=0,vjust=-1) +
            theme(axis.text.x=element_text(angle=45,vjust=0.75,hjust=0.75,color="black")) +
            theme(legend.text=element_text(size=12)) +
            theme(legend.title=element_blank()) +    
            theme(axis.title.x = element_blank()) +
            theme(axis.text.x = element_text(size=12)) +
            theme(axis.text.y = element_text(size=12)) +
            theme(axis.title.y = element_text(color="black",size=18,face="bold")) +
            theme(plot.title=element_text(color="black",size=18,hjust=0.5,vjust=-3,face="bold")))
}

#Base vs. ILEVO by year
ggsave("just_ilevo.png",plot=(just_ilevo<-create_ggplot(df_balevo,"location","yield_mtha","product","")),width=12,height=12,units="in",dpi=300)

#all trts by year
ggsave("facet_yield.png",plot=(facet_yield<-create_ggplot(df,"location","yield_mtha","product","")),width=12,height=12,units="in",dpi=300)
```

### FDX Piano plots of ILEVO and Saltro
#### this needs a mutated column called mean_fdx_diff to run
```{r eval=FALSE, message=TRUE, warning=FALSE, include=FALSE}

  piano<-ggplot(data=subset(ilevo_plot,!is.na(mean_fdx_diff)), aes(x=X,y=mean_fdx_diff)) + 
   geom_bar(aes(fill=mean_fdx_diff),position="dodge",stat="identity",color="darkgray",fill="lightgray") +
    aes(x=fct_reorder(X,mean_fdx_diff)) +
    #ylim(-2000,2200) +
    labs(title=element_text("Differential SDS foliar disease response to ILEVO: 2017-2024"),y=expression(paste("Relative foliar disease index (FDX) difference")),x=sprintf("Mean yield response by location and replicate (n = %s)",length(ilevo_plot$mean_fdx_diff))) +
    theme_classic2() +
    theme(axis.text.y=element_text(color="black",size=14)) +
    theme(axis.text.x=element_blank()) + 
    theme(axis.ticks.x=element_blank()) +
    theme(axis.title.y=element_text(color="black",size=18)) +
    theme(axis.title.x=element_text(color="black",size=18)) +
    theme(plot.title=element_text(color="black",size=18,hjust=0.5,,vjust=-4,face="bold")) +
    geom_hline(yintercept=mean(ilevo_plot$mean_fdx_diff,na.rm=T)) + #numeric difference between treatment means (basically 0)
    geom_hline(linetype="dashed",yintercept=mean(ilevo_plot$mean_fdx_diff,na.rm=T)+(1.96*se(ilevo_plot$mean_fdx_diff))) + #line at the upper 95% CI limit of the mean yield
    geom_hline(linetype="dashed",yintercept=mean(ilevo_plot$mean_fdx_diff,na.rm=T)-(1.96*se(ilevo_plot$mean_fdx_diff))) #line at the lower 95% CI limit of the mean yield
  piano
  piano_leg<-piano
  piano_noleg<-piano +
    theme(legend.position="none")

ggsave("fdx_piano_leg.png",path="C:/Longitudinal",plot=piano_leg,width=12,height=8,units="in",dpi=320)
ggsave("fdx_piano_noleg.png",path="C:/Longitudinal",plot=piano_noleg,width=12,height=8,units="in",dpi=320)

 piano2<-ggplot(data=subset(saltro_plot,!is.na(mean_fdx_diff)), aes(x=X,y=mean_fdx_diff)) + 
    geom_bar(aes(fill=mean_fdx_diff),position="dodge",stat="identity",color="darkgray",fill="lightgray") +
    aes(x=fct_reorder(X,mean_fdx_diff)) +
    #ylim(-2000,2200) +
    labs(title=element_text("Differential SDS foliar disease response to Saltro: 2020-2024"),y=expression(paste("Relative foliar disease index (FDX) difference")),x=sprintf("Mean yield response by location and replicate (n = %s)",length(saltro_plot$mean_fdx_diff))) +
    theme_classic2() +
    theme(axis.text.y=element_text(color="black",size=14)) +
    theme(axis.text.x=element_blank()) + 
    theme(axis.ticks.x=element_blank()) +
    theme(axis.title.y=element_text(color="black",size=18)) +
    theme(axis.title.x=element_text(color="black",size=18)) +
    theme(plot.title=element_text(color="black",size=18,hjust=0.5,,vjust=-4,face="bold")) +
    geom_hline(yintercept=mean(saltro_plot$mean_fdx_diff,na.rm=T)) + #numeric difference between treatment means (basically 0)
    geom_hline(linetype="dashed",yintercept=mean(saltro_plot$mean_fdx_diff,na.rm=T)+(1.96*se(saltro_plot$mean_fdx_diff))) + #line at the upper 95% CI limit of the mean yield
    geom_hline(linetype="dashed",yintercept=mean(saltro_plot$mean_fdx_diff,na.rm=T)-(1.96*se(saltro_plot$mean_fdx_diff))) #line at the lower 95% CI limit of the mean yield
  piano2
  piano_leg2<-piano2
  piano_noleg2<-piano2 +
    theme(legend.position="none")
  
ggsave("fdx_piano_leg2.png",path="C:/Longitudinal",plot=piano_leg2,width=12,height=8,units="in",dpi=320)
ggsave("fdx_piano_noleg2.png",path="C:/Longitudinal",plot=piano_noleg2,width=12,height=8,units="in",dpi=320)
```

```{r eval=FALSE, message=TRUE, warning=FALSE, include=FALSE}
    geom_hline(linetype="dashed",yintercept=mean(ilevo_plot$mean_fdx_diff,na.rm=T)+(1.96*se(ilevo_plot$mean_fdx_diff))) + #line at the upper 95% CI limit of the mean yield
```

### function/loop that auto-plots piano plots of FDX in each year
```{r eval=FALSE, include=FALSE}
#creates a function that makes ggplots and assigns them to a variable to call in cow plot, etc.    
create_ggplot <- function(data,x_col,y_col,fill,title){
          #creates the ggplot
          (plot <- ggplot(data=subset(data,!is.na(mean_fdx_diff)), aes_string(x=x_col,y=y_col,fill=fill))) + 
            geom_bar(aes(fill=mean_fdx_diff),position="dodge",stat="identity",color="darkgreen",fill="lightgreen") +
            #ylim(-2000,2200) +
            aes(x=fct_reorder(X,mean_fdx_diff)) +  
            labs(title=title,y=expression(paste("Relative SDS foliar disease index (FDX) difference")),x=sprintf("Mean SDS foliar symptom response by location and replicate (n = %s)",length(data$mean_fdx_diff))) +
            theme_classic2() +
            theme(axis.text.y=element_text(color="black",size=14)) +
            theme(axis.text.x=element_blank()) + 
            theme(axis.ticks.x=element_blank()) +
            theme(axis.title.y=element_text(color="black",size=18)) +
            theme(axis.title.x=element_text(color="black",size=18)) +
            theme(plot.title=element_text(color="black",size=18,hjust=0.5,face="bold")) +
            geom_hline(yintercept=mean(data$mean_fdx_diff,na.rm=T)) +
            geom_hline(linetype="dashed",yintercept=mean(data$mean_fdx_diff,na.rm=T)+(1.96*se(data$mean_fdx_diff))) +
            geom_hline(linetype="dashed",yintercept=mean(data$mean_fdx_diff,na.rm=T)-(1.96*se(data$mean_fdx_diff)))
}
```

### function/loop that auto-plots FDX piano plots of each year with color-fill by location
```{r eval=FALSE, include=FALSE}

#creates a function that makes ggplots and assigns them to a variable to call in cow plot, etc.    
create_ggplot <- function(data,x_col,y_col,fill,title){
          #creates the ggplot
          plot <- ggplot(data=subset(data,!is.na(mean_fdx_diff)), aes_string(x=x_col,y=y_col,fill=as.factor(data$color),drop=FALSE)) + 
            geom_bar(aes(fill=location),position="dodge",stat="identity",color="darkgray") +
            scale_fill_manual(values=colorz,labels=locationz,drop=F) + 
            ylim(-25,25) +
            aes(x=fct_reorder(X,mean_fdx_diff)) +  
            labs(title=title,y=expression(paste("Relative SDS foliar disease index (FDX) difference")),x=sprintf("Mean SDS foliar symptom response by location and replicate (n = %s)",length(data$mean_fdx_diff))) +
            theme_classic2() +
            theme(legend.text=element_text(size=18)) +
            theme(legend.title=element_blank()) +   
            theme(axis.text.y=element_text(color="black",size=14)) +
            theme(axis.text.x=element_blank()) + 
            theme(axis.ticks.x=element_blank()) +
            theme(axis.title.y=element_text(color="black",size=18)) +
            theme(axis.title.x=element_text(color="black",size=18)) +
            theme(plot.title=element_text(color="black",size=18,hjust=0.5,face="bold")) +
            geom_hline(yintercept=mean(data$mean_fdx_diff,na.rm=T)) +
            geom_hline(linetype="dashed",yintercept=mean(data$mean_fdx_diff,na.rm=T)+(1.96*se(data$mean_fdx_diff))) +
            geom_hline(linetype="dashed",yintercept=mean(data$mean_fdx_diff,na.rm=T)-(1.96*se(data$mean_fdx_diff))) +
            theme(plot.title=element_text(color="black",size=18,hjust=0.5,vjust=-3,face="bold")) +
            geom_hline(yintercept=mean(data$mean_fdx,na.rm=T),color="red")
}

###ILEVO pianos

#2018
```

### Lets Cow! -plot the ILEVO FDX pianos together
```{r eval=FALSE, include=FALSE}
title<-ggdraw() + 
  draw_label("Figure Title Here",size=32,fontface="bold") #sets up a function-call with the title
#sets the layout grid
cow_piano_ilevo_fdx<-plot_grid(
  ilevo_fdx_piano_2017 + theme(legend.position="none"), #removes legend from individual plots
  ilevo_fdx_piano_2018 + theme(legend.position="none") + theme(axis.title.y=element_blank()),
  ilevo_fdx_piano_2019 + theme(legend.position="none"),
  ilevo_fdx_piano_2020 + theme(legend.position="none") + theme(axis.title.y=element_blank()),
  ilevo_fdx_piano_2021 + theme(legend.position="none"),
  ilevo_fdx_piano_2022 + theme(legend.position="none") + theme(axis.title.y=element_blank()),
  ilevo_fdx_piano_2023 + theme(legend.position="none"),
  ilevo_fdx_piano_2024 + theme(legend.position="none") + theme(axis.title.y=element_blank()),
  ncol=2,nrow=4,align='v',scale=1,labels="AUTO",label_size=24,vjust=2,hjust=-1.75) #arranges all 4 plots together and scales them down a bit to add room between plots
(cow_piano_ilevo_fdx<-plot_grid(cow_piano_ilevo_fdx,rel_widths=c(1,0.15)))#adds the legend
#cow_combo<-plot_grid(title,cow_combo,ncol=1,rel_heights=c(0.05,1)) #adds the title -- rel_heights values control vertical title margins
ggsave("cow_piano_ilevo_fdx.png",path="C:/Longitudinal",plot=cow_piano_ilevo_fdx,width=28,height=40,units="in",dpi=320)

#sets the layout grid
cow_piano_ilevo_fdx_color<-plot_grid(
  ##### SAMPLE SIZES ARE NO LONGER CORRECT; CAN ADD THEM MANUALLY BELOW :( #####
  ilevo_fdx_piano_color_2017 + theme(legend.position="none"), #removes legend from individual plots
  ilevo_fdx_piano_color_2018 + theme(legend.position="none") + theme(axis.title.y=element_blank()),
  ilevo_fdx_piano_color_2019 + theme(legend.position="none"),
  ilevo_fdx_piano_color_2020 + theme(legend.position="none") + theme(axis.title.y=element_blank()),
  ilevo_fdx_piano_color_2021 + theme(legend.position="none"),
  ilevo_fdx_piano_color_2022 + theme(legend.position="none") + theme(axis.title.y=element_blank()),
  ilevo_fdx_piano_color_2023 + theme(legend.position="none"),
  ilevo_fdx_piano_color_2024 + theme(legend.position="none") + theme(axis.title.y=element_blank()),
  ncol=2,nrow=4,align='v',scale=1,labels="AUTO",label_size=24,vjust=2,hjust=-1.75) #arranges all 4 plots together and scales them down a bit to add room between plots
(cow_piano_ilevo_fdx_color<-plot_grid(cow_piano_ilevo_fdx_color,leg3,rel_widths=c(1,0.2)))#adds the legend
#cow_combo<-plot_grid(title,cow_combo,ncol=1,rel_heights=c(0.05,1)) #adds the title -- rel_heights values control vertical title margins
ggsave("cow_piano_ilevo_fdx_color.png",path="C:/Longitudinal",plot=cow_piano_ilevo_fdx_color,width=28,height=40,units="in",dpi=320)
```

#### fixed!!! ####
###MAPS###
```{r eval=FALSE, include=FALSE}
# Removed 3 redundant package-loading/install line(s); packages are loaded in the setup section.

# ---- Packages ----
# install.packages(c("ggplot2","dplyr","readr","maps"))  # uncomment if needed
#library(ggplot2)
#library(dplyr)
#detach("package:purrr", unload = TRUE) #prevents masking of 'map' object 

#df_map<-ddply(df, .(year,location), summarize,mean_yield=mean(as.numeric(yield_kgha),na.rm=T),se_yield=sd((as.numeric(yield_kgha))/sqrt(length(yield_kgha)),na.rm=T),mean_diff=mean(yield_diff,na.rm=T),mean_fdx=mean(as.numeric(fdx),na.rm=T),mean_fdx_diff=mean(as.numeric(fdx_diff),na.rm=T),mean_scn=mean(as.numeric(scn_count_rep),na.rm=T),mean_fv=mean(as.numeric(mean_Fv_ratio),na.rm=T))
    #df_map<-arrange(trt_yield_sum,mean_diff)
    #write.csv(df_map,"C:/Longitudinal/df_map.csv")

###exported df to append with GPS coord for mapping###

df_map <- read.csv("df_map2.csv",header = T)


# Download and unzip the cartographic boundary shapefile (20m resolution is usually fine)
url <- "https://www2.census.gov/geo/tiger/GENZ2022/shp/cb_2022_us_county_20m.zip"
download.file(url, "cb_2022_us_county_20m.zip")
unzip("cb_2022_us_county_20m.zip", exdir = "counties")

counties <- st_read("counties/cb_2022_us_county_20m.shp", quiet = TRUE)

target_names <- c("Allegan", "Clinton", "Hillsdale", "Ingham", "Lenawee", "Montcalm", "Saginaw", "Sanilac", "St. Joseph", "Van Buren")

centroids_ll <- counties %>%
  filter(STATEFP == "26", NAME %in% target_names) %>%
  st_transform(3078) %>%
  st_centroid() %>%
  st_transform(4326)

coords <- st_coordinates(centroids_ll)

df_centroids <- centroids_ll %>%
  st_drop_geometry() %>%
  mutate(
    Latitude  = round(coords[, 2], 2),
    Longitude = round(coords[, 1], 2)
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(County = NAME, Latitude, Longitude) %>%
  arrange(County)

df_centroids
write_csv(df_centroids, "county_land_centroids_MI.csv") #provides the geographic centroids of the counties WITHOUT the great lakes

# Ensure consistent casing for matching
df_centroids <- df_centroids %>% dplyr::rename(county = County, lat = Latitude, lon = Longitude)
df_centroids <- df_centroids %>% mutate(county = toupper(county)) #makes all caps
df_centroids$county <- gsub("^ST\\.\\s*JOSEPH$", "STJOSEPH", toupper(df_centroids$county)) #removes space and period to match df_map
df_centroids$county <- gsub("\\s+", "", toupper(df_centroids$county)) #removes space to match df_map
df_centroids <- dplyr::bind_rows(df_centroids, data.frame(county = "MSU CAMPUS", lat = 42.70, lon = -84.49)) #adds campus
df_centroids <- dplyr::arrange(df_centroids, county) #sorts by county name


# Update df_map with new coordinates by join
df_map <- df_map %>%
  left_join(df_centroids %>%
              dplyr::select(county, lat, lon),
            by = "county") %>%  mutate(lat = coalesce(lat.y, lat.x),
            lon = coalesce(lon.y, lon.x)) %>%
  dplyr::select(-lat.x, -lon.x, -lat.y, -lon.y, -county)

df_map

# ---- Michigan basemap (simple) ----
us_states <- map_data("state")
mi <- us_states |> dplyr::filter(region == "michigan")

# Example data with site names
#sites <- data.frame(
  #lon = c(-86.03, -84.49, -85.18),   # replace with your longitudes
  #lat = c(42.12, 42.70, 43.35),      # replace with your latitudes 
  #site = c("Decatur", "East Lansing", "Montcalm")  # site names 
#)

# ---- Choose 4 simple symbols (pch values) ----
# 21 = circle, 22 = square, 24 = triangle, 25 = diamond (filled shapes with border)
#shape_map <- c("Small plot" = 24, "On-farm 2023" = 22)

site_names <- c("Allegan", "Clinton", "Hillsdale", "Ingham", "Lenawee", "Montcalm", "MSU Campus", "Saginaw", "Sanilac", "St. Joseph", "Van Buren")

# 1) Build a normalized key; count unique years per location
df_map2 <- df_map %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    loc_key = toupper(trimws(.data[["loc_col"]]))
  )

df_map2 <- df_map2 %>%
  mutate(
    loc_key = case_when(
      loc_key %in% c("E_LANSING_PLP", "E_LANSING_AGR") ~ "MSU CAMPUS",
      TRUE ~ loc_key
    )
  )

# First, count appearances of each location
df_counts <- df_map2 %>% #this produces a count-table for # of years at each county/campus
  dplyr::mutate(location = trimws(loc_key)) %>%                 # tidy names just in case
  dplyr::group_by(loc_key) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(years_tested = dplyr::n_distinct(year), lat=lat, lon=lon, .groups = "drop") %>%
  dplyr::arrange(dplyr::desc(years_tested))

df_counts

# ---- Plot ----

# nice breaks for the size legend (1..max years observed)
size_breaks <- sort(unique(df_counts$years_tested))

# your map bounds (keep in sync with coord_quickmap)
lon_range <- c(-90.2, -82.5)
lat_range <- c(41.9, 47.3)

lon_breaks <- seq(ceiling(lon_range[1]), floor(lon_range[2]), by = 1)  # -90:-83
lat_breaks <- seq(ceiling(lat_range[1]), floor(lat_range[2]), by = 1)  # 42:47

p <- ggplot() +
  geom_polygon(
    data = mi, aes(x = long, y = lat, group = group),
    fill = "lightgreen", color = "grey40", linewidth = 0.3
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  geom_point(data = df_counts,aes(x = lon, y = lat, size = years_tested), shape = 21, fill = "grey", color = "grey20",
    alpha = 0.15, stroke = 0.4) +
  scale_size_continuous(range = c(2, 7),
  breaks = size_breaks, guide = guide_legend(title = "Years\nconducted\nat each\nlocation", override.aes = list(alpha = 1))) +
  theme(legend.title.align=0.5) +
  coord_quickmap(xlim = c(-90.2, -82.5), ylim = c(41.9, 47.3)) + # MI bounding box (tweak if needed)
  labs(title = "SDHI seed treatment trial locations (2017-2024)",
    x = "Longitude (°W)", y = "Latitude (°N)", shape = "Year") +
  theme(plot.title.position="plot") +
  theme(legend.title = element_text(size = 10), legend.text = element_text(size = 8)) +
  theme(legend.position = c(1.15, 0.5)) +
  theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    strip.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
    strip.text = element_text()) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = lon_breaks, labels = function(x) paste0(abs(x), "°")) +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = lat_breaks, labels = function(y) paste0(y, "°")
  )

p

# ---- (Optional) Single-panel version with color by year ----
# ggplot() +
#   geom_polygon(data = mi, aes(long, lat, group = group),
#                fill = "grey95", color = "grey60", linewidth = 0.3) +
#   geom_point(data = df, aes(lon, lat, shape = group, color = factor(year)),
#              size = 3.5, stroke = 1) +
#   scale_shape_manual(values = shape_map, name = "Symbol group") +
#   coord_quickmap(xlim = c(-90.5, -82.0), ylim = c(41.6, 48.5)) +
#   labs(title = "Michigan locations (all years)",
#        x = "Longitude (°)", y = "Latitude (°)", color = "Year") +
#   theme_minimal(base_size = 12)

# ---- Save (optional) ----
ggsave("longitudinal_locations.png", p, width = 7, height = 4, dpi = 600)


```

###economics analyses###
```{r eval=FALSE, include=FALSE}
#optimized yield model
price_mod_op<-lmer(diff_price_kgha~product*sds_tol+(1|year:location:soil_pH:mean_Fv_ratio:scn_count_rep:rep:sds_tol),data=high_df,na.action=na.omit)
#summary(y_mod_op) 
joint_tests(price_mod_op) #optimized for modeling seed trt product effects
drop1(price_mod_op) #looks good
plot(price_mod_op)
lsmeans<-emmeans(price_mod_op,~ sds_tol|product) #calculates lsmeans -- OK, this is actually informative
cld(lsmeans,Letters=letters,alpha=.05) #default is Tukey
pairs(lsmeans) #all pairwise comparisons
weights(lsmeans)
```



# Source module: rdx_mini

<!-- Imported and lightly cleaned from `rdx_mini.Rmd`. -->

# 1. Data import and initial cleaning

> 🔧 **Assumption:** Your master dataset is saved as  
> `C:/Longitudinal/full2017_2024.csv`  
> with one row per plot (year × location × treatment × replicate).

Adjust the path if needed.

```{r import, include=FALSE}
raw <- readr::read_csv("C:/Longitudinal/rdx.csv") %>%
  janitor::clean_names()

# Quick skim
#dplyr::glimpse(raw)
```

---

# 2. Derive SCN level and set up factors

We categorize **SCN egg + J2 counts** into three levels:

- `none`: SCN count = 0  
- `low`: 0 < SCN < 2000  
- `high`: SCN ≥ 2000  

We also coerce key design variables into factors for plotting and downstream modelling.

```{r factors, warning=FALSE, include=FALSE}
raw <- raw %>%
  mutate(
    # SCN level factor
    scn_level = case_when(
      scn_count_rep == 0 ~ "none",
      scn_count_rep > 0 & scn_count_rep < 2000 ~ "low",
      scn_count_rep >= 2000 ~ "high",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ),
    scn_level = factor(scn_level, levels = c("none", "low", "high"))
  )

df<- raw %>%
  mutate(
    year              = factor(year),
    location          = factor(location),
    mean_fv_ratio_int = factor(mean_fv_ratio_int, ordered = TRUE),
    sds_tol           = factor(sds_tol),
    product           = factor(product),
    rep               = factor(rep),
    fdx_level         = factor(fdx_level),
    volatility        = factor(volatility), 
    harv_price_us_dkg     = factor(harv_price_us_dkg),
    trt_reponse_us_dha   = as.numeric(trt_reponse_us_dha), #response var that includes market price at harv
    fdx_std           = (scale(fdx)) #rescaled fdx values based on mean = 0, sd = 1
  )

dplyr::glimpse(df)
```

---

```{r echo=FALSE}
#optimized RDX model
#df_nona<-drop_na(df)
#rdx_mod<-lmer(rdx~sds_tol+year+(1|year:scn_count_rep:rep:mean_fv_ratio),data=df,na.action=na.omit)
#summary(rdx_mod)
#joint_tests(rdx_mod) #optimized for modeling seed trt product effects
#drop1(rdx_mod) #looks good
#plot(return_mod)
#lsmeans<-emmeans(rdx_mod,~ product) #calculates lsmeans -- OK, this is actually informative
#multcomp::cld(lsmeans,Letters=letters,alpha=.05,ordered=T) #default is Tukey
#pairs(lsmeans) #all pairwise comparisons
#weights(lsmeans)

se <- function(x, na.rm = TRUE) {
  x <- as.numeric(x)
  if (na.rm) x <- x[!is.na(x)]
  sd(x) / sqrt(length(x))
} #sets up a function to calculate SE from sd, corrected for sample size

df_summary <- df %>%
  group_by(year,location,sds_tol) %>%
  summarise(
    across(
      c(rdx, fdx, yield_kgha),
      list(mean = ~mean(.x, na.rm = TRUE),
           se   = ~se(.x, na.rm = TRUE)),
      .names = "{.col}_{.fn}"
    ),
    n = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

safe_dunn <- purrr::possibly(
  ~ rstatix::dunn_test(.x, rdx ~ sds_tol, p.adjust.method = "BH"),
  otherwise = tibble()   # return empty tibble on failure
)

df_dunn <- df %>%
  group_by(year, location) %>%
  nest() %>%
  mutate(
    res = map(data, ~ {
      dat <- .x %>% filter(!is.na(sds_tol), !is.na(rdx))
      if (dplyr::n_distinct(dat$sds_tol) < 2) return(tibble())
      safe_dunn(dat)
    })
  ) %>%
  select(-data) %>%
  unnest(res) %>%
  ungroup()
```

---

### pH figure?
```{r}
# Removed 2 redundant package-loading/install line(s); packages are loaded in the setup section.

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Expected columns:
# soil_ph
# RDX
# FDX
# yield_kgha
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------

df_plot <- df %>%
  mutate(
    soil_p_h    = as.numeric(soil_p_h),
    rdx        = as.numeric(rdx),
    fdx        = as.numeric(fdx),
    yield_kgha = as.numeric(yield_kgha)
  )

theme_ph <- theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major = element_line(linewidth = 0.2),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 11),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.text = element_text(color = "black"),
    legend.position = "none"
  )

# A. Soil pH vs rdx
p_rdx <- df_plot %>%
  filter(!is.na(soil_p_h), !is.na(rdx)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = soil_p_h, y = rdx)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.7, size = 2) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, linewidth = 0.8) +
  labs(
    title = "A. Root disease index",
    x = "Soil pH",
    y = "rdx"
  ) +
  theme_ph

# B. Soil pH vs fdx
p_fdx <- df_plot %>%
  filter(!is.na(soil_p_h), !is.na(fdx)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = soil_p_h, y = fdx)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.7, size = 2) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, linewidth = 0.8) +
  labs(
    title = "B. Foliar disease index",
    x = "Soil pH",
    y = "fdx"
  ) +
  theme_ph

# C. Soil pH vs yield
p_yield <- df_plot %>%
  filter(!is.na(soil_p_h), !is.na(yield_kgha)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = soil_p_h, y = yield_kgha)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.7, size = 2) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, linewidth = 0.8) +
  labs(
    title = "C. Soybean yield",
    x = "Soil pH",
    y = expression("Yield (kg " * ha^{-1} * ")")
  ) +
  theme_ph

fig_ph <- (p_rdx | p_fdx | p_yield) +
  plot_annotation(
    title = "Observed relationships between soil pH, disease indices, and soybean yield"
  )

fig_ph

ggsave(
  "soil_p_h_observed_scale_panels.png",
  plot = fig_ph,
  width = 13,
  height = 4.5,
  dpi = 600
)
```



# Source module: table_summaries_dplyr

<!-- Imported and lightly cleaned from `table_summaries_dplyr.Rmd`. -->

# 1. Data import and initial cleaning

> 🔧 **Assumption:** Your master dataset is saved as  
> `C:/Longitudinal/full2017_2024.csv`  
> with one row per plot (year × location × treatment × replicate).

Adjust the path if needed.

```{r import, include=FALSE}
raw <- readr::read_csv("C:/Longitudinal/full2017_2024.csv") %>%
  janitor::clean_names()

# Quick skim
#dplyr::glimpse(raw)
```

---

# 2. Derive SCN level and set up factors

We categorize **SCN egg + J2 counts** into three levels:

- `none`: SCN count = 0  
- `low`: 0 < SCN < 2000  
- `high`: SCN ≥ 2000  

We also coerce key design variables into factors for plotting and downstream modelling.

```{r factors, warning=FALSE, include=FALSE}
raw <- raw %>%
  mutate(
    # SCN level factor
    scn_level = case_when(
      scn_count_rep == 0 ~ "none",
      scn_count_rep > 0 & scn_count_rep < 2000 ~ "low",
      scn_count_rep >= 2000 ~ "high",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ),
    scn_level = factor(scn_level, levels = c("none", "low", "high"))
  )

df<- raw %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    year              = factor(year),
    location          = factor(location),
    mean_fv_ratio_int = factor(mean_fv_ratio_int, ordered = TRUE),
    sds_tol           = factor(sds_tol),
    product           = factor(product),
    rep               = factor(rep),
    fdx_level         = factor(fdx_level),
    volatility        = factor(volatility), 
    fdx_std           = (scale(fdx)) #rescaled fdx values based on mean = 0, sd = 1
  )

dplyr::glimpse(df)
```

---

```{r echo=FALSE}
#optimized RDX model
#df_nona<-drop_na(df)
#rdx_mod<-lmer(rdx~sds_tol+year+(1|year:scn_count_rep:rep:mean_fv_ratio),data=df,na.action=na.omit)
#summary(rdx_mod)
#joint_tests(rdx_mod) #optimized for modeling seed trt product effects
#drop1(rdx_mod) #looks good
#plot(return_mod)
#lsmeans<-emmeans(rdx_mod,~ product) #calculates lsmeans -- OK, this is actually informative
#multcomp::cld(lsmeans,Letters=letters,alpha=.05,ordered=T) #default is Tukey
#pairs(lsmeans) #all pairwise comparisons
#weights(lsmeans)

se <- function(x, na.rm = TRUE) {
  x <- as.numeric(x)
  if (na.rm) x <- x[!is.na(x)]
  sd(x) / sqrt(length(x))
} #sets up a function to calculate SE from sd, corrected for sample size

df_summary <- df %>%
  group_by(year,location,sds_tol,product) %>%
  summarise(
    across(
      c(fdx, yield_kgha),
      list(mean = ~mean(.x, na.rm = TRUE),
           se   = ~se(.x, na.rm = TRUE)),
      .names = "{.col}_{.fn}"
    ),
    n = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

#(df %>% group_by(year,location) %>% 
    #dunn_test(yield_kgha~sds_tol,p.adjust.method = "BH"))

safe_dunn <- purrr::possibly(
  ~ rstatix::dunn_test(.x, yield_kgha ~ sds_tol, p.adjust.method = "BH"),
  otherwise = tibble()
)

df_dunn <- df %>%
  group_by(mean_fv_ratio_int) %>%
  nest() %>%
  mutate(
    res = map(data, ~{
      dat <- .x %>% filter(!is.na(sds_tol), !is.na(yield_kgha), !is.na(mean_fv_ratio))
      if (n_distinct(dat$product) < 2) return(tibble())  # key change
      safe_dunn(dat)
    })
  ) %>%
  select(-data) %>%
  unnest(res) %>%
  ungroup()
```

---

### summarizes Fv abundance by site year
```{r}
df_fv <- df %>%
  group_by(year,location) %>%
  summarise(mean_fv = mean(mean_fv_ratio,na.rm = TRUE))
    #n = n(),
    #.groups = "drop"
  #)
df_fv <- drop_na(df_fv)
```
---

### summarizes seed trt response over all years
```{r}
df_trt <- df %>%
  group_by(product) %>%
  summarise(mean_kg = mean(yield_diff,na.rm = TRUE),mean_return_ha = mean(diff_price_kgha,na.rm = TRUE),mean_bu = mean(yield_diff_bua,na.rm = TRUE),mean_return_a = mean(diff_price_bua,na.rm = TRUE))
    #n = n(),
    #.groups = "drop"
  #)
df_trt <- drop_na(df_trt)

df_trt <- df %>%
  group_by(product) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_kg        = mean(yield_diff, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_return_ha = mean(diff_price_kgha, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_bu        = mean(yield_diff_bua, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_return_a  = mean(diff_price_bua, na.rm = TRUE),
    n              = sum(!is.na(yield_diff)),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

```



# Source module: figs & tables for MI only LMM results

<!-- Imported and lightly cleaned from `figs & tables for MI only LMM results.Rmd`. -->

# 1. Data import and initial cleaning

> 🔧 **Assumption:** Your master dataset is saved as  
> `C:/Longitudinal/full2017_2024.csv`  
> with one row per plot (year × location × treatment × replicate).

Adjust the path if needed.

```{r import, include=FALSE}
raw <- readr::read_csv("C:/Longitudinal/full2017_2024.csv") %>%
  janitor::clean_names()

# Quick skim
#dplyr::glimpse(raw)
```

---

# 2. Derive SCN level and set up factors

We categorize **SCN egg + J2 counts** into three levels:

- `none`: SCN count = 0  
- `low`: 0 < SCN < 2000  
- `high`: SCN ≥ 2000  

We also coerce key design variables into factors for plotting and downstream modelling.

### reads in and parses data and plots soil pH by FDX and yield LMM output###
```{r}

#-----------------------------
# Data prep
#-----------------------------
df <- raw %>%
  mutate(
    state    = as.factor(state),
    location = as.factor(location),
    year     = as.factor(year),
    rep      = as.factor(rep),
    cultivar = as.factor(cultivar),
    product  = as.factor(product),
    sds_tol  = as.factor(sds_tol),

    siteyear = factor(paste(location, year, sep = "_")),

    mean_fv_ratio  = as.numeric(mean_fv_ratio),
    scn_count_rep  = as.numeric(scn_count_rep),
    soil_p_h       = as.numeric(soil_p_h),
    fdx            = as.numeric(fdx),
    yield_kgha     = as.numeric(yield_kgha),
    proj_price_kg  = as.numeric(proj_price_kg),
    harv_price_kg  = as.numeric(harv_price_kg),
    volatility     = as.numeric(volatility),

    log10_fv  = log10(mean_fv_ratio + 0.001),
    log10_scn = log10(scn_count_rep + 1),
    log1p_fdx = log1p(fdx),

    fdx_level = case_when(
      is.na(fdx)         ~ NA_character_,
      fdx == 0           ~ "none",
      fdx > 0 & fdx < 10 ~ "low",
      fdx >= 10          ~ "high"
    ) %>% factor(levels = c("none", "low", "high")),

    scn_level = case_when(
      is.na(scn_count_rep)  ~ NA_character_,
      scn_count_rep == 0    ~ "none",
      scn_count_rep < 2000  ~ "low",
      scn_count_rep >= 2000 ~ "high"
    ) %>% factor(levels = c("none", "low", "high")),

    fv_level_int = case_when(
      is.na(mean_fv_ratio) ~ NA_character_,
      mean_fv_ratio == 0 ~ "0",
      mean_fv_ratio > 0 & mean_fv_ratio < 1 ~ "0-1",
      mean_fv_ratio >= 1 & mean_fv_ratio < 2 ~ "1",
      mean_fv_ratio >= 2 & mean_fv_ratio < 3 ~ "2",
      mean_fv_ratio >= 3 & mean_fv_ratio < 4 ~ "3",
      mean_fv_ratio >= 4 & mean_fv_ratio < 5 ~ "4",
      mean_fv_ratio >= 5 & mean_fv_ratio < 6 ~ "5",
      mean_fv_ratio >= 6 & mean_fv_ratio < 7 ~ "6",
      mean_fv_ratio >= 7 ~ ">=7"
    ) %>%
      factor(levels = c("0", "0-1", "1", "2", "3", "4", "5", "6", ">=7"))
  )

df_plot <- df %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(soil_p_h),
    soil_p_h >= 6.0,
    soil_p_h <= 7.8
  )

#-----------------------------
# Models
#-----------------------------
mod_yield <- lmer(
  yield_kgha ~ soil_p_h + log1p_fdx + (1 | siteyear:log10_scn:log1p_fdx:rep:log10_fv:product),
  data = df_plot
)
emmeans::joint_tests(mod_yield)

mod_fdx <- lmer(
  fdx ~ soil_p_h + log10_fv + (1 | siteyear:log10_scn:rep:log10_fv:product),
  data = df_plot
)
emmeans::joint_tests(mod_fdx)

#-----------------------------
# Partial-effect predictions across soil pH
# Response is already on observed scale
#-----------------------------
pred_fdx <- ggpredict(mod_fdx, terms = "soil_p_h [all]")
pred_yield <- ggpredict(mod_yield, terms = "soil_p_h [all]")

df_siteyear <- df_plot %>%
  group_by(siteyear) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_soil_p_h   = mean(soil_p_h, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_fdx        = mean(fdx, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_yield_kgha = mean(yield_kgha, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )
#-----------------------------
# Helper: auto x breaks by 0.5 pH
#-----------------------------
ph_breaks <- function(x) {
  rng <- range(x, na.rm = TRUE)
  seq(floor(rng[1] * 2) / 2, ceiling(rng[2] * 2) / 2, by = 0.5)
}

all_x <- c(pred_fdx$x, pred_yield$x)
xbreaks <- ph_breaks(all_x)

#-----------------------------
# Helper: model R2 + soil pH P
# Italicized for parse = TRUE
#-----------------------------
model_label <- function(model, term = "soil_p_h") {
  coefs <- as.data.frame(coef(summary(model)))

  if (!term %in% rownames(coefs)) {
    stop(paste("Term not found in model coefficients:", term))
  }

  if (!"Pr(>|t|)" %in% colnames(coefs)) {
    stop("P-value column 'Pr(>|t|)' not found. Make sure lmerTest is loaded before fitting the model.")
  }

  p_val <- coefs[term, "Pr(>|t|)"]

  r2_obj <- performance::r2_nakagawa(model)
  r2_val <- r2_obj$R2_marginal

  p_txt <- if (p_val < 0.0001) {
    "italic(P) < 0.0001"
  } else {
    paste0("italic(P) == ", formatC(p_val, format = "f", digits = 3))
  }

  paste0(
    "italic(R)^2 == ", formatC(r2_val, format = "f", digits = 2),
    "*','~~", p_txt
  )
}

fdx_lab   <- model_label(mod_fdx, "soil_p_h")
yield_lab <- model_label(mod_yield, "soil_p_h")

#-----------------------------
# Theme
#-----------------------------
theme_siteyear <- theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major = element_line(linewidth = 0.2),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
    #axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.text = element_text(color = "black"),
    legend.position = "none"
  )

#-----------------------------
# Panel A: FDX
#-----------------------------
p_fdx_mean <- ggplot(pred_fdx, aes(x = x, y = predicted)) +
  geom_point(
    data = df_siteyear,
    aes(x = mean_soil_p_h, y = mean_fdx),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    alpha = 0.30,
    size = 2.0,
    color = "black"
  ) +
  geom_ribbon(
    aes(ymin = conf.low, ymax = conf.high),
    fill = "gray50", alpha = 0.18
  ) +
  geom_line(
    color = "darkred", linewidth = 1.1
  ) +
  annotate(
    "text",
    x = -Inf,
    y = Inf,
    label = fdx_lab,
    parse = TRUE,
    hjust = -0.15,
    vjust = 1.5,
    size = 3
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    limits = c(6.4, 7.6),
    breaks = seq(6.5, 7.5, by = 0.25),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.02, 0.02))
  ) +
  #coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, max(df_plot$fdx, na.rm = TRUE))) +
  labs(
    title = bquote(bold("A. LMM-predicted foliar disease index (FDX) by soil pH")),
    x = "Soil pH",
    y = "FDX"
  ) +
  theme_siteyear

#-----------------------------
# Panel B: Yield
#-----------------------------
p_yield_mean <- ggplot(pred_yield, aes(x = x, y = predicted)) +
  geom_point(
    data = df_siteyear,
    aes(x = mean_soil_p_h, y = mean_yield_kgha),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    alpha = 0.30,
    size = 2.0,
    color = "black"
  ) +
  geom_ribbon(
    aes(ymin = conf.low, ymax = conf.high),
    fill = "gray50", alpha = 0.18
  ) +
  geom_line(
    color = "darkred", linewidth = 1.1
  ) +
  annotate(
    "text",
    x = -Inf,
    y = Inf,
    label = yield_lab,
    parse = TRUE,
    hjust = -0.15,
    vjust = 1.5,
    size = 3
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    limits = c(6.4, 7.6),
    breaks = seq(6.5, 7.5, by = 0.25),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.02, 0.02))
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    breaks = seq(
      floor(min(df_plot$yield_kgha, na.rm = TRUE) / 500) * 500,
      ceiling(max(df_plot$yield_kgha, na.rm = TRUE) / 500) * 500,
      by = 500
    ),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.02, 0.05))
  ) +
  labs(
    title = bquote(bold("B. LMM-predicted soybean yield by soil pH")),
    x = "Soil pH",
    y = bquote("Yield (" * kg ~ ha^{-1} * ")")
  ) +
  theme_siteyear

#-----------------------------
# Combine
#-----------------------------
fig_siteyear <- (p_fdx_mean | p_yield_mean)

fig_siteyear

ggsave(
  "soil_pH_LMM_only_FDX_yield.png",
  plot = fig_siteyear,
  width = 13,
  height = 5,
  dpi = 600
)

```

### SCN LMM output ###
```{r}

#-----------------------------
# Filter plotting data
#-----------------------------
df_plot_scn <- df %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(log10_scn),
    !is.na(fdx),
    !is.na(yield_kgha)
  )

#-----------------------------
# Models
#-----------------------------
mod_yield_scn <- lmer(
  yield_kgha ~ log10_scn + log10_fv + soil_p_h + (1 | siteyear:soil_p_h:rep:log10_fv:product),
  data = df_plot_scn
)
emmeans::joint_tests(mod_yield_scn)

mod_fdx_scn <- lmer(
  fdx ~ log10_scn + log10_fv + (1 | siteyear:soil_p_h:rep:log10_fv:product),
  data = df_plot_scn
)
emmeans::joint_tests(mod_fdx_scn)

#-----------------------------
# Partial-effect predictions across log10_scn
# Response is already on observed scale
#-----------------------------
pred_fdx_scn <- ggpredict(mod_fdx_scn, terms = "log10_scn [0:4.5 by=0.01]")
pred_yield_scn <- ggpredict(mod_yield_scn, terms = "log10_scn [0:4.5 by=0.01]")

#-----------------------------
# Site-year means
#-----------------------------
df_siteyear_scn <- df_plot_scn %>%
  group_by(siteyear) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_log10_scn   = mean(log10_scn, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_fdx         = mean(fdx, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_yield_kgha  = mean(yield_kgha, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

#-----------------------------
# Helper: auto x breaks for log10_scn
#-----------------------------
scn_breaks <- function(x) {
  rng <- range(x, na.rm = TRUE)
  seq(floor(rng[1] * 2) / 2, ceiling(rng[2] * 2) / 2, by = 1)
}

all_x_scn <- c(pred_fdx_scn$x, pred_yield_scn$x)
xbreaks_scn <- scn_breaks(all_x_scn)

#-----------------------------
# Helper: model R2 + x-variable P
# Italicized for parse = TRUE
#-----------------------------
model_label_term <- function(model, term) {
  coefs <- as.data.frame(coef(summary(model)))

  if (!term %in% rownames(coefs)) {
    stop(paste("Term not found in model coefficients:", term))
  }

  if (!"Pr(>|t|)" %in% colnames(coefs)) {
    stop("P-value column 'Pr(>|t|)' not found. Make sure lmerTest is loaded before fitting the model.")
  }

  p_val <- coefs[term, "Pr(>|t|)"]

  r2_obj <- performance::r2_nakagawa(model)
  r2_val <- r2_obj$R2_marginal

  p_txt <- if (p_val < 0.0001) {
    "italic(P) < 0.0001"
  } else {
    paste0("italic(P) == ", formatC(p_val, format = "f", digits = 4))
  }

  paste0(
    "italic(R)^2 == ", formatC(r2_val, format = "f", digits = 2),
    "*','~~", p_txt
  )
}

fdx_lab_scn   <- model_label_term(mod_fdx_scn, "log10_scn")
yield_lab_scn <- model_label_term(mod_yield_scn, "log10_scn")

#-----------------------------
# Theme
#-----------------------------
theme_siteyear <- theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major = element_line(linewidth = 0.2),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
    axis.text = element_text(color = "black"),
    legend.position = "none"
  )

#-----------------------------
# Panel A: FDX by log10_scn
#-----------------------------
p_fdx_scn <- ggplot(pred_fdx_scn, aes(x = x, y = predicted)) +
  geom_point(
    data = df_siteyear_scn,
    aes(x = mean_log10_scn, y = mean_fdx),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    alpha = 0.30,
    size = 2.0,
    color = "black"
  ) +
  geom_vline(
    xintercept = log10(200),
    linetype = "dotted",
    color = "black",
    linewidth = 0.4
  ) +
  geom_vline(
    xintercept = log10(2000),
    linetype = "dotted",
    color = "black",
    linewidth = 0.4
  ) +
  geom_ribbon(
    aes(ymin = conf.low, ymax = conf.high),
    fill = "gray50", alpha = 0.18
  ) +
  geom_line(
    color = "darkred", linewidth = 1.1
  ) +
  annotate(
    "text",
    x = -Inf,
    y = Inf,
    label = fdx_lab_scn,
    parse = TRUE,
    hjust = -0.15,
    vjust = 1.5,
    size = 3
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = xbreaks_scn,
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.02, 0.02))
  ) +
  labs(
    title = bquote(bold("A. LMM-predicted foliar disease index (FDX) by SCN density")),
    x = expression(log[10] * "SCN density (eggs + J2∙100 cm"^3 * " soil)"),
    y = "FDX"
  ) +
  theme_siteyear

#-----------------------------
# Panel B: Yield by log10_scn
#-----------------------------
p_yield_scn <- ggplot(pred_yield_scn, aes(x = x, y = predicted)) +
  geom_point(
    data = df_siteyear_scn,
    aes(x = mean_log10_scn, y = mean_yield_kgha),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    alpha = 0.30,
    size = 2.0,
    color = "black"
  ) +
  geom_vline(
  xintercept = log10(200),
  linetype = "dotted",
  color = "black",
  linewidth = 0.4
  ) +
  geom_vline(
    xintercept = log10(2000),
    linetype = "dotted",
    color = "black",
    linewidth = 0.4
  ) +
  geom_ribbon(
    aes(ymin = conf.low, ymax = conf.high),
    fill = "gray50", alpha = 0.18
  ) +
  geom_line(
    color = "darkred", linewidth = 1.1
  ) +
  annotate(
    "text",
    x = -Inf,
    y = Inf,
    label = yield_lab_scn,
    parse = TRUE,
    hjust = -0.15,
    vjust = 1.5,
    size = 3
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = xbreaks_scn,
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.02, 0.02))
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    breaks = seq(
      floor(min(df_plot_scn$yield_kgha, na.rm = TRUE) / 500) * 500,
      ceiling(max(df_plot_scn$yield_kgha, na.rm = TRUE) / 500) * 500,
      by = 500
    ),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.02, 0.05))
  ) +
  labs(
    title = bquote(bold("B. LMM-predicted soybean yield by SCN density")),
    x = expression(log[10] * "SCN density (eggs + J2∙100 cm"^3 * " soil)"),
    y = bquote("Yield (" * kg ~ ha^{-1} * ")")
  ) +
  theme_siteyear

#-----------------------------
# Combine
#-----------------------------
fig_scn <- (p_fdx_scn | p_yield_scn)

fig_scn

ggsave(
  "log10SCN_LMM_FDX_yield.png",
  plot = fig_scn,
  width = 13,
  height = 5,
  dpi = 600
)


```

### Fv abundance vs. FDX and yield ###
```{r}
#-----------------------------
# Filter plotting data
#-----------------------------
df_plot_fv <- df %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(mean_fv_ratio),
    !is.na(fdx),
    !is.na(yield_kgha)
  )

#-----------------------------
# Models
#-----------------------------
mod_yield_fv <- lmer(
  yield_kgha ~ mean_fv_ratio + log10_scn + soil_p_h + (1 | siteyear:soil_p_h:log10_scn:rep:product),
  data = df_plot_fv
)
emmeans::joint_tests(mod_yield_fv)

mod_fdx_fv <- lmer(
  fdx ~ mean_fv_ratio + log10_scn + (1 | siteyear:soil_p_h:log10_scn:rep:product),
  data = df_plot_fv
)
emmeans::joint_tests(mod_fdx_fv)

#-----------------------------
# Partial-effect predictions
#-----------------------------
fv_min <- floor(min(df_plot_fv$mean_fv_ratio, na.rm = TRUE))
fv_max <- ceiling(max(df_plot_fv$mean_fv_ratio, na.rm = TRUE))

pred_fdx_fv <- ggpredict(
  mod_fdx_fv,
  terms = paste0("mean_fv_ratio [", fv_min, ":", fv_max, " by=0.01]")
)

pred_yield_fv <- ggpredict(
  mod_yield_fv,
  terms = paste0("mean_fv_ratio [", fv_min, ":", fv_max, " by=0.01]")
)

#-----------------------------
# Site-year means
#-----------------------------
df_siteyear_fv <- df_plot_fv %>%
  group_by(siteyear) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_fv_ratio   = mean(mean_fv_ratio, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_fdx        = mean(fdx, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_yield_kgha = mean(yield_kgha, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

#-----------------------------
# Model labels
#-----------------------------
fdx_lab_fv   <- model_label_term(mod_fdx_fv, "mean_fv_ratio")
yield_lab_fv <- model_label_term(mod_yield_fv, "mean_fv_ratio")

#-----------------------------
# FV bin boundaries (IMPORTANT)
#-----------------------------
fv_breaks_lines <- c(1, 2, 3, 4, 5)
fv_xlim <- range(df_siteyear_fv$mean_fv_ratio, na.rm = TRUE)

#-----------------------------
# Panel A: FDX
#-----------------------------
p_fdx_fv <- ggplot(pred_fdx_fv, aes(x = x, y = predicted)) +
  geom_point(
    data = df_siteyear_fv,
    aes(x = mean_fv_ratio, y = mean_fdx),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    alpha = 0.30,
    size = 2.0,
    color = "black"
  ) +
  geom_vline(
    xintercept = fv_breaks_lines,
    linetype = "dotted",
    color = "black",
    linewidth = 0.4
  ) +
  geom_ribbon(
    aes(ymin = conf.low, ymax = conf.high),
    fill = "gray50", alpha = 0.18
  ) +
  geom_line(
    color = "darkred", linewidth = 1.1
  ) +
  annotate(
    "text",
    x = -Inf,
    y = Inf,
    label = fdx_lab_fv,
    parse = TRUE,
    hjust = -0.15,
    vjust = 1.5,
    size = 3
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
  limits = c(0, fv_xlim[2]),
  breaks = seq(0, floor(fv_xlim[2]), by = 1),
  expand = expansion(mult = c(0.02, 0.02))
  ) +
  labs(
    title = bquote(bold("A. LMM-predicted foliar disease by " * italic("F. virguliforme") * " DNA ratio")),
    #x = expression("F. virguliforme DNA (fg∙ng"^-1 * " total eDNA)"),
    x = expression(italic("F. virguliforme") ~ "DNA abundance (fg∙ng"^-1 * "total eDNA)"),
    y = "Foliar diesease index (FDX)"
  ) +
  theme_siteyear

#-----------------------------
# Panel B: Yield
#-----------------------------
p_yield_fv <- ggplot(pred_yield_fv, aes(x = x, y = predicted)) +
  geom_point(
    data = df_siteyear_fv,
    aes(x = mean_fv_ratio, y = mean_yield_kgha),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    alpha = 0.30,
    size = 2.0,
    color = "black"
  ) +
  geom_vline(
    xintercept = fv_breaks_lines,
    linetype = "dotted",
    color = "black",
    linewidth = 0.4
  ) +
  geom_ribbon(
    aes(ymin = conf.low, ymax = conf.high),
    fill = "gray50", alpha = 0.18
  ) +
  geom_line(
    color = "darkred", linewidth = 1.1
  ) +
  annotate(
    "text",
    x = -Inf,
    y = Inf,
    label = yield_lab_fv,
    parse = TRUE,
    hjust = -0.15,
    vjust = 1.5,
    size = 3
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
  limits = c(0, fv_xlim[2]),
  breaks = seq(0, floor(fv_xlim[2]), by = 1),
  expand = expansion(mult = c(0.02, 0.02))
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    breaks = seq(
      floor(min(df_plot_fv$yield_kgha, na.rm = TRUE) / 500) * 500,
      ceiling(max(df_plot_fv$yield_kgha, na.rm = TRUE) / 500) * 500,
      by = 500
    ),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.02, 0.05))
  ) +
  labs(
    title = bquote(bold("B. LMM-predicted soybean yield by " * italic("F. virguliforme") * " DNA ratio")),
    #x = expression("F. virguliforme DNA (fg∙ng"^-1 * " total eDNA)"),
    x = expression(italic("F. virguliforme") ~ "DNA abundance (fg∙ng"^-1 * "total eDNA)"),
    y = bquote("Yield (" * kg ~ ha^{-1} * ")")
  ) +
  theme_siteyear

#-----------------------------
# Combine
#-----------------------------
fig_fv <- (p_fdx_fv | p_yield_fv)

fig_fv

ggsave(
  "FVratio_LMM_FDX_yield.png",
  plot = fig_fv,
  width = 13,
  height = 5,
  dpi = 600
)
```


### FDX by yield ###
```{r}

#-----------------------------
# Prepare plotting data
#-----------------------------
df_plot_fdx <- df %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(fdx),
    !is.na(yield_kgha)
  )

#-----------------------------
# Site-year means
#-----------------------------
df_siteyear_fdx <- df_plot_fdx %>%
  group_by(siteyear) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_fdx        = mean(fdx, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_yield_kgha = mean(yield_kgha, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

#-----------------------------
# Fit LMM
#-----------------------------
mod_fdx_yield <- lmer(
  yield_kgha ~ fdx + log10_fv + log10_scn + soil_p_h +
    (1 | siteyear:soil_p_h:log10_scn:mean_fv_ratio:rep:product),
  data = df_plot_fdx
)

emmeans::joint_tests(mod_fdx_yield)

#-----------------------------
# Partial-effect predictions across FDX
# Response is already on observed scale
#-----------------------------
pred_fdx_yield <- ggpredict(mod_fdx_yield, terms = "fdx [all]")

#-----------------------------
# Helper: model R2 + FDX P
# Italicized for parse = TRUE
#-----------------------------
model_label_term <- function(model, term) {
  coefs <- as.data.frame(coef(summary(model)))

  if (!term %in% rownames(coefs)) {
    stop(paste("Term not found in model coefficients:", term))
  }

  if (!"Pr(>|t|)" %in% colnames(coefs)) {
    stop("P-value column 'Pr(>|t|)' not found. Make sure lmerTest is loaded before fitting the model.")
  }

  p_val <- coefs[term, "Pr(>|t|)"]

  r2_obj <- performance::r2_nakagawa(model)
  r2_val <- r2_obj$R2_marginal

  p_txt <- if (p_val < 0.0001) {
    "italic(P) < 0.0001"
  } else {
    paste0("italic(P) == ", formatC(p_val, format = "f", digits = 4))
  }

  paste0(
    "italic(R)^2 == ", formatC(r2_val, format = "f", digits = 2),
    "*','~~", p_txt
  )
}

fdx_yield_lab <- model_label_term(mod_fdx_yield, "fdx")

#-----------------------------
# Plot
#-----------------------------
p_fdx_yield <- ggplot(pred_fdx_yield, aes(x = x, y = predicted)) +
  geom_point(
    data = df_siteyear_fdx,
    aes(x = mean_fdx, y = mean_yield_kgha),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    alpha = 0.35,
    size = 2.2,
    color = "black"
  ) +
  geom_ribbon(
    aes(ymin = conf.low, ymax = conf.high),
    fill = "gray50",
    alpha = 0.18
  ) +
  geom_line(
    color = "darkred",
    linewidth = 1.1
  ) +
  geom_vline(
    xintercept = 10,
    linetype = "dotted",
    color = "black",
    linewidth = 0.4
  ) +
  annotate(
    "text",
    x = -Inf,
    y = Inf,
    label = fdx_yield_lab,
    parse = TRUE,
    hjust = -0.15,
    vjust = 1.5,
    size = 3
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
  limits = c(0, ceiling(max(df_siteyear_fdx$mean_fdx, na.rm = TRUE))),
  breaks = seq(0, ceiling(max(df_siteyear_fdx$mean_fdx, na.rm = TRUE)), by = 5),
  expand = expansion(mult = c(0.02, 0.02))
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    breaks = seq(
      floor(min(df_siteyear_fdx$mean_yield_kgha, na.rm = TRUE) / 500) * 500,
      ceiling(max(df_siteyear_fdx$mean_yield_kgha, na.rm = TRUE) / 500) * 500,
      by = 500
    ),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.02, 0.05))
  ) +
  labs(
    title = bquote(bold("LMM-predicted soybean yield by foliar disease index (FDX)")),
    x = "Foliar disease index (FDX)",
    y = bquote("Yield (" * kg ~ ha^{-1} * ")")
  ) +
  theme_siteyear

p_fdx_yield

ggsave(
  "FDX_vs_yield.png",
  plot = p_fdx_yield,
  width = 6.5,
  height = 5,
  dpi = 600
)

```


### SDS tolerance by seed treatement by FDX level ###
```{r}
#-----------------------------
# Prepare plotting data
#-----------------------------
df_plot_tol <- df %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(sds_tol),
    !is.na(product),
    !is.na(yield_kgha)
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    product = factor(product, levels = c("Base", "ILEVO", "Saltro")),
    product_label = factor(
      product,
      levels = c("Base", "ILEVO", "Saltro"),
      labels = c("CB\u2020", "CB + fluopyram", "CB + pydiflumetofen")
    ),
    sds_tol_label = factor(
      sds_tol,
      levels = c("r", "s"),
      labels = c("Partially resistant (R)", "Susceptible (S)")
    ),
    fdx_level_label = case_when(
      is.na(fdx_level) ~ "FDX not available",
      fdx_level == "none" ~ "None (FDX = 0)",
      fdx_level == "low"  ~ "Low (0 < FDX < 10)",
      fdx_level == "high" ~ "High (FDX \u2265 10)"
    ) %>%
      factor(
        levels = c(
          "None (FDX = 0)",
          "Low (0 < FDX < 10)",
          "High (FDX \u2265 10)",
          "FDX not available"
        )
      )
  )

min_yield_tol <- min(df_plot_tol$yield_kgha, na.rm = TRUE)

#-----------------------------
# Plot
#-----------------------------
p_tol_yield <- ggplot(
  df_plot_tol,
  aes(x = product_label, y = yield_kgha, fill = product)
  ) +
  geom_boxplot(
    color = "black",
    outlier.shape = NA,
    alpha = 0.8,
    width = 0.8,
    linewidth = 0.4
  ) +
  geom_jitter(
    width = 0.12,
    alpha = 0.22,
    size = 1.4,
    color = "gray35"
  ) +
  facet_grid(
    rows = vars(fdx_level_label),
    cols = vars(sds_tol_label)
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(
      "Base" = "#F8766D",
      "ILEVO" = "#00BA38",
      "Saltro" = "#619CFF"
    )
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    breaks = c(0, 2000, 4000, 6000),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.01, 0.03))
  ) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 8500)) +
  labs(
    x = "Seed treatment product",
    y = bquote("Yield (" * kg ~ ha^{-1} * ")")
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major = element_line(linewidth = 0.2),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.text = element_text(color = "black"),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.title.x = element_text(margin = margin(t = 10)),
    legend.position = "none"
  ) +
  stat_compare_means(
  comparisons = list(
    c("CB\u2020", "CB + fluopyram"),
    c("CB\u2020", "CB + pydiflumetofen"),
    c("CB + fluopyram", "CB + pydiflumetofen")
  ),
  method = "t.test",
  p.adjust.method = "BH",
  label = "p.signif",     #exact p-values
  hide.ns = FALSE,
  size = 3,
  step.increase = 0.1,
  #label.x.npc = 0.2   #default is ~0.5, so this shifts ~10% left
  )

p_tol_yield

ggsave(
  "sds_tol_product_fdxlevel_yield_boxplot.png",
  plot = p_tol_yield,
  width = 10,
  height = 10,
  dpi = 600
)

```



# Source module: Longitudinal_SCN_SDS_PCA

<!-- Imported and lightly cleaned from `Longitudinal_SCN_SDS_PCA.Rmd`. -->

# 1. Overview

This document is a reproducible workflow for:

- Preprocessing multi-year SCN / SDS seed treatment field trial data  
- Running **Principal Component Analysis (PCA)** on agronomic and disease-related variables  
- Visualizing PCs by **SCN level**, **F. virguliforme (SDS) level**, and **location/year**  
- Exploring multivariate patterns via **k-means clustering**  
- Optionally launching an **interactive PCA GUI** for exploratory analysis.

The code is written so colleagues can adapt it to similar longitudinal plant-pathology trials.

---

# 2. Data import and initial cleaning

> 🔧 **Assumption:** Your master dataset is saved as  
> `C:/Longitudinal/full2017_2024.csv`  
> with one row per plot (year × location × treatment × replicate).

Adjust the path if needed.

```{r import}
raw <- readr::read_csv("C:/Longitudinal/full2017_2024_pca.csv") %>%
  janitor::clean_names()

# Quick skim
dplyr::glimpse(raw)
```

---

# 3. Derive SCN level and set up factors

We categorize **SCN egg + J2 counts** into three levels:

- `none`: SCN count = 0  
- `low`: 0 < SCN < 2000  
- `high`: SCN ≥ 2000  

We also coerce key design variables into factors for plotting and downstream modelling.

```{r factors}
raw <- raw %>%
  mutate(
    # SCN level factor
    scn_level = case_when(
      scn_count_rep == 0 ~ "none",
      scn_count_rep > 0 & scn_count_rep < 2000 ~ "low",
      scn_count_rep >= 2000 ~ "high",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ),
    scn_level = factor(scn_level, levels = c("none", "low", "high"))
  )

df <- raw %>%
  mutate(
    year              = factor(year),
    location          = factor(location),
    mean_fv_ratio_int = factor(mean_fv_ratio_int, ordered = TRUE),
    soil_p_h          = factor(soil_p_h, ordered = TRUE),
    cultivar          = factor(cultivar),
    sds_tol           = factor(sds_tol),
    product           = factor(product),
    rep               = factor(rep),
    fdx_level         = factor(fdx_level),
    volatility        = factor(volatility)
  )

num_vars <- c("scn_count_rep", "mean_fv_ratio",
              "fdx","yield_mtha")

df_mod<- raw %>%
  mutate(
    year              = factor(year),
    location          = factor(location),
    mean_fv_ratio_int = factor(mean_fv_ratio_int, ordered = TRUE),
    cultivar          = factor(cultivar),
    sds_tol           = factor(sds_tol),
    product           = factor(product),
    rep               = factor(rep),
    fdx_level         = factor(fdx_level),
    volatility        = factor(volatility),
    usd_ha = (yield_mtha*harv_price_kg), #response var that includes market price at harv
    fdx_std = (scale(fdx)) #rescaled fdx values based on mean = 0, sd = 1
  )

df[num_vars] <- scale(df[num_vars]) #standardizes continuous numeric vars

dplyr::glimpse(df)
```

---

# 4. Select numeric features for PCA

We will:

1. Keep **all numeric variables** as initial candidates  
2. Drop obvious **ID / economic** variables that could dominate PCA (e.g., yield & price)  
3. Define a small set of **metadata columns** to carry along for plotting/stratification.

```{r select-numeric}
# ID-like / outcome variables to exclude from PCA feature space
id_like <- c(
  "yield_mtha","treatment_effect_fdx","treatment_effect_yield","soil_p_h","yield_bua","yield_diff_bua",
  "proj_price_kg","proj_price_bu","harv_price_kg",
  "harv_price_bu","diff_price_bua", "diff_price_kgha"
)

# Metadata we want for colouring / faceting later
meta_cols <- c("year", "location", "mean_fv_ratio_int",
               "fdx_level", "scn_level", "product")

# All numeric predictors
num_df <- df %>%
  dplyr::select(where(is.numeric)) %>%
  dplyr::select(-any_of(intersect(names(.), id_like)))

dplyr::glimpse(num_df)
```

We now create a **complete-case mask** based on both the metadata and numeric predictors. This ensures:

- `df_nona` (metadata)  
- `num_df_nona` (numeric matrix)  
- PCA result (`pr$x`)  

are all perfectly aligned by row.

```{r complete-cases}
keep <- complete.cases(
  df[, meta_cols],
  num_df
)

df_nona      <- df[keep, , drop = FALSE]
num_df_nona  <- num_df[keep, , drop = FALSE]

#grouping factor for year:location:rep (matches your random effect)
df_nona$SiteYear<- interaction(df_nona$year, df_nona$location, df_nona$rep, drop = TRUE)

#df_nona$soil_pH_std <- scale(df_nona$soil_p_h)[,1]

# Optional: rename yield_diff if you prefer a clearer name
# df_nona     <- dplyr::rename(df_nona, treatment_effect_kgha = yield_diff)
# num_df_nona <- dplyr::rename(num_df_nona, treatment_effect_kgha = yield_diff)

dim(df_nona)
dim(num_df_nona)
```

---

# 5. PCA via `recipes` + `prcomp`

We use `recipes` to:

- Median-impute missing numeric values (if any remain)  
- Remove zero-variance and near-zero-variance predictors  
- Standardize numeric variables (z-scores)  

Then we run PCA on the resulting matrix.

```{r pca-recipes}
preproc <- recipes::recipe(~ ., data = num_df_nona) %>%
  recipes::step_impute_median(recipes::all_numeric_predictors()) %>%
  recipes::step_zv(recipes::all_numeric_predictors()) %>%
  recipes::step_nzv(recipes::all_numeric_predictors()) %>%
  recipes::step_normalize(recipes::all_numeric_predictors()) %>%
  recipes::prep()

X <- recipes::juice(preproc, composition = "data.frame")
stopifnot(nrow(X) > 0, ncol(X) > 1)

pr <- prcomp(as.matrix(X), center = FALSE, scale. = FALSE, retx = TRUE)

# Attach metadata to PCA scores
pca_scores <- as.data.frame(pr$x) %>%
  as_tibble(rownames = "row_id") %>%
  bind_cols(df_nona %>% dplyr::select(all_of(meta_cols)))

head(pca_scores)
```

---

# 6. Variance explained and loadings

## 6.1 Scree plot

```{r scree}
# Percent variance for each principal component
var_pct <- round(100 * (pr$sdev^2 / sum(pr$sdev^2)), 1)

pca_variance <- tibble(
  pc_index  = seq_along(var_pct),             # 1, 2, 3, ...
  pc_label  = paste0("PC", pc_index),         # "PC1", "PC2", ...
  value     = var_pct
)

pca_variance

scree_plot <- ggplot(pca_variance,
                     aes(x = pc_index, y = value)) +
  geom_col() +
  geom_point() +
  geom_line() +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = pca_variance$pc_index,
    labels = pca_variance$pc_label
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Principal Component",
    y = "Percent variance explained",
    title = "PCA Scree Plot"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

scree_plot

ggsave("scree_plot.png",scree_plot,width = 8,height = 6,units = "in",dpi = 600)
```

## 6.2 Loadings and top contributors per PC
### note: having 'plyr' loaded will break this code
```{r loadings}
pca_loadings <- as.data.frame(pr$rotation) %>%
  as_tibble(rownames = "variable") %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols      = starts_with("PC"),
    names_to  = "PC",
    values_to = "loading"
  )

head(pca_loadings)

make_pc_axis_labels <- function(pr, pca_loadings, n_top = 3) {
  # get variance explained
  var_expl <- round(100 * (pr$sdev^2 / sum(pr$sdev^2)), 1)
  
  # summarize top loadings for each PC
  label_tbl <- pca_loadings %>%
    group_by(PC) %>%
    slice_max(order_by = abs(loading), n = n_top, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
    mutate(direction = if_else(loading > 0, "↑", "↓")) %>%
    summarise(
      vars = paste0(variable, direction, collapse = ", "),
      .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    mutate(
      pc_num = as.integer(gsub("PC", "", PC)),
      label  = glue::glue("{PC} ({var_expl[pc_num]}%): {vars}")
    )
  
  # output as named list for ggplot labs
  labels <- list(
    x = label_tbl$label[label_tbl$PC == "PC1"],
    y = label_tbl$label[label_tbl$PC == "PC2"],
    xx = label_tbl$label[label_tbl$PC == "PC3"],
    yy = label_tbl$label[label_tbl$PC == "PC4"]
  )
  
  return(labels)
}

axis_labels <- make_pc_axis_labels(pr, pca_loadings, n_top = 3)

```

Focus on the top contributors for PC1 and PC2:

```{r loadings-top}
top_n <- 4

pca_loadings_pc12_top <- pca_loadings %>%
  filter(PC %in% c("PC1", "PC2", "PC3", "PC4")) %>%
  group_by(PC) %>%
  slice_max(order_by = abs(loading), n = top_n, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
  ungroup()

load_plot<-ggplot(pca_loadings_pc12_top,
       aes(x = reorder(variable, abs(loading)),
           y = loading, fill = loading)) +
  geom_col() +
  scale_fill_gradient2(name = "Loading") +
  facet_wrap(~ PC, scales = "free_y") +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    x = "Variable",
    y = "Loading",
    title = glue("Top {top_n} loadings for PC1 - PC4")
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

load_plot

ggsave("load_plot.png",load_plot,width = 8,height = 6,units = "in",dpi = 600)

```

---

# 7. PCA biplots for SCN & SDS interpretation

Here we colour PCA scores by:

- **SCN level** (`scn_level`)  
- **F. virguliforme level** (`mean_fv_ratio_int`)  
- **Location** (optional exploratory look)

We first compute a helper table for plotting:

```{r pca-scores-prep}
scores_tbl <- as.data.frame(pr$x) %>%
  as_tibble(rownames = "row_id") %>%
  bind_cols(df_nona %>% dplyr::select(all_of(meta_cols)))

var_pct <- round(100 * (pr$sdev^2 / sum(pr$sdev^2)), 1)
xlab_pc12 <- glue("PC1 ({var_pct[1]}%)")
ylab_pc12 <- glue("PC2 ({var_pct[2]}%)")
```

## 7.1 Biplot helper function

```{r biplot-helper}
pca_biplot_static <- function(pr,
                              df_meta,
                              x_pc = 1,
                              y_pc = 2,
                              group_var = "product",
                              show_ellipses = TRUE,
                              show_arrows   = TRUE,
                              ellipse_type  = "t",
                              palette       = NULL,
                              title         = NULL) {

  scores <- as.data.frame(pr$x)
  colnames(scores) <- paste0("PC", seq_len(ncol(scores)))
  dat <- scores %>%
    as_tibble(rownames = "row_id") %>%
    bind_cols(df_meta)

  x_name <- paste0("PC", x_pc)
  y_name <- paste0("PC", y_pc)

  var_pct <- round(100 * (pr$sdev^2 / sum(pr$sdev^2)), 1)
  xlab <- paste0(axis_labels$x)
  ylab <- paste0(axis_labels$y)

  # grouping
  if (!is.null(group_var) && group_var != "none" && group_var %in% names(dat)) {
    hab <- forcats::fct_explicit_na(dat[[group_var]], na_level = "NA")
    dat$hab <- hab
    if (is.null(palette)) {
      palette <- hcl.colors(nlevels(hab), "Dynamic")
    } else if (length(palette) < nlevels(hab)) {
      palette <- rep(palette, length.out = nlevels(hab))
    }
    pal_named <- setNames(palette, levels(hab))
    grp_label <- group_var
  } else {
    dat$hab <- NULL
    pal_named <- NULL
    grp_label <- "none"
    show_ellipses <- FALSE
  }

  # loadings for these PCs
  loadings <- as.data.frame(pr$rotation[, c(x_pc, y_pc), drop = FALSE]) %>%
    rownames_to_column("variable") %>%
    rename(lx = 2, ly = 3)

  range_x <- range(dat[[x_name]], na.rm = TRUE)
  range_y <- range(dat[[y_name]], na.rm = TRUE)
  scale_factor <- 0.7 * min(diff(range_x), diff(range_y))
  max_loading  <- max(abs(c(loadings$lx, loadings$ly)))
  loadings <- loadings %>%
    mutate(
      ax = lx / max_loading * scale_factor,
      ay = ly / max_loading * scale_factor
    )

  # base plot
  if (!is.null(dat$hab)) {
    p <- ggplot(dat, aes(x = .data[[x_name]], y = .data[[y_name]], color = hab)) +
      geom_point(size = 2.3, alpha = 0.85) +
      scale_color_manual(values = pal_named, name = grp_label)
  } else {
    p <- ggplot(dat, aes(x = .data[[x_name]], y = .data[[y_name]])) +
      geom_point(size = 2.3, alpha = 0.85, color = "black")
  }

  if (!is.null(dat$hab) && isTRUE(show_ellipses)) {
    p <- p +
      stat_ellipse(aes(group = hab, color = hab),
                   type = ellipse_type,
                   level = 0.95,
                   linewidth = 0.7,
                   show.legend = FALSE)
  }

  if (isTRUE(show_arrows)) {
    p <- p +
      geom_segment(
        data = loadings,
        aes(x = 0, y = 0, xend = ax, yend = ay),
        inherit.aes = FALSE,
        arrow = arrow(length = unit(0.18, "cm")),
        color = "black", linewidth = 0.3
      ) +
      geom_text(
        data = loadings,
        aes(x = ax, y = ay, label = variable),
        inherit.aes = FALSE,
        size = 3,
        vjust = 1.1
      )
  }

  if (is.null(title)) {
    title <- glue("PCA Biplot: PC{x_pc} vs PC{y_pc} (grouped by {grp_label})")
  }

  p +
    coord_equal() +
    theme_minimal() +
    labs(title = title, x = xlab, y = ylab) +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(size = 13, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
      legend.position = "right",
      panel.grid = element_blank()
    )
}
```

## 7.2 Biplots by SCN level and SDS level

```{r biplots-scn-sds}
pal_scn <- c("none" = "#66A61E", "low" = "#E6AB02", "high" = "#D95F02")
pal_fdx <- c("none" = "#D95F02", "low" = "#E6AB02", "high" = "#66A61E")
pal_trt <- c("none" = "#438FFF", "low" = "#478F48", "high" = "#FFAC00")

p_trt <- pca_biplot_static(
  pr,
  df_meta     = df_nona,
  x_pc        = 1,
  y_pc        = 2,
  group_var   = "product",
  show_ellipses = TRUE,
  show_arrows   = TRUE,
  ellipse_type  = "t",
  palette       = pal_trt,
  title         = "PC1 vs PC2 by seed treatment"
)+ ggplot2::labs(x = axis_labels$x,
    y = axis_labels$y)

p_fv<- pca_biplot_static(
  pr,
  df_meta     = df_nona,
  x_pc        = 1,
  y_pc        = 2,
  group_var   = "mean_fv_ratio_int",
  show_ellipses = TRUE,
  show_arrows   = TRUE,
  ellipse_type  = "t",
  palette       = NULL,
  title         = "PC1 vs PC2 by F. virguliforme level"
) + ggplot2::labs(x = axis_labels$x,
    y = axis_labels$y)

p_scn<- pca_biplot_static(
  pr,
  df_meta     = df_nona,
  x_pc        = 1,
  y_pc        = 2,
  group_var   = "scn_level",
  show_ellipses = TRUE,
  show_arrows   = TRUE,
  ellipse_type  = "t",
  palette       = pal_scn,
  title         = "PC1 vs PC2 by SCN level"
) + ggplot2::labs(x = axis_labels$x,
    y = axis_labels$y)

p_site <- pca_biplot_static(
  pr,
  df_meta     = df_nona,
  x_pc        = 1,
  y_pc        = 2,
  group_var   = "location",
  show_ellipses = TRUE,
  show_arrows   = TRUE,
  ellipse_type  = "t",
```
