#========================================================
# Figure 1. Michigan SDHI seed treatment trial locations
#========================================================
#
# Manuscript output:
#   Figure 1 -> Locations of SDS-specific SDHI seed treatment trials in Michigan.
#
# Notes:
#   - This block uses Michigan county land centroids for county-level trial locations.
#   - East Lansing locations are grouped as "MSU CAMPUS".
#   - Bubble size represents the number of years conducted at each location.
#   - If df_map2.csv already exists with latitude/longitude, the centroid-building
#     portion can be skipped and df_map can be read directly.
#========================================================

```{r figure-1-map-mi, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# Removed 5 redundant package-loading/install line(s); packages are loaded in the setup section.

# ---- Build or import mapping data ----
# Option A: use exported mapping file with location/year/county columns
df_map <- read.csv("df_map2.csv", header = TRUE)

# ---- Generate Michigan county centroids from Census cartographic boundary file ----
# This produces land-aware county centroids and avoids centroids falling in the Great Lakes.
url <- "https://www2.census.gov/geo/tiger/GENZ2022/shp/cb_2022_us_county_20m.zip"

if (!file.exists("cb_2022_us_county_20m.zip")) {
  download.file(url, "cb_2022_us_county_20m.zip", mode = "wb")
}

if (!dir.exists("counties")) {
  unzip("cb_2022_us_county_20m.zip", exdir = "counties")
}

counties <- st_read("counties/cb_2022_us_county_20m.shp", quiet = TRUE)

target_names <- c(
  "Allegan", "Clinton", "Hillsdale", "Ingham", "Lenawee",
  "Montcalm", "Saginaw", "Sanilac", "St. Joseph", "Van Buren"
)

centroids_ll <- counties %>%
  filter(STATEFP == "26", NAME %in% target_names) %>%
  st_transform(3078) %>%
  st_centroid() %>%
  st_transform(4326)

coords <- st_coordinates(centroids_ll)

df_centroids <- centroids_ll %>%
  st_drop_geometry() %>%
  mutate(
    Latitude  = round(coords[, 2], 2),
    Longitude = round(coords[, 1], 2)
  ) %>%
  select(County = NAME, Latitude, Longitude) %>%
  arrange(County)

write_csv(df_centroids, "county_land_centroids_MI.csv")

# ---- Normalize centroid keys and add MSU campus point ----
df_centroids <- df_centroids %>%
  rename(county = County, lat = Latitude, lon = Longitude) %>%
  mutate(
    county = toupper(county),
    county = gsub("^ST\\.\\s*JOSEPH$", "STJOSEPH", county),
    county = gsub("\\s+", "", county)
  ) %>%
  bind_rows(data.frame(county = "MSU CAMPUS", lat = 42.70, lon = -84.49)) %>%
  arrange(county)

# ---- Join centroids to mapping dataframe ----
df_map <- df_map %>%
  mutate(
    county = toupper(trimws(county)),
    county = gsub("^ST\\.\\s*JOSEPH$", "STJOSEPH", county),
    county = gsub("\\s+", "", county)
  ) %>%
  left_join(df_centroids %>% select(county, lat, lon), by = "county") %>%
  mutate(
    lat = coalesce(lat.y, lat.x),
    lon = coalesce(lon.y, lon.x)
  ) %>%
  select(-any_of(c("lat.x", "lon.x", "lat.y", "lon.y")))

# ---- Michigan basemap ----
us_states <- map_data("state")
mi <- us_states %>% filter(region == "michigan")

# ---- Count years tested at each location ----
df_counts <- df_map %>%
  mutate(
    loc_key = toupper(trimws(.data[["loc_col"]])),
    loc_key = case_when(
      loc_key %in% c("E_LANSING_PLP", "E_LANSING_AGR") ~ "MSU CAMPUS",
      TRUE ~ loc_key
    )
  ) %>%
  group_by(loc_key) %>%
  summarise(
    years_tested = n_distinct(year),
    lat = first(lat),
    lon = first(lon),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(years_tested))

# ---- Axis breaks and bounds ----
size_breaks <- sort(unique(df_counts$years_tested))

lon_range <- c(-90.2, -82.5)
lat_range <- c(41.9, 47.3)

lon_breaks <- seq(ceiling(lon_range[1]), floor(lon_range[2]), by = 1)
lat_breaks <- seq(ceiling(lat_range[1]), floor(lat_range[2]), by = 1)

# ---- Plot ----
p_fig1_map <- ggplot() +
  geom_polygon(
    data = mi,
    aes(x = long, y = lat, group = group),
    fill = "grey92",
    color = "grey40",
    linewidth = 0.3
  ) +
  geom_point(
    data = df_counts,
    aes(x = lon, y = lat, size = years_tested),
    shape = 21,
    fill = "grey60",
    color = "black",
    alpha = 0.7,
    stroke = 0.3
  ) +
  scale_size_continuous(
    range = c(2, 7),
    breaks = size_breaks,
    guide = guide_legend(
      title = "Years\nconducted\nat each\nlocation",
      override.aes = list(alpha = 1)
    )
  ) +
  coord_quickmap(xlim = lon_range, ylim = lat_range) +
  labs(
    title = "SDHI seed treatment trial locations (2017–2024)",
    x = "Longitude (°W)",
    y = "Latitude (°N)"
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = lon_breaks,
    labels = function(x) paste0(abs(x), "°")
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    breaks = lat_breaks,
    labels = function(y) paste0(y, "°")
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    legend.title.align = 0.5,
    legend.title = element_text(size = 10, face = "bold"),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 8),
    legend.position = "right",
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA, linewidth = 0.5),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0)
  )

p_fig1_map

ggsave(
  "Figure_1_trial_locations_MI.png",
  p_fig1_map,
  width = 7,
  height = 4,
  dpi = 600
)
```


# Source module: Longitudinal_PCA

<!-- Imported and lightly cleaned from `Longitudinal_PCA.Rmd`. -->

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
# setwd("C:/Longitudinal")  # Local path removed for GitHub portability #sets working directory for entire markdown
knitr::opts_chunk$set(include = FALSE)
```

### Start Clean
```{r eval = TRUE, echo = FALSE, results = 'hide'}
rm(list = ls(all=TRUE)) # removes all variables in the global environment so you start fresh

options(scipen = 999) # stops anything from being in scientific notation
```

### Load dependencies, and define some basic functions
```{r load dependencies, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# Removed 36 redundant package-loading/install line(s); packages are loaded in the setup section.
#tidyverse/miscellaneous packages
#library("plyr")
#library("latticeExtra")

#visualization/ggplot packages

#PCA packages

#mapping packages
#library("purrr")
#library("terra")

#statistical analysis packages

#trims leading and trailing spaces
trim <- function (x) gsub("^\\s+|\\s+$", "", x)

#converts factors to numeric values
as.numeric.factor<-function(x) {as.numeric(levels(x))[x]}

#creates standard error function from sd and sqrt of number of observations
### fixed by RH on 2/6/2025
se<-function(x) sd((as.numeric(x,na.rm=T))/sqrt(length(x)),na.rm=T)

conflicts_prefer(
  dplyr::summarise,   # not ggplot2::summarise
  dplyr::summarize,   # optional alias
  dplyr::filter,
  dplyr::select,
  dplyr::mutate,
  dplyr::rename,
  
  factoextra::fviz_pca_ind
)
```

### cool, so let's PCA, etc. -- this chunk sets up the PCA and produces figures related to SCN levels (hab = scn_level)
```{r echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}

raw <- read_csv("C:/Longitudinal/full2017_2024_pca.csv") |> clean_names()

# quick look
skimr::skim(raw)

raw<-raw %>% mutate(scn_level = case_when(
      scn_count_rep == 0 ~ "none",
      scn_count_rep > 0 & scn_count_rep < 2000 ~ "low",
      scn_count_rep >= 2000 ~ "high",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_   # catch unexpected or missing values
    ),
    scn_level = factor(scn_level, levels = c("none", "low", "high"))
  )

#Ensure key metadata are present; coerce to factors
df <- raw %>% mutate(
  year = factor(year),
  location = factor(location),
  mean_fv_ratio_int = factor(mean_fv_ratio_int, ordered = TRUE),
  cultivar = factor(cultivar),
  sds_tol = factor(sds_tol),
  product = factor(product),
  rep = factor(rep),
  fdx_level = factor(fdx_level),
  volatility = factor(volatility)
)

#Choose numeric features for PCA/FA ----------------------------------
#Keep all numeric columns as candidates, but exclude obvious IDs if you prefer.
id_like <- c("soil_p_h","yield_mtha","yield_bua","yield_diff_bua","proj_price_kg","proj_price_bu","harv_price_kg","harv_price_bu","diff_price_kgha","diff_price_bua")  # adjust as needed
meta_cols <- c("year","location","mean_fv_ratio_int","fdx_level","scn_level","product")  # keep for plotting/stratification


num_df <- df |>
  # select all numeric columns first
  select(where(is.numeric)) |>
  # drop ID-like columns
  select(-any_of(intersect(names(df), id_like)))

# Build a common complete-case mask across NUMERICS + META, then filter both
keep <- complete.cases(
  df[, c(meta_cols)],
  df %>% select(where(is.numeric))
)

df_nona  <- df[keep, , drop = FALSE]
  #df_nona <- dplyr::rename(df_nona, treatment_effect_kgha = yield_diff)

num_df_nona <- num_df %>% select(where(is.numeric)) %>% .[keep, , drop = FALSE]
  #num_df_nona <- dplyr::rename(num_df_nona, treatment_effect_kgha = yield_diff)

# ---- 3) Preprocess (median impute + z-score scale) via recipes ---------------
pca_rec <- recipe(~ ., data = num_df_nona) |>
  step_impute_median(all_numeric_predictors()) |>
  step_normalize(all_numeric_predictors()) |>
  step_pca(all_numeric_predictors(), id = "pca")

#Prep and bake
pca_prep <- prep(pca_rec)
pca_scores <- bake(pca_prep, new_data = NULL)

# Add back metadata for plotting
pca_scores <- pca_scores |>
bind_cols(df_nona |> dplyr::select(all_of(meta_cols)))

#PCA diagnostics: variance explained & loadings -----------------------
# Percent variance by component
pca_obj <- tidy(pca_prep, id = "pca", type = "variance")
pca_variance <- pca_obj |> filter(terms == "percent variance")

# --- Robust extraction of PCA loadings from the prepped recipe ----------------
# Find the step_pca within the recipe
pca_idx  <- which(vapply(pca_prep$steps, inherits, logical(1), "step_pca"))
stopifnot(length(pca_idx) == 1)
pca_step <- pca_prep$steps[[pca_idx]]

# after prep(...)
pca_obj_var <- broom::tidy(pca_prep, id = "pca", type = "variance")
pca_var_tbl <- dplyr::filter(pca_obj_var, terms == "percent variance")

#loadings (rotation) as tidy tibble
pca_loadings <- broom::tidy(pca_prep, id = "pca", type = "coef") %>%
  # columns: terms (variable), value (loading), component (PC)
  dplyr::rename(variable = terms, loading = value) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(component, dplyr::desc(abs(loading)))

dplyr::glimpse(pca_loadings)

# pca_loadings may have (variable, loading) OR (terms, value)
var_col <- if ("variable" %in% names(pca_loadings)) "variable" else "terms"
val_col <- if ("loading"  %in% names(pca_loadings)) "loading"  else "value"

pca_loadings_std <- pca_loadings %>%
  dplyr::rename(variable = !!rlang::sym(var_col),
                loading  = !!rlang::sym(val_col))

#Scree plot
scree_plot<-ggplot(pca_variance, aes(x = as.integer(component), y = value)) +
  geom_col() +
  geom_point() +
  geom_line() +
  labs(x = "Principal Component", y = "Percent variance explained",
       title = "PCA Scree Plot") +
  theme_minimal()
  ggsave("scree_plot.png", plot = scree_plot,
                width = 8, height = 6, units = "in", dpi = 300)  

# Variable loading plot for first two PCs
top_n <- 9
pca_loadings_pc9_top <- pca_loadings_std %>%
  dplyr::filter(component %in% c("PC1","PC2")) %>%
  dplyr::group_by(component) %>%
  dplyr::slice_max(order_by = abs(loading), n = top_n, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
  dplyr::ungroup()

var_loading<-ggplot2::ggplot(pca_loadings_pc9_top,
  ggplot2::aes(x = reorder(variable, abs(loading)),
               y = loading, fill = loading)) +
  ggplot2::geom_col() +
  ggplot2::scale_fill_gradient2(name = "Loading") +
  ggplot2::facet_wrap(~ component, scales = "free_y") +
  ggplot2::coord_flip() +
  ggplot2::labs(x = "Variable", y = "Loading",
                title = paste0("Top ", top_n, " loadings for PC1 & PC2")) +
  ggplot2::theme_minimal()
  ggsave("variable_loading.png", plot = var_loading,
                width = 10, height = 6, units = "in", dpi = 300)  

```

### SHINY? SHINY!!! ###
```{r message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, include=FALSE}
# Removed 8 redundant package-loading/install line(s); packages are loaded in the setup section.

## ---------- libraries ----------

## IMPORTANT: in your session you must have:
## pr      = your prcomp object
## df_nona = your metadata (same row order as pr$x)
#Robust PCA plots using preprocessed matrix ---------------------------------
#Build a preprocessing recipe to impute + scale the numeric features you chose in num_df
        preproc <- recipes::recipe(~ ., data = num_df_nona) %>%
          recipes::step_impute_median(recipes::all_numeric_predictors()) %>%
          recipes::step_zv(recipes::all_numeric_predictors()) %>%
          recipes::step_nzv(recipes::all_numeric_predictors()) %>%
          recipes::step_normalize(recipes::all_numeric_predictors()) %>%
          recipes::prep()
        
        # (2) Extract the clean numeric matrix for PCA
        X <- recipes::juice(preproc, composition = "data.frame")
        stopifnot(nrow(X) > 0, ncol(X) > 1)
        
        # (3) Run PCA on the already scaled matrix (no need to center/scale again)
        pr <- prcomp(as.matrix(X), center = FALSE, scale. = FALSE, retx = TRUE)

## IMPORTANT: in your session you must have
##   pr      = prcomp object
##   df_nona = metadata with same row order as pr$x

## ---------- helper: journal style presets ----------
apply_style_preset <- function(p, preset = "Default") {
  base <- theme(
    text = element_text(family = "", colour = "black"),
    panel.grid = element_blank()
  )

  if (preset == "Plant Disease") {
    p + base +
      theme(
        panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = "black", linewidth = 0.6),
        axis.line = element_line(colour = "black", linewidth = 0.6),
        axis.ticks = element_line(colour = "black", linewidth = 0.6),
        axis.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
        axis.text  = element_text(size = 10),
        legend.key = element_blank(),
        legend.background = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA),
        legend.title = element_text(size = 11, face = "bold"),
        legend.text  = element_text(size = 9),
        plot.title = element_text(size = 13, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5)
      )
  } else if (preset == "Phytopathology") {
    p + base +
      theme(
        panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = "black", linewidth = 0.5),
        axis.line = element_line(colour = "black", linewidth = 0.5),
        axis.ticks = element_line(colour = "black", linewidth = 0.5),
        axis.title = element_text(size = 11, face = "bold"),
        axis.text  = element_text(size = 9),
        legend.key = element_blank(),
        legend.background = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA),
        legend.title = element_text(size = 10, face = "bold"),
        legend.text  = element_text(size = 9),
        plot.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5)
      )
  } else if (preset == "Crop Protection") {
    p + base +
      theme(
        panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = "grey40", linewidth = 0.4),
        axis.line = element_line(colour = "black", linewidth = 0.5),
        axis.ticks = element_line(colour = "black", linewidth = 0.5),
        axis.title = element_text(size = 11, face = "bold"),
        axis.text  = element_text(size = 9),
        legend.key = element_blank(),
        legend.background = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA),
        legend.title = element_text(size = 10, face = "bold"),
        legend.text  = element_text(size = 9),
        plot.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5)
      )
  } else if (preset == "Nature-style") {
    p + base +
      theme(
        panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA),
        axis.line = element_line(colour = "black", linewidth = 0.6),
        axis.ticks = element_line(colour = "black", linewidth = 0.6),
        axis.title = element_text(size = 11, face = "plain"),
        axis.text  = element_text(size = 9),
        legend.key = element_blank(),
        legend.background = element_rect(fill = "white", colour = NA),
        legend.title = element_text(size = 9, face = "plain"),
        legend.text  = element_text(size = 8),
        plot.title = element_text(size = 11, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5)
      )
  } else {
    # Default: minimal-ish but not too bare
    p + theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
      theme(
        panel.grid.major = element_line(colour = "grey85", linewidth = 0.3),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        axis.title = element_text(size = 11, face = "bold"),
        axis.text  = element_text(size = 9),
        plot.title = element_text(size = 13, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5)
      )
  }
}

## ---------- helper: static PCA biplot for publication ----------
pca_biplot_static <- function(pr,
                              df_meta,
                              x_pc = 1,
                              y_pc = 2,
                              group_var = "scn_level",
                              show_ellipses = TRUE,
                              show_arrows   = TRUE,
                              ellipse_type  = "t",
                              palette       = NULL,
                              title         = NULL) {

  scores <- as.data.frame(pr$x)
  colnames(scores) <- paste0("PC", seq_len(ncol(scores)))
  dat <- scores %>% bind_cols(df_meta)

  x_name <- paste0("PC", x_pc)
  y_name <- paste0("PC", y_pc)

  var_pct <- round(100 * (pr$sdev^2 / sum(pr$sdev^2)), 1)
  xlab <- paste0("PC", x_pc, " (", var_pct[x_pc], "%)")
  ylab <- paste0("PC", y_pc, " (", var_pct[y_pc], "%)")

  # grouping
  if (!is.null(group_var) && group_var != "none" && group_var %in% names(dat)) {
    hab <- forcats::fct_explicit_na(dat[[group_var]], na_level = "NA")
    dat$hab <- hab
    if (is.null(palette)) {
      palette <- hcl.colors(nlevels(hab), "Dynamic")
    } else if (length(palette) < nlevels(hab)) {
      palette <- rep(palette, length.out = nlevels(hab))
    }
    pal_named <- setNames(palette, levels(hab))
    grp_label <- group_var
  } else {
    dat$hab <- NULL
    pal_named <- NULL
    grp_label <- "none"
    show_ellipses <- FALSE
  }

  # loadings for these PCs
  loadings <- as.data.frame(pr$rotation[, c(x_pc, y_pc), drop = FALSE]) %>%
    rownames_to_column("variable") %>%
    rename(lx = 2, ly = 3)

  range_x <- range(dat[[x_name]], na.rm = TRUE)
  range_y <- range(dat[[y_name]], na.rm = TRUE)
  scale_factor <- 0.7 * min(diff(range_x), diff(range_y))
  max_loading  <- max(abs(c(loadings$lx, loadings$ly)))
  loadings <- loadings %>%
    mutate(
      ax = lx / max_loading * scale_factor,
      ay = ly / max_loading * scale_factor
    )

  # base plot
  if (!is.null(dat$hab)) {
    p <- ggplot(dat, aes(x = .data[[x_name]], y = .data[[y_name]], color = hab)) +
      geom_point(size = 2.3, alpha = 0.85) +
      scale_color_manual(values = pal_named, name = grp_label)
  } else {
    p <- ggplot(dat, aes(x = .data[[x_name]], y = .data[[y_name]])) +
      geom_point(size = 2.3, alpha = 0.85, color = "black")
  }

  if (!is.null(dat$hab) && isTRUE(show_ellipses)) {
    p <- p +
      stat_ellipse(aes(group = hab, color = hab),
                   type = ellipse_type,
                   level = 0.95,
                   linewidth = 0.7,
                   show.legend = FALSE)
  }

  if (isTRUE(show_arrows)) {
    p <- p +
      geom_segment(
        data = loadings,
        aes(x = 0, y = 0, xend = ax, yend = ay),
        inherit.aes = FALSE,
        arrow = arrow(length = unit(0.18, "cm")),
        color = "black", linewidth = 0.3
      ) +
      geom_text(
        data = loadings,
        aes(x = ax, y = ay, label = variable),
        inherit.aes = FALSE,
        size = 3,
        vjust = 1.1
      )
  }

  if (is.null(title)) {
    title <- glue("PCA Biplot: PC{x_pc} vs PC{y_pc} (grouped by {grp_label})")
  }

  p +
    coord_equal() +
    theme_minimal() +   # base; style preset will refine
    labs(title = title, x = xlab, y = ylab)
}

## ---------- UI ----------
pc_choices <- paste0("PC", 1:8)  # adjust max PC as needed
group_choices <- c(
  "No grouping"           = "none",
  "F. virguliforme level" = "mean_fv_ratio_int",
  "SCN level"             = "scn_level",
  "Year"                  = "year",
  "Location"              = "location",
  "Product"               = "product"
)

style_choices <- c(
  "Default",
  "Plant Disease",
  "Phytopathology",
  "Crop Protection",
  "Nature-style"
)

ui <- fluidPage(
  titlePanel("Interactive PCA Explorer + Publication Figure"),
  sidebarLayout(
    sidebarPanel(
      h4("Interactive PCA (PC1 vs PC2)"),
      selectInput(
        "group_var", "Color / group by (interactive):",
        choices = group_choices,
        selected = "scn_level"
      ),
      checkboxInput("show_ellipses", "Show ellipses (interactive)", value = TRUE),
      selectInput("ellipse_type", "Ellipse type:",
                  choices = c("Normal" = "norm", "t-distribution" = "t"),
                  selected = "t"),
      sliderInput("alpha", "Point alpha:", min = 0.2, max = 1, value = 0.8, step = 0.05),
      sliderInput("point_size", "Point size:", min = 1.5, max = 4, value = 2.5, step = 0.25),

      hr(),
      h4("Publication figure (4 panels)"),
      selectInput("style_preset", "Style preset:",
                  choices = style_choices,
                  selected = "Plant Disease"),   # <-- default to Plant Disease
      checkboxInput("show_pub_arrows", "Show arrows in pub panels", value = TRUE),
      checkboxInput("lock_pub_axes", "Lock axes across panels", value = TRUE),

      h5("Panel A"),
      selectInput("pub_A_group", "Group by:", choices = group_choices, selected = "scn_level"),
      selectInput("pub_A_x", "X PC:", choices = pc_choices, selected = "PC1"),
      selectInput("pub_A_y", "Y PC:", choices = pc_choices, selected = "PC2"),

      h5("Panel B"),
      selectInput("pub_B_group", "Group by:", choices = group_choices, selected = "mean_fv_ratio_int"),
      selectInput("pub_B_x", "X PC:", choices = pc_choices, selected = "PC1"),
      selectInput("pub_B_y", "Y PC:", choices = pc_choices, selected = "PC2"),

      h5("Panel C"),
      selectInput("pub_C_group", "Group by:", choices = group_choices, selected = "scn_level"),
      selectInput("pub_C_x", "X PC:", choices = pc_choices, selected = "PC1"),
      selectInput("pub_C_y", "Y PC:", choices = pc_choices, selected = "PC3"),

      h5("Panel D"),
      selectInput("pub_D_group", "Group by:", choices = group_choices, selected = "scn_level"),
      selectInput("pub_D_x", "X PC:", choices = pc_choices, selected = "PC2"),
      selectInput("pub_D_y", "Y PC:", choices = pc_choices, selected = "PC3"),

      hr(),
      textInput("pub_obj_name", "Save pub figure as object:", "pca_pub_figure"),
      actionButton("save_pub_to_env", "Export to ggplot object"),
      br(), br(),
      downloadButton("download_pub", "Download publication figure")
    ),
    mainPanel(
      tabsetPanel(
        tabPanel("Interactive PCA",
                 plotlyOutput("pcaPlot", height = "650px")),
        tabPanel("Publication figure",
                 plotOutput("pubFigure", height = "650px"))
      )
    )
  )
)

## ---------- SERVER ----------
server <- function(input, output, session) {

  # ---- Interactive PCA data (PC1/PC2) ----
  pca_data <- reactive({
    scores <- as.data.frame(pr$x)[, 1:2, drop = FALSE]
    scores <- scores %>%
      rename(PC1 = 1, PC2 = 2) %>%
      bind_cols(df_nona)
    scores
  })

  # ---- Interactive PCA plot (plotly) ----
  output$pcaPlot <- renderPlotly({
    dat <- pca_data()

    if (input$group_var == "none") {
      hab <- NULL
      title_suffix <- "No grouping"
    } else {
      hab <- fct_explicit_na(dat[[input$group_var]], na_level = "NA")
      title_suffix <- paste("colored by", input$group_var)
    }

    pc_var <- round(summary(pr)$importance[2, 1:2] * 100, 1)
    xlab <- paste0("PC1 (", pc_var[1], "%)")
    ylab <- paste0("PC2 (", pc_var[2], "%)")

    if (is.null(hab)) {
      g <- ggplot(dat, aes(x = PC1, y = PC2)) +
        geom_point(alpha = input$alpha, size = input$point_size) +
        coord_equal()
    } else {
      pal <- hcl.colors(nlevels(hab), "Dynamic")

      g <- ggplot(dat, aes(x = PC1, y = PC2, color = hab)) +
        geom_point(alpha = input$alpha, size = input$point_size) +
        scale_color_manual(values = pal, name = NULL) +
        coord_equal()

      if (isTRUE(input$show_ellipses)) {
        g <- g +
          stat_ellipse(
            aes(group = hab),
            type = input$ellipse_type,
            level = 0.95,
            linewidth = 0.7,
            show.legend = FALSE
          )
      }
    }

    g <- g +
      labs(
        title = paste("PCA (PC1 vs PC2) –", title_suffix),
        x = xlab,
        y = ylab
      )

    # Apply style preset to interactive plot too
    g <- apply_style_preset(g, input$style_preset)

    tooltip_vars <- c("PC1", "PC2", input$group_var)
    if ("location" %in% names(dat)) tooltip_vars <- c(tooltip_vars, "location")
    if ("year" %in% names(dat))     tooltip_vars <- c(tooltip_vars, "year")

    ggplotly(g, tooltip = intersect(tooltip_vars, names(dat)))
  })

  # ---- Publication figure (4 panels, patchwork) ----
  pub_figure_reactive <- reactive({
    pc_num <- function(pc) as.integer(sub("PC", "", pc))

    pal_scn <- c("none" = "#66A61E", "low" = "#E6AB02", "high" = "#D95F02")

    pA <- pca_biplot_static(
      pr,
      df_meta = df_nona,
      x_pc    = pc_num(input$pub_A_x),
      y_pc    = pc_num(input$pub_A_y),
      group_var     = input$pub_A_group,
      show_ellipses = TRUE,
      show_arrows   = input$show_pub_arrows,
      ellipse_type  = input$ellipse_type,
      palette       = if (input$pub_A_group == "scn_level") pal_scn else NULL,
      title         = glue("Panel A: {input$pub_A_x} vs {input$pub_A_y} – {input$pub_A_group}")
    )

    pB <- pca_biplot_static(
      pr,
      df_meta = df_nona,
      x_pc    = pc_num(input$pub_B_x),
      y_pc    = pc_num(input$pub_B_y),
      group_var     = input$pub_B_group,
      show_ellipses = TRUE,
      show_arrows   = input$show_pub_arrows,
      ellipse_type  = input$ellipse_type,
      palette       = if (input$pub_B_group == "scn_level") pal_scn else NULL,
      title         = glue("Panel B: {input$pub_B_x} vs {input$pub_B_y} – {input$pub_B_group}")
    )

    pC <- pca_biplot_static(
      pr,
      df_meta = df_nona,
      x_pc    = pc_num(input$pub_C_x),
      y_pc    = pc_num(input$pub_C_y),
      group_var     = input$pub_C_group,
      show_ellipses = TRUE,
      show_arrows   = input$show_pub_arrows,
      ellipse_type  = input$ellipse_type,
      palette       = if (input$pub_C_group == "scn_level") pal_scn else NULL,
      title         = glue("Panel C: {input$pub_C_x} vs {input$pub_C_y} – {input$pub_C_group}")
    )

    pD <- pca_biplot_static(
      pr,
      df_meta = df_nona,
      x_pc    = pc_num(input$pub_D_x),
      y_pc    = pc_num(input$pub_D_y),
      group_var     = input$pub_D_group,
      show_ellipses = TRUE,
      show_arrows   = input$show_pub_arrows,
      ellipse_type  = input$ellipse_type,
      palette       = if (input$pub_D_group == "scn_level") pal_scn else NULL,
      title         = glue("Panel D: {input$pub_D_x} vs {input$pub_D_y} – {input$pub_D_group}")
    )

    if (isTRUE(input$lock_pub_axes)) {
      builds <- lapply(list(pA, pB, pC, pD), ggplot_build)
      x_all <- range(unlist(lapply(builds, function(b) b$layout$panel_params[[1]]$x.range)))
      y_all <- range(unlist(lapply(builds, function(b) b$layout$panel_params[[1]]$y.range)))

      pA <- pA + coord_equal(xlim = x_all, ylim = y_all)
      pB <- pB + coord_equal(xlim = x_all, ylim = y_all)
      pC <- pC + coord_equal(xlim = x_all, ylim = y_all)
      pD <- pD + coord_equal(xlim = x_all, ylim = y_all)
    }

    combined <- (pA | pB) /
                (pC | pD) +
      plot_layout(guides = "collect")

    combined_tagged <- combined +
      plot_annotation(
        tag_levels = "A",
        tag_suffix = "."
      )

    # Apply style preset to whole combined figure
    combined_tagged <- apply_style_preset(combined_tagged, input$style_preset)

    combined_tagged
  })

  output$pubFigure <- renderPlot({
    pub_figure_reactive()
  })

  # ---- Export publication figure as ggplot object in .GlobalEnv ----
  observeEvent(input$save_pub_to_env, {
    obj_name <- input$pub_obj_name
    if (is.null(obj_name) || !nzchar(obj_name)) {
      obj_name <- "pca_pub_figure"
    }
    assign(obj_name, pub_figure_reactive(), envir = .GlobalEnv)
    showNotification(
      paste("Saved publication figure as object:", obj_name),
      type = "message"
    )
  })

  # ---- Download high-res PNG ----
  output$download_pub <- downloadHandler(
    filename = function() {
      paste0("PCA_publication_figure_", Sys.Date(), ".png")
    },
    content = function(file) {
      ggsave(
        filename = file,
        plot     = pub_figure_reactive(),
        width    = 8,
        height   = 8,
        units    = "in",
        dpi      = 600
      )
    }
  )
}

## ---------- RUN APP ----------
shinyApp(ui, server)

```

### Correlation analysis and figure
```{r}

cor.mat <- cor(num_df_nona, use = "pairwise.complete.obs") #creates correlation matrix
    cor.mat <- cor.mat[rowSums(is.finite(cor.mat)) == ncol(cor.mat),
    colSums(is.finite(cor.mat)) == nrow(cor.mat), drop = FALSE]
    head(cor.mat[, 1:4]) #view top 6 variables
    cor_plot<-corrplot(cor.mat, type="upper", order="hclust", tl.col="black", tl.srt=45) #plots the correlation matrix
    png("correlation_matrix.png", width = 2000, height = 2000, res = 300)
    corrplot(cor.mat,type = "upper",order = "hclust",tl.col = "black",tl.srt = 45)
    dev.off()  # VERY important — closes and writes the file

```
### K-means analysis
```{r}
# ---------- K-means (reproducible) ----------
set.seed(1)
conflicts_prefer(rstatix::desc)
# Matrix of features for clustering (already scaled PCs)
pca_mat <- pr$x[, 1:5]  # or your existing pca_mat
stopifnot(is.matrix(pca_mat))

# Choose a reasonable K search range
K.max <- 12
K.seq.elbow <- 1:K.max
K.seq.sil   <- 2:K.max

# Compute a single distance object to reuse for silhouette
D <- dist(pca_mat)

# --- Elbow: total within-cluster SS ---
wss <- map_dbl(K.seq.elbow, ~ kmeans(pca_mat, centers = .x, nstart = 25)$tot.withinss)
df_elbow <- tibble(k = K.seq.elbow, wss = wss)

# Heuristic elbow picker: second derivative "knee"
dd <- diff(diff(wss))
k_elbow <- which.min(dd) + 1     # +1 because diff shrinks length by 1 twice

# --- Silhouette: average silhouette width ---
sil_df <- map_df(K.seq.sil, function(k) {
  km <- kmeans(pca_mat, centers = k, nstart = 25)
  ss <- silhouette(km$cluster, D)
  tibble(k = k, avg_sil = mean(ss[, "sil_width"]))
})
k_sil <- sil_df$k[which.max(sil_df$avg_sil)]

# --- Gap statistic ---
set.seed(1)
gap <- clusGap(pca_mat, FUN = kmeans, K.max = K.max, B = 50)
# First local maximum of the gap within 1*SE (Tibshirani+)
k_gap <- maxSE(gap$Tab[,"gap"], gap$Tab[,"SE.sim"], method = "Tibs2001SEmax")
# (k returned as index in 1:K.max; convert if needed)
k_gap <- as.integer(k_gap)

# --- Simple consensus rule ---
# Majority vote; if tie, prefer silhouette; if still tie, prefer smaller k
votes <- c(k_elbow, k_sil, k_gap)
k_best <- as.integer(names(sort(table(votes), decreasing = TRUE))[1])
if (sum(votes == k_best) == 1) {
  # tie between three different or all different -> pick silhouette’s
  k_best <- k_sil
}

cat(sprintf("Suggested k values — Elbow: %d, Silhouette: %d, Gap: %d\n", k_elbow, k_sil, k_gap))
cat(sprintf(">> Chosen k_best: %d\n", k_best))

# --- Plots ---
p_elbow <- ggplot(df_elbow, aes(k, wss)) +
  geom_line() + geom_point(size = 2) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = k_elbow, linetype = 2) +
  labs(title = "Elbow (WSS)", x = "k", y = "Total within-cluster SS") +
  theme_minimal()

p_sil <- ggplot(sil_df, aes(k, avg_sil)) +
  geom_line() + geom_point(size = 2) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = k_sil, linetype = 2) +
  labs(title = "Average Silhouette", x = "k", y = "Avg silhouette width") +
  theme_minimal()

p_gap <- fviz_gap_stat(gap) +
  ggplot2::geom_vline(xintercept = k_gap, linetype = 2) +
  ggplot2::ggtitle("Gap Statistic")

k_means_combiplot<-(p_elbow | p_sil) / p_gap +
  plot_annotation(title = paste0("Optimal k diagnostics (k_best = ", k_best, ")"))
ggplot2::ggsave("k_means_diagnostics.png", plot = k_means_combiplot, width =10, height = 8, units = "in", dpi = 300)

# Use k_best downstream:
# km <- kmeans(pca_mat, centers = k_best, nstart = 50)

# Choose k (set after viewing elbow/silhouette)
k <- 11
km <- kmeans(pca_mat, centers = k, nstart = 25)
pca_scores$cluster <- factor(km$cluster)

# Plot clusters
loc_clusters<-ggplot(pca_scores, aes(PC1, PC2, color = location)) +
  geom_point(size = 2.5, alpha = 0.85) +
  labs(title = paste("k-means (k =", k, ") on PCA scores"),
       color = "Location") +
  theme_minimal()
  ggsave("loc_clusters.png", plot = loc_clusters + coord_equal(),
                width = 8, height = 8, units = "in", dpi = 300)
# Plot clusters
scn_clusters<-ggplot(pca_scores, aes(PC1, PC2, color = scn_level)) +
  geom_point(size = 2.5, alpha = 0.85) +
  labs(title = paste("k-means (k =", k, ") on PCA scores"),
       color = "SCN level") +
  theme_minimal()
  ggsave("scn_clusters.png", plot = scn_clusters + coord_equal(),
                width = 8, height = 8, units = "in", dpi = 300)
  
# Cluster composition by location and scn level
pca_scores_location<-pca_scores |>
  dplyr::count(cluster, location) |>
  group_by(cluster) |>
  mutate(pct = 100 * n / sum(n)) |>
  arrange(cluster, desc(pct)) |>
  print(n = 100)

pca_scores_scn_level<-pca_scores |>
  dplyr::count(cluster, scn_level) |>
  group_by(cluster) |>
  mutate(pct = 100 * n / sum(n)) |>
  arrange(cluster, desc(pct)) |>
  print(n = 100)

#Write to disk if desired:
write_csv(pca_variance,"pca_variance.csv")
write_csv(pca_loadings,"pca_loadings.csv")
write_csv(pca_scores_location,"pca_scores_location.csv")
write_csv(pca_scores_scn_level,"pca_scores_scn_level.csv")
```



# Source module: seed_trt_2017_2024_old

<!-- Imported and lightly cleaned from `seed_trt_2017_2024_old.Rmd`. -->

### 1) set working directory, load dependencies, and define some basic functions
```{r load dependencies, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, include=FALSE}
# Removed 34 redundant package-loading/install line(s); packages are loaded in the setup section.

#sets working directory
#not sure why this line is being difficult, but whatevs... be that way
# setwd("E:/Soy Seed Treatment Analysis")  # Local path removed for GitHub portability

#sets the openAI API key as a global variable... because money
# [REDACTED] Removed local API-key/environment variable line.

#converts numbers in scientific notation to numeric format
options(digits = 3,scipen = 999, max.print = 500)

#tidyverse/miscellaneous packages

#visualization/ggplot packages

#enables chatGPT

#statistical analysis packages

#trims leading and trailing spaces
trim <- function (x) gsub("^\\s+|\\s+$", "", x)

#converts factors to numeric values
as.numeric.factor <- function(x) {as.numeric(levels(x))[x]}

#creates standard error function from sd and sqrt of number of observations
se <- function(x) sd(x)/sqrt(length(x))

```

### 2) Let's look at the data
```{r message=FALSE}
full2017_2024<-read.csv(file = "full2017_2024.csv",header=T)

  #parses categorical variables as factors
  cols=c(1:7)
  full2017_2024[cols]<-lapply(full2017_2024[cols],as.factor)
  
  #and parses continuous variables as numbers 
  cols2=c(8:11)
  full2017_2024[cols2]<-lapply(full2017_2024[cols2],as.numeric)

  str(full2017_2024) #looks good

attach(full2017_2024)

  #plots variables to get a feel for distribution
    plot(product,yield_bua)
    boxplot(yield_bua,na.rm=T)
    boxplot(fdx,na.rm=T)
    boxplot(scn_count_rep,na.rm=T)
    
    hist(yield_bua)
    hist(fdx)
    hist(scn_count_rep)
    hist(yield_diff)
    barplot(yield_diff)

#sets up a function to preform shapiro-wilk test for normality
perform_shapiro_test <- function(variable) {
  result <- shapiro.test(variable)
  return(result$p.value)
}

#nothing is normally distributed, but what really matters are the residuals
perform_shapiro_test(yield_bua)
perform_shapiro_test(fdx)
perform_shapiro_test(scn_count_rep)

#detach(full2017_2024) #removes the dataframe from local environment 
```


### 3) summarizing data
```{r message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}

#let's produce a summary by seed treatment product
prod_sum<-ddply(full2017_2024, .(product), summarize, mean_yield=mean(as.numeric(yield_bua),na.rm=T), se_yield=sd(as.numeric(yield_bua)/sqrt((length(yield_bua))),na.rm=T))

#let's produce a summary by year by seed treatment product
year_sum<-ddply(full2017_2024, .(year,product), summarize,mean_fdx=mean(as.numeric(fdx),na.rm=T), mean_yield=mean(as.numeric(yield_bua),na.rm=T), se_yield=sd(as.numeric(yield_bua)/sqrt((length(yield_bua))),na.rm=T),mean_diff=mean(as.numeric(yield_diff),na.rm=T), se_diff=sd(as.numeric(yield_diff)/sqrt((length(yield_diff))),na.rm=T))

  plot(year_sum$mean_fdx,year_sum$mean_yield_diff)

#let's produce a summary by location by seed treatment product
loc_sum<-ddply(full2017_2024, .(year,location,product), summarize,mean_fdx=mean(as.numeric(fdx),na.rm=T),se_fdx=sd(as.numeric(fdx)/sqrt((length(fdx))),na.rm=T), mean_yield=mean(as.numeric(yield_bua),na.rm=T), se_yield=sd(as.numeric(yield_bua)/sqrt((length(yield_bua))),na.rm=T))
```

### 4) modeling for type III ANOVA
```{r}
df_lm<-full2017_2024 #new df for modelling
df_lm<-subset(df_lm,select=-c(fdx,scn_count_rep,soil_pH)) #strips unneeded columns
#names(df_lm)
#sort(sapply(df_lm, function(x) sum(is.na(x))),decreasing=T)
df_lm<-df_lm%>%filter(!is.na(yield_bua)) #strips NAs
df_lm<-df_lm%>%filter(!is.na(yield_diff))
str(df_lm) #looks good

###UMMMMM... let's strip out the lines with unknown resistance levels
df_lm<-df_lm[!grepl("unknown",df_lm$cultivar),]

### Ok, since Saltro was only included since 2020, we need to partition and model ILEVO and Saltro responses separately, or together if used in the same year(s)

  #ALSO -- these reduced dfs will be nice for piano plots
    #strip out the base trt lines and plot the relative yield differences by trt

df_ilevo<-df_lm[!grepl("Saltro",df_lm$product),] #selects only treatments: base and ilevo
df_both<-df_lm[grepl("2020|2021|2022|2023",df_lm$year),] #selects only years with both treatments: saltro and ilevo
df_saltro<-df_both[!grepl("ILEVO",df_both$product),] #selects only treatments: base and saltro

### use this model to examine within-site differences by product and sds_tol levels:
df_dcf<-df_lm[grepl("DECATUR",df_lm$location),] 
lmod_dcf<-lmer(yield_bua~product+sds_tol+(1|year),data=df_dcf)
Anova(lmod_dcf,type=3)
dcf_prod<-lsmeans(lmod_dcf,c("sds_tol","product"))
cld(dcf_prod,by=c("sds_tol"),sort=F,Letters=letters,response=T,alpha=.05)

lmod<-lmer(yield_bua~product+(1|year:location)+(1|sds_tol),data=df_ilevo)
plot(lmod)
(drop1(lmod, test = "Chisq"))
joint_tests(lmod)
summary(lmod) #not great model fit across all sites 
#boxcox(lmod) #lambda estimate = ~1.5)
shapiro.test(resid(lmod)) #not normally distributed, but not really that bad tbh
(var.test(yield_bua~product,data=df_ilevo)) #both product and sds_tol have homogeneous variance with yield_bua, so type I ANOVA could be ok *within sites*

loc_prod<-lsmeans(lmod,"product")
cld(loc_prod,by="product",sort=F,Letters=letters,response=T,alpha=.05)
#plot(yield_prod_year)

    #converts fdx and SCN count zeroes to some tiny number -- only use if                  log/sqrt/etc. transformation is needed
    #full2020_2024 <- full2020_2024 %>% mutate(across(fdx:scn_count_rep, ~ replace(.,     . == 0.0000 , 0.0001)))

```



# Source module: boxplot_gui

<!-- Imported and lightly cleaned from `boxplot_gui.Rmd`. -->

# 1. Data import and initial cleaning

> 🔧 **Assumption:** Your master dataset is saved as  
> `C:/Longitudinal/full2017_2024.csv`  
> with one row per plot (year × location × treatment × replicate).

Adjust the path if needed.

```{r import, include=FALSE}
raw <- readr::read_csv("C:/Longitudinal/full2017_2024.csv") %>%
  janitor::clean_names()

# Quick skim
#dplyr::glimpse(raw)
```

---

# 2. Derive SCN level and set up factors

We categorize **SCN egg + J2 counts** into three levels:

- `none`: SCN count = 0  
- `low`: 0 < SCN < 2000  
- `high`: SCN ≥ 2000  

We also coerce key design variables into factors for plotting and downstream modelling.

```{r factors, warning=FALSE, include=FALSE}
raw <- raw %>%
  mutate(
    # SCN level factor
    scn_level = case_when(
      scn_count_rep == 0 ~ "none",
      scn_count_rep > 0 & scn_count_rep < 2000 ~ "low",
      scn_count_rep >= 2000 ~ "high",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ),
    scn_level = factor(scn_level, levels = c("none", "low", "high"))
  )

df<- raw %>%
  mutate(
    year              = factor(year),
    location          = factor(location),
    mean_fv_ratio_int = factor(mean_fv_ratio_int, ordered = TRUE),
    cultivar          = factor(cultivar),
    sds_tol           = factor(sds_tol),
    product           = factor(product),
    rep               = factor(rep),
    fdx_level         = factor(fdx_level),
    volatility        = factor(volatility),
    return_USDha      = (yield_kgha*harv_price_kg), #response var that includes market price at harv,
    harv_price_kg     = factor(harv_price_kg),
    diff_price_kgha   = as.numeric(diff_price_kgha),
    fdx_std           = (scale(fdx)) #rescaled fdx values based on mean = 0, sd = 1
  )

#dplyr::glimpse(df)
```

---

```{r include=FALSE}
# Removed 1 redundant package-loading/install line(s); packages are loaded in the setup section.
#optimized cash return model
df_nona<-drop_na(df)
return_mod<-lmer(diff_price_kgha~product+(1|year)+(1|location:rep),data=df_nona,na.action=na.omit)
summary(return_mod)
joint_tests(return_mod) #optimized for modeling seed trt product effects
drop1(return_mod) #looks good
#plot(return_mod)
lsmeans<-emmeans(return_mod,~ product) #calculates lsmeans -- OK, this is actually informative
multcomp::cld(lsmeans,Letters=letters,alpha=.05,ordered=T) #default is Tukey
pairs(lsmeans) #all pairwise comparisons
weights(lsmeans)

(df %>% group_by(mean_fv_ratio_int) %>% 
    dunn_test(return_USDha~product,p.adjust.method = "BH"))
```

---

---

```{r}
# Removed 6 redundant package-loading/install line(s); packages are loaded in the setup section.
## =====================================================================
## Per-FV CLD table + method/effect-size metadata
## - response: return_USDha
## - factor 1 (rows): product
## - factor 2 (cols): mean_fv_ratio_int
## - Per-FV choice of ANOVA+Tukey vs KW+Dunn (BH)
## - Effect sizes: eta2 (ANOVA), eta2[H] (KW)
## - "Robust" ANOVA rule: normality violation allowed if min n > 30
##   and homogeneity OK (Levene p > alpha)
## =====================================================================


## helper: build CLD letters from a pairwise table with group1, group2, p.col
cld_from_contrasts <- function(tbl,
                               group1 = "group1",
                               group2 = "group2",
                               p_col  = "p.adj",
                               alpha  = 0.05) {
  g1 <- as.character(tbl[[group1]])
  g2 <- as.character(tbl[[group2]])
  p  <- tbl[[p_col]]

  lvls <- sort(unique(c(g1, g2)))
  mat  <- matrix(1, nrow = length(lvls), ncol = length(lvls),
                 dimnames = list(lvls, lvls))

  for (i in seq_along(p)) {
    if (is.na(p[i])) next
    i1 <- g1[i]
    i2 <- g2[i]
    mat[i1, i2] <- p[i]
    mat[i2, i1] <- p[i]
  }

  multcompView::multcompLetters(mat, threshold = alpha)$Letters
}

## main builder
build_fv_cld_and_metadata <- function(df,
                                      response_var = "return_USDha",
                                      product_var  = "product",
                                      fv_var       = "mean_fv_ratio_int",
                                      alpha        = 0.05) {

  # basic checks
  stopifnot(
    response_var %in% names(df),
    product_var  %in% names(df),
    fv_var       %in% names(df)
  )

  df <- df %>%
    as_tibble() %>%
    filter(
      !is.na(.data[[response_var]]),
      !is.na(.data[[product_var]]),
      !is.na(.data[[fv_var]])
    )

  # ensure FV is a factor, keep level order
  df[[fv_var]] <- as.factor(df[[fv_var]])
  fv_levels    <- levels(df[[fv_var]])

  # storage
  all_cells   <- list()
  meta_list   <- list()

  # loop over FV levels
  for (fv in fv_levels) {

    dat_i <- df %>%
      filter(.data[[fv_var]] == fv)

    # rename to simple names for tests
    dat_i2 <- dat_i %>%
      transmute(
        prod = .data[[product_var]],
        resp = .data[[response_var]]
      )

    # group sizes
    gcounts <- dat_i2 %>%
      count(prod, name = "n")

    n_groups <- nrow(gcounts)
    n_min    <- min(gcounts$n, na.rm = TRUE)
    n_max    <- max(gcounts$n, na.rm = TRUE)

    # skip if too small
    if (n_groups < 2 || sum(gcounts$n) < 3) {

      # means/se/n (still compute, CLD all "a")
      summ <- dat_i2 %>%
        group_by(prod) %>%
        summarise(
          mean = mean(resp, na.rm = TRUE),
          se   = sd(resp, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(sum(!is.na(resp))),
          n    = sum(!is.na(resp)),
          .groups = "drop"
        )

      summ <- summ %>%
        mutate(
          cld  = "a",
          fv   = fv,
          cell = sprintf("%.1f \u00b1 %.1f%s (n = %d)", mean, se, cld, n)
        )

      all_cells[[fv]] <- summ

      meta_list[[fv]] <- tibble(
        fv_level            = fv,
        method              = "insufficient",
        multiple_comparison = NA_character_,
        effect_type         = NA_character_,
        effect_value        = NA_real_,
        n_groups            = n_groups,
        n_min               = n_min,
        n_max               = n_max,
        shapiro_p           = NA_real_,
        levene_p            = NA_real_
      )

      next
    }

    # -------------------- assumption checks --------------------
    # simple LM residuals for normality + Levene for homogeneity
    fit_lm <- lm(resp ~ prod, data = dat_i2)
    resid_vec <- residuals(fit_lm)

    shap <- rstatix::shapiro_test(resid_vec)   # returns p.value
    lev  <- rstatix::levene_test(resp ~ prod, data = dat_i2)  # column "p"

    shap_p <- shap$p.value[1]
    lev_p  <- lev$p[1]

    # ----------------------------------------------------------------
    # "Robust" ANOVA rule:
    # - all groups n >= 3
    # - homogeneity OK (Levene p > alpha)
    # - AND either:
    #     * Shapiro p > alpha  (normal)
    #     * OR Shapiro p <= alpha AND n_min > 30 (large-sample robustness)
    # ----------------------------------------------------------------
    use_anova <- (
      all(gcounts$n >= 3) &&
      lev_p > alpha &&
      (shap_p > alpha || (shap_p <= alpha && n_min > 30))
    )

    # ----------------------------------------------------------------
    # summary stats: means, se, n (always on observed scale)
    # ----------------------------------------------------------------
    summ <- dat_i2 %>%
      group_by(prod) %>%
      summarise(
        mean = mean(resp, na.rm = TRUE),
        se   = sd(resp, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(sum(!is.na(resp))),
        n    = sum(!is.na(resp)),
        .groups = "drop"
      )

    # ----------------------------------------------------------------
    # inferential + effect size + CLD
    # ----------------------------------------------------------------
    if (use_anova) {
      ## ---------------- ANOVA + Tukey ----------------
      fit_aov <- aov(resp ~ prod, data = dat_i2)
      aov_tab <- summary(fit_aov)[[1]]

      ss_prod <- aov_tab["prod", "Sum Sq"]
      ss_tot  <- sum(aov_tab[, "Sum Sq"])
      eta2    <- as.numeric(ss_prod / ss_tot)

      tuk <- rstatix::tukey_hsd(fit_aov)

      letters <- cld_from_contrasts(
        tuk,
        group1 = "group1",
        group2 = "group2",
        p_col  = "p.adj",
        alpha  = alpha
      )

      cld_vec <- letters[match(summ$prod, names(letters))]

      method_label <- "ANOVA + Tukey HSD"
      effect_type  <- "eta2"
      effect_value <- eta2
      mult_label   <- "Tukey (BH-adjusted within method)"

    } else {
      ## ---------------- KW + Dunn ----------------
      kw_res <- rstatix::kruskal_test(resp ~ prod, data = dat_i2)
      # eta2[H] effect size
      kw_eff <- rstatix::kruskal_effsize(resp ~ prod, data = dat_i2)
      eta2H  <- kw_eff$effsize[1]

      dunn_res <- rstatix::dunn_test(
        resp ~ prod,
        data = dat_i2,
        p.adjust.method = "BH"
      )

      letters <- cld_from_contrasts(
        dunn_res,
        group1 = "group1",
        group2 = "group2",
        p_col  = "p.adj",
        alpha  = alpha
      )

      cld_vec <- letters[match(summ$prod, names(letters))]

      method_label <- "Kruskal–Wallis + Dunn"
      effect_type  <- "eta2[H]"
      effect_value <- eta2H
      mult_label   <- "Dunn (BH-adjusted)"
    }

    summ <- summ %>%
      mutate(
        cld  = cld_vec,
        fv   = fv,
        cell = sprintf("%.1f \u00b1 %.1f%s (n = %d)", mean, se, cld, n)
      )

    all_cells[[fv]] <- summ

    meta_list[[fv]] <- tibble(
      fv_level            = fv,
      method              = method_label,
      multiple_comparison = mult_label,
      effect_type         = effect_type,
      effect_value        = effect_value,
      n_groups            = n_groups,
      n_min               = n_min,
      n_max               = n_max,
      shapiro_p           = shap_p,
      levene_p            = lev_p
    )
  }

  # -------------------- bind & pivot to wide table --------------------
  all_cells_df <- bind_rows(all_cells)

  # wide cell table: rows = product, cols = FV levels
  cld_table <- all_cells_df %>%
    select(prod, fv, cell) %>%
    mutate(
      prod = as.character(prod),
      fv   = as.character(fv)
    ) %>%
    pivot_wider(
      names_from  = fv,
      values_from = cell
    ) %>%
    arrange(prod)

  # add conservative n to FV column headers (min n per FV)
  fv_ns <- all_cells_df %>%
    group_by(fv) %>%
    summarise(
      n_min = min(n, na.rm = TRUE),
      .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    mutate(
      old_name = as.character(fv),
      new_name = paste0(old_name, " (n \u2265 ", n_min, ")")
    )

  # rename columns in cld_table (except first: product)
  current_names <- names(cld_table)
  for (i in seq_len(nrow(fv_ns))) {
    old <- fv_ns$old_name[i]
    new <- fv_ns$new_name[i]
    if (old %in% current_names) {
      names(cld_table)[names(cld_table) == old] <- new
    }
  }

  # rename first column to requested product_var for manuscript friendliness
  names(cld_table)[1] <- product_var

  # metadata table
  method_effects <- bind_rows(meta_list) %>%
    arrange(fv_level)

  list(
    cld_table      = cld_table,
    method_effects = method_effects
  )
}

## ========================= USAGE EXAMPLE =========================
## Assuming df has:
##  - return_USDha
##  - product
##  - mean_fv_ratio_int

res <- build_fv_cld_and_metadata(df)
res$cld_table
res$method_effects

```


---

```{r warning=FALSE}
# Removed 7 redundant package-loading/install line(s); packages are loaded in the setup section.
## ================================================================
## Stratified CLD + metadata builder with "robust" ANOVA logic
## (patched for rstatix versions that return numeric for effect sizes)
## ================================================================


## ------------------------------------------------
## Helper: build CLD letters from pairwise p-values
## ------------------------------------------------
make_cld_from_pairs <- function(levels_vec, comp_df,
                                group1_col = "group1",
                                group2_col = "group2",
                                p_col      = "p_adj") {
  # if no comparisons or 1 level, everyone gets "a"
  if (nrow(comp_df) == 0 || length(levels_vec) < 2) {
    return(setNames(rep("a", length(levels_vec)), levels_vec))
  }

  comp_df <- comp_df %>%
    mutate(
      g1 = .data[[group1_col]],
      g2 = .data[[group2_col]]
    ) %>%
    filter(!is.na(.data[[p_col]]))

  if (nrow(comp_df) == 0) {
    return(setNames(rep("a", length(levels_vec)), levels_vec))
  }

  pair_names <- paste(comp_df$g1, comp_df$g2, sep = "-")
  p_vec      <- comp_df[[p_col]]
  names(p_vec) <- pair_names

  letters_obj <- multcompView::multcompLetters(p_vec)
  letters_vec <- letters_obj$Letters

  out <- setNames(
    rep("a", length(levels_vec)),
    levels_vec
  )
  common <- intersect(names(letters_vec), names(out))
  out[common] <- letters_vec[common]
  out
}

## ------------------------------------------------
## Helper: robust extraction of η² from rstatix::eta_squared()
## Works if eta_squared() returns tibble OR numeric
## ------------------------------------------------
extract_eta2 <- function(aov_fit, product_var) {
  eff_tab <- rstatix::eta_squared(aov_fit)

  # Newer rstatix: tibble with term + Eta2
  if (is.data.frame(eff_tab)) {
    # best guess at numeric effect-size column
    num_cols <- eff_tab %>% dplyr::select(where(is.numeric))
    if ("term" %in% names(eff_tab) && nrow(num_cols) > 0) {
      row_idx <- which(eff_tab$term == product_var)
      if (length(row_idx) == 0) row_idx <- 1L
      return(as.numeric(num_cols[row_idx[1], 1]))
    } else {
      # fallback: first numeric value
      return(as.numeric(num_cols[1, 1]))
    }
  }

  # Older rstatix: numeric vector
  as.numeric(eff_tab[1])
}

## ------------------------------------------------
## Helper: robust extraction of ε² from rstatix::kruskal_effsize()
## Works if kruskal_effsize() returns tibble OR numeric
## ------------------------------------------------
extract_eps2 <- function(df_sub, form, product_var) {
  eff_tab <- rstatix::kruskal_effsize(df_sub, formula = form)

  if (is.data.frame(eff_tab)) {
    num_cols <- eff_tab %>% dplyr::select(where(is.numeric))
    if ("term" %in% names(eff_tab) && nrow(num_cols) > 0) {
      row_idx <- which(eff_tab$term == product_var)
      if (length(row_idx) == 0) row_idx <- 1L
      return(as.numeric(num_cols[row_idx[1], 1]))
    } else {
      return(as.numeric(num_cols[1, 1]))
    }
  }

  as.numeric(eff_tab[1])
}

## ------------------------------------------------
## Core builder: one stratifier at a time
## ------------------------------------------------
build_strat_cld_and_metadata <- function(
  df,
  strat_var    = "mean_fv_ratio_int",
  response_var = "return_USDha",
  product_var  = "product"
) {

  strat_sym   <- sym(strat_var)
  resp_sym    <- sym(response_var)
  prod_sym    <- sym(product_var)

  df_clean <- df %>%
    filter(
      !is.na(!!strat_sym),
      !is.na(!!prod_sym),
      !is.na(!!resp_sym)
    )

  strat_levels <- sort(unique(df_clean[[strat_var]]))

  cld_pieces   <- list()
  meta_pieces  <- list()

  for (lev in strat_levels) {
    df_sub <- df_clean %>%
      filter(.data[[strat_var]] == lev)

    n_total <- nrow(df_sub)
    groups  <- na.omit(unique(df_sub[[product_var]]))

    if (n_total < 3 || length(groups) < 2) {
      summary_tbl <- df_sub %>%
        group_by(!!prod_sym) %>%
        summarise(
          mean = mean(!!resp_sym, na.rm = TRUE),
          se   = sd(!!resp_sym, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(dplyr::n()),
          n    = dplyr::n(),
          .groups = "drop"
        ) %>%
        mutate(cld = "a")

      cld_pieces[[as.character(lev)]] <- summary_tbl %>%
        mutate(
          label = sprintf("%.1f ± %.1f%s", mean, se, cld)
        ) %>%
        select(!!prod_sym, label)

      meta_pieces[[as.character(lev)]] <- tibble::tibble(
        stratifier  = strat_var,
        level       = lev,
        method      = "Not tested (insufficient groups/n)",
        effect_type = NA_character_,
        effect_size = NA_real_,
        shapiro_p   = NA_real_,
        levene_p    = NA_real_,
        min_n       = NA_integer_,
        n_total     = n_total,
        use_anova   = FALSE
      )
      next
    }

    ## ---------------- Assumptions + robust ANOVA decision ----------------
    form <- as.formula(paste(response_var, "~", product_var))

    lm_fit <- lm(form, data = df_sub)

    shapiro_p <- tryCatch(
      shapiro.test(resid(lm_fit))$p.value,
      error = function(e) NA_real_
    )

    levene_p <- tryCatch(
      rstatix::levene_test(df_sub, formula = form)$p,
      error = function(e) NA_real_
    )

    group_n <- df_sub %>%
      count(.data[[product_var]], name = "n_group")
    min_n   <- min(group_n$n_group, na.rm = TRUE)

    use_anova <- !is.na(levene_p) &&
                 levene_p >= 0.05 &&
                 !is.na(shapiro_p) &&
                 (
                   shapiro_p >= 0.05 ||
                   (shapiro_p < 0.05 && n_total >= 30)
                 )

    ## ---------------- Summary stats (observed scale) ----------------
    summary_tbl <- df_sub %>%
      group_by(!!prod_sym) %>%
      summarise(
        mean = mean(!!resp_sym, na.rm = TRUE),
        se   = sd(!!resp_sym, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(dplyr::n()),
        n    = dplyr::n(),
        .groups = "drop"
      )

    ## ---------------- Testing + effect size + pairwise p-values ----------------
    if (use_anova) {
      aov_fit <- aov(form, data = df_sub)

      effect_val   <- extract_eta2(aov_fit, product_var)
      effect_label <- "η²"

      emm   <- emmeans::emmeans(aov_fit, specs = product_var)
      contr <- emmeans::contrast(emm, method = "pairwise", adjust = "tukey") %>%
        as.data.frame()

      comps <- contr %>%
        tidyr::separate(contrast, into = c("group1","group2"), sep = " - ") %>%
        rename(p_adj = p.value)

      cld_letters <- make_cld_from_pairs(
        levels_vec = summary_tbl[[product_var]],
        comp_df    = comps,
        group1_col = "group1",
        group2_col = "group2",
        p_col      = "p_adj"
      )

      method_label <- "ANOVA (Tukey HSD)"

    } else {
      kw_res <- rstatix::kruskal_test(df_sub, formula = form)

      effect_val   <- extract_eps2(df_sub, form, product_var)
      effect_label <- "ε²"

      dunn_res <- rstatix::dunn_test(
        df_sub,
        formula          = form,
        p.adjust.method  = "BH"
      )

      comps <- dunn_res %>%
        rename(
          group1 = group1,
          group2 = group2,
          p_adj  = p.adj
        )

      cld_letters <- make_cld_from_pairs(
        levels_vec = summary_tbl[[product_var]],
        comp_df    = comps,
        group1_col = "group1",
        group2_col = "group2",
        p_col      = "p_adj"
      )

      method_label <- "Kruskal–Wallis (Dunn + BH)"
    }

    summary_tbl <- summary_tbl %>%
      mutate(
        cld   = cld_letters[.data[[product_var]]],
        label = sprintf("%.1f ± %.1f%s", mean, se, cld)
      )

    cld_pieces[[as.character(lev)]] <- summary_tbl %>%
      select(!!prod_sym, label)

    meta_pieces[[as.character(lev)]] <- tibble::tibble(
      stratifier  = strat_var,
      level       = lev,
      method      = method_label,
      effect_type = effect_label,
      effect_size = effect_val,
      shapiro_p   = shapiro_p,
      levene_p    = levene_p,
      min_n       = min_n,
      n_total     = n_total,
      use_anova   = use_anova
    )
  }

  ## ---------------- Combine CLD pieces into wide table ----------------
  cld_long <- purrr::imap_dfr(
    cld_pieces,
    ~ .x %>%
      mutate(
        !!prod_sym := as.character(.data[[product_var]]),
        !!strat_var := .y
      )
  )

  cld_table <- cld_long %>%
    select(!!prod_sym, !!strat_sym, label) %>%
    tidyr::pivot_wider(
      names_from  = !!strat_sym,
      values_from = label
    ) %>%
    arrange(!!prod_sym)

  meta_table <- bind_rows(meta_pieces) %>%
    arrange(level)

  list(
    cld_table   = cld_table,
    meta_table  = meta_table
  )
}

## ------------------------------------------------
## Meta-function: run across multiple stratifiers
## ------------------------------------------------
run_all_stratifiers <- function(
  df,
  strat_vars = c("mean_fv_ratio_int",
                 "year",
                 "location",
                 "scn_level",
                 "fdx_level",
                 "sds_tol"),
  response_var = "return_USDha",
  product_var  = "product"
) {
  res_list <- purrr::map(
    strat_vars,
    ~ build_strat_cld_and_metadata(
      df           = df,
      strat_var    = .x,
      response_var = response_var,
      product_var  = product_var
    )
  )
  names(res_list) <- strat_vars
  res_list
}

## ------------------------------------------------
## EXAMPLES
## ------------------------------------------------
## 1) Just FV
 fv_results <- build_strat_cld_and_metadata(
   df           = df,
   strat_var    = "mean_fv_ratio_int",
   response_var = "return_USDha",
   product_var  = "product"
 )
 fv_results$cld_table
 fv_results$meta_table

## 2) All stratifiers
 all_strat <- run_all_stratifiers(df)
 names(all_strat)
 all_strat$mean_fv_ratio_int$cld_table
 all_strat$mean_fv_ratio_int$meta_table
 
write.csv(file="tbl_df.csv",fv_results$cld_table)

```



# Source module: PCA minimal (factoextra color test)

<!-- Imported and lightly cleaned from `PCA minimal (factoextra color test).Rmd`. -->

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
# Removed 5 redundant package-loading/install line(s); packages are loaded in the setup section.
# Start spotless
rm(list = ls(all = TRUE))
if ("package:plyr" %in% search()) detach("package:plyr", unload = TRUE)


# Helper to show versions
sessionInfo()

```


###test on built-in dataset iris
```{r}
X_iris <- iris %>%
select(where(is.numeric)) %>%
scale() %>%
as.data.frame()




pr_iris <- stats::prcomp(X_iris, center = FALSE, scale. = FALSE, retx = TRUE)




hab_iris <- factor(iris$Species) # grouping factor
pal_iris <- c("#1B9E77", "#D95F02", "#7570B3") # 3 colors




p_iris <- fviz_pca_ind(
pr_iris,
geom = "point",
repel = TRUE,
habillage = hab_iris,
addEllipses = TRUE,
ellipse.type = "norm",
pointshape = 16,
palette = pal_iris,
ggtheme = theme_minimal()
) + ggtitle("IRIS — should be COLORED") + coord_equal()




p_iris
```

###ok, that worked
###now to try with my dataset
```{r}
raw <- readr::read_csv("C:/Longitudinal/full2017_2024.csv") %>% janitor::clean_names()

raw <- raw %>%
mutate(
scn_level = case_when(
scn_count_rep == 0 ~ "none",
scn_count_rep > 0 & scn_count_rep < 2000 ~ "low",
scn_count_rep >= 2000 ~ "high",
TRUE ~ NA_character_
),
scn_level = factor(scn_level, levels = c("none","low","high"))
)

df <- raw # no extra factoring here—keep it lean

id_like <- c("yield_mtha","yield_diff","yield_bua","yield_diff_bua","proj_price_kg",
"proj_price_bu","harv_price_kg","harv_price_bu","diff_price_bua")

num_df <- df %>%
select(where(is.numeric)) %>%
select(-any_of(intersect(names(df), id_like)))

stopifnot(ncol(num_df) > 1)

preproc <- recipe(~ ., data = num_df) %>%
step_impute_median(all_numeric_predictors()) %>%
step_zv(all_numeric_predictors()) %>%
step_normalize(all_numeric_predictors()) %>%
prep()

X <- juice(preproc, composition = "data.frame")
pr <- stats::prcomp(X, center = FALSE, scale. = FALSE, retx = TRUE)

hab <- forcats::fct_explicit_na(df$scn_level, na_level = "NA")
pal <- c("#66A61E", "#E6AB02", "#D95F02", "#BDBDBD")[seq_len(nlevels(hab))]

# Map each point to a hex color by its group
cols_by_point <- pal[as.integer(hab)]   # length == nrow(pr$x)

# Individuals
pca_points <- factoextra::fviz_pca_ind(
  pr,
  geom = "point",
  repel = TRUE,
  habillage   = hab,            # keeps grouping for ellipses & legend
  addEllipses = TRUE,
  ellipse.type = "norm",
  pointshape = 16,
  col.ind = cols_by_point       # <-- force per-point colors
) + ggplot2::scale_color_manual(
    name = "SCN level",
    values = setNames(pal, levels(hab))
  ) +
  ggplot2::ggtitle("PCA Individuals (SCN level)") +
  ggplot2::coord_equal()

ggplot2::ggsave("pca_points.png", pca_points, width = 6, height = 6, units = "in", dpi = 300)

# Variables (unchanged)
pca_arrows <- factoextra::fviz_pca_var(
  pr, repel = TRUE, col.var = "contrib"
) + ggplot2::ggtitle("PCA Variables (contribution)") +
  ggplot2::coord_equal()
ggplot2::ggsave("pca_arrows.png", pca_arrows, width = 6, height = 6, units = "in", dpi = 300)

# Biplot
pca_biplot <- factoextra::fviz_pca_biplot(
  pr,
  geom = "point",
  repel = TRUE,
  habillage   = hab,
  addEllipses = TRUE,
  ellipse.type = "norm",
  pointshape = 16,
  col.ind = cols_by_point       # <-- force per-point colors
) + ggplot2::ggtitle("Combined PCA Biplot (SCN level)") +
  ggplot2::coord_equal()

ggplot2::ggsave("pca_biplot.png", pca_biplot, width = 6, height = 6, units = "in", dpi = 300)

```


###"deep" diagnostics?
```{r}

p_build <- ggplot_build(p_you)
length(p_build$plot$scales$scales) # should be >= 1

if (length(p_build$plot$scales$scales) >= 1) {
p_build$plot$scales$scales[[1]]
}

p_force <- p_you + scale_color_manual(values = setNames(unname(pal), levels(hab)))
p_force

```
